p1.heading: Subject matter
p1.text:
(1) This Act incorporates relevant European Union regulations^1), is based on directly applicable European Union regulations^1a) and regulates, in matters of civil aviation,

- a) conditions for the construction and operation of aircraft,

- b) conditions for the establishment, operation and certification of aerodromes,

- c) conditions for aeronautical structures,

- d) conditions for the activities of aviation personnel,

- e) conditions for the use of airspace,

- f) conditions for the provision of air navigation services,

- g) conditions for the operation and control of unmanned aircraft systems,

- h) conditions for the operation of aviation activities,

- i) scope and conditions for aviation security,

- j) conditions for the use of sport flying equipment,

- k) scope and conditions for the exercise of state administration.

(2) This Act applies, to the defined extent, to military aviation in matters of aviation personnel, military aerodromes and aeronautical structures, use of airspace, provision of air navigation services and operation of aviation activities.

p2.heading: Basic definitions
p2.text:
(1) Civil aviation means aviation activities operated in the Czech Republic by civil aircraft of any nationality for civil purposes, as well as aviation activities operated by aircraft of Czech Republic nationality abroad for civil purposes, and the operation of civil aerodromes and the provision of air navigation services within the territory of the Czech Republic.

(2) An aircraft means any machine that can derive support in the atmosphere from the reactions of the air other than the reactions of the air against the earth’s surface.

(3) An aeronautical product (hereinafter referred to as a „product“) for the purposes of this Act means an aircraft, engine or propeller.

(4) Aircraft parts and appliances mean any instrument, equipment, mechanism, apparatus, accessory or assembly, including communication equipment, which is used or intended for use in the operation or control of an aircraft in flight and is installed in or attached to the aircraft. It includes parts of the airframe, engine or propeller.

(5) Aeronautical ground equipment means technical equipment located on the ground and used to ensure air traffic.

(6) The airspace of the Czech Republic is the space above the territory of the Czech Republic up to the height that can be used for air traffic.

(7) An aerodrome is a geographically defined and suitably prepared area, including a set of aeronautical structures and aerodrome facilities, permanently designated for the take-off and landing of aircraft and related aircraft movements.

(8) An aerodrome plot is any plot of land on which an aerodrome or part thereof is located.

(9) Commercial air transport means the transport of persons, animals, baggage, mail or other cargo by aircraft for remuneration.

(10) An air carrier means a person authorized to operate commercial air transport on the basis of a license or other similar authorization.

(11) A domestic air carrier means an air carrier holding a license to operate commercial air transport issued by the competent administrative authority of the Czech Republic.

(12) An air carrier of another Member State means an air carrier holding a license to operate commercial air transport issued by the competent authority of another Member State of the European Union, another state forming the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation.

(13) A third-country air carrier means an air carrier holding a license to operate commercial air transport or a similar document issued by the competent authority of a state that is not a Member State of the European Union, a state forming the European Economic Area or the Swiss Confederation.

(14) A series of irregular flights means the performance of more than 3 flights within 2 consecutive calendar months.

(15) Passenger name record means records of passenger travel requests contained in the air carrier's reservation system, the departure control system used by the air carrier for passenger check-in, or a similar air carrier system, which include all data provided by or on behalf of the passenger to the air carrier for the purpose of processing and controlling seat reservations for a specific flight.

p3.heading: Establishment of the Civil Aviation Authority
p3.text:
(1) The Civil Aviation Authority, with its registered office in Prague (hereinafter referred to as the “Authority”), is hereby established as an administrative authority for the exercise of state administration in civil aviation matters. The Authority is subordinate to the Ministry of Transport.

(2) The Authority is headed by a Director, whose selection, appointment, and dismissal are governed by the State Service Act.

(3) The Authority cooperates with the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (hereinafter referred to as the “Agency”) on the basis of directly applicable European Union legislation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c). The scope and conditions of cooperation are further regulated by an agreement concluded between the Authority and the Agency.

(4) If the Authority carries out activities for the Agency, it is obliged to establish a special account for the revenues from these activities and to keep separate accounting of costs and revenues. The Authority is authorized to use the funds in the account only for increasing the qualifications of its employees, purchasing technical equipment, and financing other needs essential for carrying out activities for the Agency. Unspent funds in the account in a calendar year shall be revenue of the state budget. The Authority is obliged to publish a statement of account management for the relevant calendar year in a manner enabling remote access^1d) within 6 months from the end of the relevant calendar year.

(5) The Authority performs the tasks of a national supervisory body in accordance with directly applicable European Union legislation^1e).

(6) The Authority may, by its decision, entrust the performance of tasks in the field of conformity assessment or suitability for use of components and verification of European Air Traffic Management Network systems to a legal entity that meets the conditions stipulated by directly applicable European Union legislation^1f). The Authority shall withdraw the entrustment under the conditions stipulated by directly applicable European Union legislation^1f).

(7) The Authority supervises the fulfillment of obligations of air carriers regarding compensation and assistance to passengers in the event of denied boarding, cancellation, or significant delay of a flight in accordance with directly applicable European Union legislation^1g) and handles complaints about violations of these obligations.

(8) The Authority supervises the fulfillment of obligations of

- a) airport operators and air carriers regarding compliance with the rights of persons with disabilities and persons with reduced mobility and orientation in accordance with directly applicable European Union legislation^1h),

- b) air carriers regarding informing air transport passengers in accordance with directly applicable European Union legislation^1i).

(9) If the obligations stipulated in paragraph 8 letters a) and b) concern a travel agency operator or a travel agent operator, the municipal trade licensing office supervises their fulfillment.

(10) If the matter cannot be delayed and it is necessary for fulfilling an obligation arising from directly applicable European Union legislation, the Authority may, until its organizational structure or its change is approved in accordance with the State Service Act, provisionally proceed as if the proposed organizational structure or its change had been approved; this does not apply if it would result in the termination of the service relationship.

p4.heading: Aircraft Register
p4.text:
(1) The Aircraft Register of the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as the “aircraft register”) is a record of aircraft, with the exception of sport flying equipment, whose operator is a natural person with permanent residence or a legal entity with its registered office in the Czech Republic. The aircraft register is maintained by the Authority.

(2) The aircraft register is a publicly accessible list. Everyone is entitled to inspect the aircraft register and to request from the Authority a copy or extract of the recorded data or a confirmation that the data is not recorded in the aircraft register. The following aircraft data are entered into the aircraft register:

- a) data on its owner, namely

- 1. name, surname, domicile, and date of birth, if it is a natural person, or

- 2. business name or name and identification number, if assigned, if it is a self-employed natural person or a legal entity,

- b) data on its operator, namely

- 1. name, surname, domicile, and date of birth, if it is a natural person, or

- 2. business name or name and identification number, if assigned, if it is a self-employed natural person or a legal entity,

- c) registration mark,

- d) type and serial number of the aircraft and other basic technical data stipulated by the implementing legal regulation,

- e) establishment of a lien on the aircraft, aircraft engines and propellers, spare parts for the aircraft, its aircraft parts and equipment,

- f) aircraft address, if assigned,

- g) date of entry and deletion from the aircraft register,

- h) other data, if so stipulated by a special legal regulation.

(3) Only an aircraft that is not registered in the aircraft register of another state may be entered into the aircraft register.

(4) The Authority may, upon request, provisionally assign a registration mark before the aircraft is entered into the aircraft register. The decision on provisional assignment of a registration mark ceases to be valid upon the expiration of 6 months from its legal force. The particulars of the request are stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

(5) By entry into the aircraft register, the aircraft acquires the nationality of the Czech Republic.

(6) The Authority shall assign a registration mark to the aircraft upon entry into the aircraft register and issue an aircraft registration certificate. The aircraft registration certificate certifies ownership of the aircraft.

p5.text:
(1) An application for entry of an aircraft into the aircraft register is submitted by the owner or operator of the aircraft.

(2) The particulars of the application for entry of an aircraft into the aircraft register and the documents that must be attached to the application are stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

p5a.text:
(1) An application for the registration of a lien on an aircraft shall be filed by the aircraft owner together with the lien creditor.

(2) The particulars of the application for the registration of a lien in the aircraft register and the documents that must be attached to the application shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(3) If the conditions stipulated by this Act for the registration of a lien in the aircraft register are met, the Office shall carry out the registration and notify the applicant and the pledgor thereof.

(4) A contractual lien on an aircraft shall arise on the day of registration of the lien in the aircraft register according to the order of received applications. Contractual liens registered in the aircraft register before the effective date of this Act, if they continue to exist, shall be considered as liens that arose on the day they were registered in the aircraft register.

(5) Against a person acting in reliance on the registration of a lien on an aircraft in the aircraft register, the person to whom the registration relates may not object that the registration does not correspond to the facts.

p5b.text:
(1) Contracts on the transfer of ownership of an aircraft must be in writing. The transfer of ownership and of a lien on an aircraft registered in the aircraft register shall become effective on the day of registration in the aircraft register.

(2) The consent of the lien creditor is required for the transfer of ownership of a pledged aircraft.

(3) A lien creditor whose lien is registered in the aircraft register shall, in the enforcement of a decision, be entitled to seek preferential satisfaction from the sale of the aircraft over another lien creditor, unless it is a creditor whose lien was registered earlier in the aircraft register, as well as a creditor who has a right of retention to the aircraft.

p6.text:
(1) The operator of an aircraft registered in the aircraft register, the owner of the aircraft, the lien creditor and persons to whom the entry in the aircraft register relates are obliged to promptly report and document to the Office all changes to the data entered in the aircraft register, and, in the case of a legal entity, the assigned identification number, if it was not included in the application for registration of the aircraft in the aircraft register.

(2) The Office shall delete the registration of an aircraft from the aircraft register at the request of the operator or owner of the registered aircraft or on its own initiative, if it ascertains that the conditions stipulated for the registration of the aircraft in the aircraft register have changed or ceased to exist. If the aircraft operator is not simultaneously the aircraft owner, the deletion of the aircraft registration from the aircraft register may only be carried out with the consent of the aircraft owner. Unless it concerns the sale of an aircraft in the enforcement of a decision, the Office may delete the aircraft from the aircraft register only if all holders of rights relating to the aircraft registered in the aircraft register have given their consent to the deletion. The Office shall notify the aircraft operator of the deletion of the aircraft registration, and if the aircraft operator is not simultaneously the aircraft owner, also the owner.

p6a.text: The Office shall delete the registration of a lien on an aircraft registered in the aircraft register at the request of the lien creditor or on its own initiative based on a document proving the extinction of the lien. The Office shall notify the aircraft owner, the pledgor, if not simultaneously the aircraft owner, and the lien creditor of the deletion of the lien registration.

p6b.text:
(1) The provisions of § 5a and 6a shall apply mutatis mutandis to the creation of liens on aircraft engines and propellers, spare parts for aircraft, their aircraft components and equipment, and to the registration of these rights in the aircraft register, as well as to their deletion from the aircraft register.

(2) The owner of aircraft engines and propellers, spare parts for aircraft, aircraft components and equipment on which a lien registered in the aircraft register is encumbered, must ensure their

- a) placement in a warehouse separately from other spare parts,

- b) provision with a visible marking of the existence of the lien, stating the name, business name or designation of the lien creditor and the aircraft register in which the lien is registered.

p7.heading: Product Type Approval
p7.text:
(1) The Office shall approve the type of product based on an application from the manufacturer, importer or other legal or natural person who demonstrates a legal interest in the product type approval. The only participant in the product type approval procedure shall be the applicant.

(2) For the purpose of product type approval, the Office shall, no later than one month from the delivery of the application, stipulate

- a) after the applicant's statement, the regulations designated for type approval by an international treaty that is part of the legal order (hereinafter referred to as the „regulatory basis“), on the basis of which the product type will be approved,

- b) the period within which the stipulated regulatory basis may be used for product type approval.

(3) During the product type approval process, the Office may require the applicant for type approval to submit documents necessary for type approval.

(4) The Office shall approve the product type based on the results of the assessment and verification of the conformity of the product's properties with the requirements stipulated for aircraft safety and its components and the environmental friendliness of aircraft operation by the regulatory basis (hereinafter referred to as „assessment and verification of property conformity“), which the applicant for type approval shall document. The Office shall proceed according to the stipulated regulatory basis when approving the product type. The applicant shall ensure the assessment and verification of property conformity at their own expense at the Office. The Office may entrust a legal entity with the assessment and verification of property conformity.

(5) The Office shall issue a decision on product type approval no later than 3 years from the initiation of the procedure; in particularly complex cases, within 5 years.

(6) The manufacturer is obliged to manufacture products according to the type approved or recognized by the Office and to demonstrate the conformity of the product with the approved or recognized type.

(7) The manufacturer is obliged to monitor the product manufactured by them in operation and, based on analyses of product failures, issue measures for maintaining or restoring the airworthiness of the aircraft.

(8) The particulars of the application for product type approval, the documents that must be attached to the application, and the method of product type approval shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p8.heading: Aircraft Airworthiness
p8.text:
(1) The Office shall decide on the airworthiness of an aircraft (hereinafter referred to as „airworthiness“) on the basis of an application from

- a) the manufacturer, importer or other legal or natural person who demonstrates a legal interest, in the case of a newly manufactured aircraft,

- b) the operator, in the case of an aircraft already in operation.

The Office shall issue an airworthiness certificate upon approval of airworthiness.

(2) A condition for the approval of airworthiness by the Office is that the aircraft conforms to the approved type and that its airworthiness has been verified. The verification of airworthiness shall be ensured by the applicant at their own expense. The Office may entrust a legal or natural person with the assessment and verification of airworthiness.

(3) The implementing regulation shall stipulate the conditions for the airworthiness of individual types of aircraft with regard to their design, technical parameters and their use.

p9.heading: Airworthiness of an individually manufactured product
p9.text: The Office shall decide on the approval of the airworthiness of an individually manufactured product on the basis of an application from the manufacturer, importer or other legal or natural person who demonstrates a legal interest in the approval. The Office shall issue an airworthiness certificate upon approval of airworthiness. The Office shall issue an airworthiness certificate if the requirements stipulated by the implementing regulation for product safety and aircraft operational ecology are met.

p10.heading: Airworthiness inspections
p10.text:
(1) Aircraft registered in the aircraft register shall be subject to airworthiness inspections, which shall be ensured by the aircraft operator at their own expense at the Office. The Office may entrust a legal person with airworthiness inspections.

(2) If the Office finds during an inspection that the aircraft does not meet the airworthiness conditions, it shall withhold the airworthiness certificate until the airworthiness defect is remedied.

(3) In the event of a finding of permanent unairworthiness of the aircraft, the Office shall decide on the unairworthiness of the aircraft and revoke the airworthiness certificate.

(4) The scope and method of conducting airworthiness inspections in operation shall be stipulated by the implementing regulation.

p11.heading: Operation of a foreign aircraft
p11.text: An aircraft registered in the aircraft register of another state, whose operator is a natural person with permanent residence or a legal person with its registered office in the Czech Republic, may be operated in the airspace of the Czech Republic if the aircraft type and its airworthiness have been approved by another state and if the Office recognizes as valid, on the basis of an international treaty that is part of the legal order, or on the basis of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c), the certificate of aircraft type approval and the airworthiness certificate issued by another state.

p12.text:
(1) It is prohibited to operate an aircraft in the airspace of the Czech Republic

- a) which does not have a valid airworthiness certificate or for which an airworthiness certificate issued by another state has not been recognized as valid by the Office,

- b) for which no certificate of registration in the aircraft register or the aircraft register of another state has been issued,

- c) whose technical and operational condition does not comply with the requirements for flight safety and environmental protection stipulated by the implementing regulation.

(2) The aircraft operator is obliged to

- a) maintain the airworthiness of the aircraft,

- b) ensure that the aircraft operated by them is presented for airworthiness inspection in accordance with the requirement stipulated in the implementing regulation,

- c) provide the aircraft manufacturer, upon request, with a list of defects concerning the technical condition of the aircraft once per calendar year,

- d) have liability insurance for damages from aircraft operation arranged and the insurance premium paid for the entire period of aircraft operation,

- e) keep records of aircraft operation.

(3) The method of keeping records of aircraft operation shall be stipulated by the implementing regulation.

p12a.text:
(1) The Office shall collect and process all data significant for flight safety concerning the technical and operational condition of aircraft (hereinafter referred to as „technical condition of aircraft“) landing in the territory of the Czech Republic and registered in the aircraft register of a state that is not a member state of the European Union.

(2) The data pursuant to paragraph 1 shall include, in particular, data

- a) from aircraft operation records,

- b) from pilot reports,

- c) obtained by a legal or natural person authorized to perform maintenance and repairs of products, aircraft parts and equipment pursuant to § 17,

- d) obtained during the investigation of causes of aviation accidents and incidents,

- e) obtained from natural and legal persons or from administrative authorities,

- f) on a flight ban imposed after an aircraft inspection on the airport apron pursuant to § 91a para. 6,

- g) on measures imposed on the aircraft operator or adopted by the aircraft operator, aimed at remedying defects in the technical condition of the aircraft found during an aircraft inspection on the airport apron pursuant to § 91a para. 6,

- h) on repeated findings of defects in the technical condition of the aircraft,

- i) on the exchange of information with the competent authority of the state where the aircraft operator has its registered office or place of business.

(3) The provisions of paragraph 1 shall not apply to state aircraft pursuant to an international treaty that is part of the legal order^1j), and to aircraft that are not used for commercial air transport and whose maximum take-off weight is less than 5,700 kg.

(4) Anyone who has data pursuant to paragraph 1 at their disposal is obliged to submit it to the Office upon request. The Office shall set a reasonable deadline for the submission of data.

(5) On the basis of the data pursuant to paragraph 1, the Office shall, without undue delay, prepare a report, which it shall immediately submit to the European Commission and, upon request, to the competent authorities of the European Union member states and the Agency. If these data lead to a suspicion of a threat to flight safety, the Office shall immediately submit the report to all competent authorities of the European Union member states and the European Commission.

(6) Employees of the Office are obliged to maintain confidentiality regarding data concerning the technical condition of aircraft provided by the competent authorities of the European Union member states. The obligation to maintain confidentiality does not apply to the mutual provision of information between administrative authorities in ensuring flight safety.

(7) The model of the report pursuant to paragraph 5 shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

p13.text:
(1) The assessment and verification of product property conformity, the assessment and verification of airworthiness, and airworthiness inspections may be carried out for the needs of the Office by a legal or natural person authorized by the Office for this activity.

(2) The assessment and verification of product property conformity, the assessment and verification of airworthiness, and airworthiness inspections mean works by which the properties of an aircraft or its components are ascertained, verified, or demonstrated for the purposes of

- a) type approval of a product,

- b) approval of an individually manufactured aircraft or its components,

- c) airworthiness approval,

- d) airworthiness inspection.

(3) The assessment and verification of the conformity of properties of aeronautical ground equipment during development, design, manufacture, installation, and operation may be carried out for the needs of the Office by a legal or natural person authorized by the Office for this activity.

p14.text:
(1) The Office may authorize a legal or natural person who

- a) is technically and structurally equipped for assessing and verifying property conformity or for assessing and verifying the airworthiness of products or for inspecting the airworthiness of products, or for assessing and verifying the conformity of properties of aeronautical ground equipment during development, design, manufacture, installation, and operation,

- b) ensures that the activities under letter a) are carried out by professionally qualified natural persons who are obliged to maintain confidentiality regarding classified information and trade secrets.

(2) The Office supervises whether the authorized legal or natural person complies with the requirements under which the authorization was granted. If the requirements are not met, the Office shall suspend or withdraw the authorization from the legal or natural person.

p15.heading: Test Flights
p15.text:
(1) An aircraft prototype, an aircraft for which an airworthiness certificate has not yet been issued, or an aircraft that does not meet airworthiness conditions due to a change in its intended use, or an aircraft that has lost its airworthiness, may be used for test flights only with the consent of the Office.

(2) The Office shall grant consent for test flights after verifying the conditions for test flights stipulated by the implementing regulation.

p16.heading: Aircraft Parts and Equipment and Aeronautical Ground Equipment
p16.text:
(1) Aircraft parts and equipment and aeronautical ground equipment specified by the implementing regulation may be used in civil aviation only if their suitability for use in civil aviation has been approved or recognized by the Office. Costs associated with the issuance of a certificate of suitability for aircraft parts and equipment and aeronautical ground equipment shall be borne by the applicant.

(2) The particulars of the application for approval of the suitability of aircraft parts and equipment and aeronautical ground equipment for use in civil aviation and the technical conditions for aircraft parts and equipment and aeronautical ground equipment shall be stipulated by the implementing regulation.

p17.text:
(1) The development, design, manufacture, testing, installation, maintenance, repair, modifications, and design changes of products, aircraft parts and equipment, and aeronautical ground equipment specified by the implementing legal regulation may be carried out by a legal or natural person who holds an authorization for this activity issued by the Office or the Agency or another Member State pursuant to a directly applicable European Union regulation^1k) or recognized pursuant to a special legal regulation^1j).

(2) The Office shall grant authorization pursuant to paragraph 1 to a legal or natural person who meets the following requirements:

- a) has technical equipment for the development, design, manufacture, installation, repair, testing, or maintenance of products, aircraft parts and equipment, and aeronautical ground equipment,

- b) ensures that the development, design, manufacture, installation, repair, testing, or maintenance of products, aircraft parts and equipment, and aeronautical ground equipment are carried out by professionally qualified natural persons.

(3) The particulars of the application for authorization for the development, design, manufacture, installation, repair, testing, or maintenance of products, aircraft parts and equipment, and aeronautical ground equipment, the conditions for granting authorization, and the professional knowledge of natural persons who carry out the development, design, manufacture, repair, testing, or maintenance of products, aircraft parts and equipment, and aeronautical ground equipment shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

p17a.text:
(1) An aircraft address is an electronic identification code, intended for the international identification of aircraft, sport flying devices (§ 81), and other devices specified by the implementing legal regulation (hereinafter referred to as „coded device“) for the needs of civil and military aviation.

(2) The Ministry of Transport administers aircraft addresses allocated to the Czech Republic by an international organization on the basis of an international treaty that is part of the legal order^1j). The administration of aircraft addresses means the compilation of a plan for the distribution of aircraft addresses (hereinafter referred to as „the plan“) and the control of the efficient use of such distributed addresses. Parts of the plan concerning aircraft and coded devices used for military purposes are compiled by the Ministry of Transport in cooperation with the Ministry of Defence. Parts of the plan concerning aircraft operated by the Police of the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as „police aircraft“) are compiled by the Ministry of Transport in cooperation with the Ministry of the Interior.

(3) The Ministry of Transport shall determine in the plan the quantity of aircraft addresses for the needs of civil aircraft, military aircraft, police aircraft, customs aircraft, sport flying devices, and coded devices.

p17b.text:
(1) The Office shall decide on the allocation of aircraft addresses based on an application from

- a) the aircraft operator,

- b) the user of a sport flying device, or

- c) the operator of a coded device.

(2) A condition for issuing a decision on the allocation of an aircraft address is the availability of aircraft address capacity according to the plan, the allocation of a registration mark or the entry of a sport flying device in the register of sport flying devices pursuant to § 84b, or proof of the purposefulness of allocating an aircraft address for a coded device.

(3) Only one aircraft address may be allocated, and always only for one aircraft, sport flying device or coded device.

(4) For the needs of military aviation, the Ministry of Defence shall allocate and withdraw aircraft addresses according to the plan. The Ministry of Defence shall promptly notify the Office of the allocation or withdrawal of an aircraft address pursuant to the first sentence. The provisions of § 17b and 17c shall apply mutatis mutandis to the allocation and withdrawal of aircraft addresses by the Ministry of Defence.

(5) For the needs of police aircraft operations, the Ministry of the Interior shall allocate and withdraw aircraft addresses according to the plan. The Ministry of the Interior shall promptly notify the Office of the allocation or withdrawal of an aircraft address pursuant to the first sentence. The provisions of paragraphs 1 to 4 and § 17c shall apply mutatis mutandis to the allocation and withdrawal of aircraft addresses by the Ministry of the Interior.

(6) The particulars of the application for the allocation of an aircraft address and the list of documents that must accompany the application shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p17c.text:
(1) The aircraft operator, the user of a sport flying device, and the operator of a coded device are obliged to use the aircraft address in accordance with the decision on its allocation. The Office shall monitor the use of aircraft addresses allocated by it.

(2) The aircraft operator, the user of a sport flying device, and the operator of a coded device are obliged to promptly notify the Office in writing of any change in the facts on the basis of which the aircraft address was allocated.

(3) The Office shall withdraw the aircraft address

- a) upon deletion of the aircraft from the aircraft register,

- b) upon deletion of the sport flying device from the records pursuant to § 84b, or

- c) if the purposefulness of allocating the aircraft address for the coded device no longer exists.

p17d.text:
(1) The Office shall maintain a list of all aircraft addresses allocated according to the plan. The Office shall maintain this list in written and electronic form.

(2) The Office shall make available data from the list of allocated aircraft addresses to the Ministry of Transport, the person authorized to provide air traffic services (§ 46), and the person authorized pursuant to § 82 para. 1 to maintain records of sport flying devices.

(3) The Office shall provide data from the list of allocated aircraft addresses to a person who demonstrates a legal interest.

(4) The particulars and method of maintaining the list of allocated aircraft addresses shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p18.text:
(1) Natural persons who perform activities to ensure safe and smooth air traffic operations are "aeronautical personnel". Natural persons who participate in a flight on board an aircraft and perform activities to ensure the safe conduct of the flight or activities related to maintaining their qualification or activities for the purpose of flight control or airworthiness control are "aircraft crew".

(2) Aeronautical personnel include

- a) flight crew members,

- b) operating personnel,

- c) ground aeronautical personnel.

(3) Activities of aeronautical personnel may be performed by persons who are professionally and medically fit and who hold a valid certificate of competency for the performance of the relevant activity. Activities of cabin crew may be performed by persons as stipulated by directly applicable European Union regulation, with no certificate of competency being issued for the performance of these activities.

(4) The activity of a flight crew member may be performed for an air carrier on the basis of an employment relationship or on the basis of a contract under the Civil Code; this contract must be in writing.

(5) The Office shall maintain records of aeronautical personnel. This provision does not apply to cabin crew.

p19.heading: Certificate of Competency for Aeronautical Personnel
p19.text:
(1) The Office shall issue certificates of competency and recognize the validity of certificates of competency issued by other states.

(2) The Office shall issue a certificate of competency to an applicant who has demonstrated professional competence for performing the relevant activity of aeronautical personnel through an examination and medical fitness.

(3) The Office shall withhold a certificate of competency and order a verification of professional competence or a re-examination of medical fitness of an aeronautical personnel member if, during the performance of the activity for which the certificate of competency entitles them, they show deficiencies that may jeopardize the safety of air traffic operations. Based on the results of the verification of professional competence or the re-examination of medical fitness, the Office may restrict or prohibit the activity for which the aeronautical personnel member is authorized. In the event of a prohibition of activity, the Office shall withdraw the certificate of competency.

(4) An aeronautical personnel member who holds a certificate of competency must not perform the activity for which the certificate of competency entitles them if they are aware of an illness or such a change in health status that restricts or precludes the performance of this activity.

p20.heading: Professional Competence of Aeronautical Personnel
p20.text:
(1) An applicant who has attained the necessary qualification may take the examination.

(2) An applicant for the performance of activities as an aeronautical personnel member must complete the prescribed instruction and training before the examination. Part of the professional competence of an applicant for the issuance of an air traffic controller certificate of competency is, in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the granting of certificates of competency and the conditions for the performance of air traffic controller activities^16), knowledge of the Czech language.

(3) Details on the categorization of aeronautical personnel members, the necessary qualification, the scope of professional knowledge required for individual types of professional competence, the method and scope of instruction and training, the method of conducting examinations, the types of certificates of competency, and the conditions for extending the validity period of certificates shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p22.heading: Medical Fitness of Aeronautical Personnel
p22.text:
(1) An applicant for the performance of duties as an aviation personnel member or a holder of a certificate of competency applying for the extension of the validity of a certificate of competency must demonstrate that they are medically fit.

(2) A medical report^1n) on medical fitness is issued on the basis of a medical examination pursuant to a special legal regulation by

- a) a designated healthcare service provider through a physician authorized by the Office,

- b) a healthcare service provider through a physician authorized by the Office.

(3) For the purposes of this Act, an „assessing physician“ means a physician authorized by the Office, unless this Act or an implementing legal regulation based thereon or a directly applicable European Union regulation stipulates that the assessing physician is a physician with competence in the field of general practical medicine. Costs associated with the assessment of medical fitness and the issuance of a medical report pursuant to paragraph 2 shall be borne by the employer to the extent not covered by public health insurance.

(4) The Office designates healthcare service providers on the basis of their written application and after demonstrating the competence of their physicians for independent practice pursuant to a special Act^15) and in agreement with the Ministry of Defence and the Ministry of Health, and authorizes the physician referred to in paragraph 3 on the basis of their written application and after demonstrating their competence for independent practice pursuant to a special Act^15).

(5) An air operator is obliged to ensure occupational health services for employees performing aviation personnel duties with a provider of these services^1n) whose physicians have knowledge of aviation medicine.

(6) If the assessing physician identifies in a holder of an aviation personnel member's certificate of competency such a change in health status that results in a change or loss of their medical fitness to hold the certificate of competency, the healthcare service provider is obliged to issue a new medical fitness report and send it to the Office.

(7) A medical report on the medical fitness of aviation personnel must not be older than three months from the date of its issuance.

(8) The types of examinations, details on the medical fitness of aviation personnel, details on the authorization of a legal entity to assess knowledge of aviation medicine, and deadlines for demonstrating knowledge of aviation medicine shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p22a.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport shall, to ensure the safety of civil air traffic, stipulate by an implementing legal regulation rules for determining the maximum duty period, flight duty period, flight time, minimum rest requirements including rest when crossing multiple time zones, principles of split duty, principles for the use of augmented and double crews, and rules for determining flight standby for aircraft crew members (hereinafter referred to as „flight safety standard“).

(2) Domestic air carriers and aerial work operators shall, in accordance with the flight safety standard, stipulate the method for determining the maximum duty period, flight duty period, flight time, minimum rest requirements including rest when crossing multiple time zones, principles of split duty, principles for the use of augmented and double crews, and rules for determining flight standby for aircraft crew members (hereinafter referred to as „operator's flight safety standard“).

(3) The Office shall decide on the approval of the operator's flight safety standard, including any changes thereto, before their entry into force, upon the request of a domestic air carrier or aerial work operator. Before approving the operator's flight safety standard, the Office shall verify the internal control system of the domestic air carrier or aerial work operator to ensure its compliance. In the event of serious deficiencies found in the internal control system, the Office shall reject the request of the domestic air carrier or aerial work operator for approval of the operator's flight safety standard.

(4) In the event of rejection of a domestic air carrier's request for approval of the operator's flight safety standard, the Office shall initiate proceedings for the revocation of the air operator's certificate. In the event of rejection of an aerial work operator's request for approval of the operator's flight safety standard, the Office shall initiate proceedings for the revocation of the aerial work permit.

(5) The Office may approve, on the basis of a reasoned request from a domestic air carrier or aerial work operator, an exemption from the operator's flight safety standard for an individual flight.

p22b.text:
A domestic air carrier or aerial work operator is obliged to

- a) ensure compliance with the limits stipulated in accordance with the flight safety standard,

- b) keep records of duty period, flight duty period, flight time and its extensions, and rest period and its reductions for at least 15 months,

- c) submit to the Office a copy of the record of these facts with justification within 28 days from the moment of extending the maximum flight duty period by more than 1 hour or reducing the rest period by more than 1 hour.

p22c.text:
(1) An aircraft crew member is obliged to

- a) comply with the operator's flight safety standard,

- b) keep records of their performed duty period, flight duty period, flight time and its extensions, and rest period and its reductions,

- c) submit the records pursuant to letter b) to the domestic air carrier or aerial work operator before each performance of flight duty, if they perform duties as an aircraft crew member for multiple air carriers or aerial work operators.

(2) An aircraft crew member must not perform flight duty if they are aware that due to their fatigue or current health status, flight safety could be jeopardized.

(3) The aircraft commander is obliged to submit to the domestic air carrier or aerial work operator a record of the extension of the flight duty period or reduction of the rest period after the completion of the flight.

p23.text: The provisions of this part of the Act also apply to military aviation personnel, with the exception of the provisions of § 22a, 22b, and 22c. In matters concerning military aviation personnel, the Ministry of Defence exercises the powers of the Office.

p24.text:
(1) Aerodromes shall be divided

- a) according to their equipment, operating conditions and primary purpose, into aerodromes that are

- 1. domestic, which are aerodromes designated and equipped for carrying out domestic flights, during which the state border of the Czech Republic is not crossed, and flights during which the external border^1n) is not crossed (hereinafter referred to as “internal flights”),

- 2. international, which are customs aerodromes designated and equipped for carrying out both domestic and internal flights, as well as flights during which the external border is crossed according to another legal regulation^1n).

- b) according to the range of users and the character of the aerodrome, into aerodromes that are

- 1. civil, which are aerodromes for the needs of civil air transport; these include public aerodromes, which are aerodromes accepting all aircraft within the limits of their technical and operational capability, and non-public aerodromes, which are aerodromes accepting all aircraft and aircraft of aerodrome users specified by the Office upon the proposal of its operator, based on a prior agreement between the operator or aircraft commander and the operator of the non-public aerodrome and within the limits of their technical and operational capability,

- 2. military, which are aerodromes for the needs of the armed forces of the Czech Republic and other authorized users entrusted by the Ministry of Defence.

(2) This Act shall apply to the use of a military aerodrome also for civil air traffic.

p25.text:
(1) The Office shall decide on the determination of the type of aerodrome and its change based on the application of the aerodrome operator after assessing the technical and operational conditions stipulated for the requested type of aerodrome. The decision of the Office, by which an aerodrome is designated as an international aerodrome, shall serve as a basis for determining the course of the border of the customs border zone around the customs aerodrome according to a special regulation.^2)

(2) When issuing a decision pursuant to paragraph 1, by which an aerodrome is designated as an international aerodrome, the Office shall be bound by the opinion of the Ministry of the Interior issued pursuant to a special legal regulation^1m). If the Office receives an opinion from the Ministry of the Interior regarding non-compliance with the security conditions of an international aerodrome pursuant to a special legal regulation^1m), it shall initiate proceedings for a change in the type of aerodrome.

(3) The technical and operational conditions for individual types of aerodromes and details regarding the content of the application pursuant to paragraph 1 shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(4) At public international aerodromes, veterinary administration authorities shall ensure veterinary control at the border veterinary station, if approved by the relevant European Union authority.

p25a.heading: Register of Aerodromes
p25a.text:
(1) The Office shall maintain a register of aerodromes, which it shall publish in a manner enabling remote access. The Office shall enter an aerodrome into the register of aerodromes within three days of the legal force of the decision by which the type of aerodrome was determined.

(2) The following data concerning the aerodrome shall be entered into the register of aerodromes:

- a) aerodrome designation,

- b) aerodrome operator and its responsible representative, if appointed,

- c) type of aerodrome,

- d) aerodrome land,

- e) information on whether an easement has been established for the aerodrome land,

- f) date of entry and deletion of the aerodrome from the register of aerodromes.

(3) The Office shall delete an aerodrome from the register of aerodromes if it ceases to exist.

p25b.heading: Obligations of the Aerodrome Owner
p25b.text:
(1) The owner of an aerodrome is obliged to ensure the operation of the aerodrome.

(2) If the owner of an aerodrome cannot ensure its operation, they are obliged to offer the aerodrome to the state to ensure its operation. The owner of the aerodrome shall submit a written offer to the Ministry of Transport within 60 days from the day they became aware of the fact according to the first sentence, but no later than 60 days before the termination of the aerodrome's operation, unless they are also the aerodrome operator.

(3) If public interest so requires, the Ministry of Transport shall ensure the operation of the aerodrome based on a written agreement concluded with the owner of the aerodrome for the period during which the owner of the aerodrome is unable to ensure its operation by their own means or through another operator.

Operation of an Aerodrome

p26.text:
(1) Operation of an aerodrome means activities that ensure

- a) the possibility for aircraft landing and take-off and related aircraft movement,

- b) protection and servicing of aircraft,

- c) carrying out aviation activities,

- d) order, security, rescue and fire-fighting services at the aerodrome,

- e) protection against unlawful acts threatening the safety of civil aviation,

- f) maintenance and development of the aerodrome,

according to the conditions stipulated for the operation of the aerodrome.

(2) The technical and operational conditions for the operation of an aerodrome shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p27.text:
(1) An aerodrome may be operated by a legal entity with its registered office or a natural person with permanent residence in the Czech Republic, based on a permit. A permit to operate an aerodrome may be issued by the Office based on a written application.

(2) A permit may be issued on condition that

- a) the natural person and their responsible representative, if appointed, have reached the age of 18, are legally competent, of good character, and at least one of them is professionally qualified,

- b) all natural persons who are members of the statutory body of the legal entity or members of the statutory body have reached the age of 18, are legally competent, of good character, and at least one member of the statutory body or the responsible representative of the legal entity meets the condition of professional qualification.

(3) The Office shall issue a permit to operate an aerodrome preferentially to an applicant who proves to be the owner of more than half of the area of the aerodrome land. The original aerodrome operator shall be entitled to compensation from the applicant according to the first sentence. The amount of compensation shall be determined primarily with regard to the amount and duration of investments purposefully expended in connection with the aerodrome.

p28.heading: Good Character
p28.text:
(1) A person shall not be considered irreproachable if they have been finally convicted of a criminal offense whose factual basis is related to the operation of an airport, or if they have been finally convicted of another criminal offense committed intentionally, provided that, given the nature of airport operations and the person of the applicant for a permit, there is a concern that they will commit the same or a similar act during airport operations, unless they are regarded as not having been convicted.

(2) For the purpose of ascertaining whether a person pursuant to § 27 para 2 letter a) and b) meets the condition of irreproachability, the Office shall request an extract from the Criminal Register records pursuant to a special legal regulation^1m). The request for an extract from the Criminal Register records and the extract from the Criminal Register records shall be submitted electronically, in a manner enabling remote access.

p29.text: Professional competence shall be demonstrated by a document proving the attainment of secondary or higher vocational education or a document proving completed university education obtained by studying in a master's degree program in the field of economics, in the field of technical sciences and technologies, or in the field of law, and a document proving at least five years of professional experience in management activities in the field of civil aviation.

p30.heading: Application for a permit to operate an airport
p30.text:
(1) An application for a permit to operate an airport must contain

- a) the business name or name, registered office, and legal form of the legal entity and its identification number, if assigned, or the name and permanent residence, business name or name, and birth number of the natural person,

- b) the designation of the airport owner, if the future operator is not simultaneously the airport owner, in the manner specified in letter a).

(2) An application for a permit to operate an airport must be supported by

- a) a certified copy of the contract or document on the establishment or foundation of the legal entity, or for legal entities registered in the commercial register, an extract from the commercial register,

- b) a document proving professional competence,

- c) a document certifying that the applicant is the owner of the airport or has another legal relationship to the airport, and a document certifying the airport owner's consent to the operation of the airport, if the owner is not simultaneously the airport operator.

p30a.heading: Easement for airport operation
p30a.text:
(1) The court shall, upon the proposal of the operator of an airport existing as of December 31, 2009, decide on the establishment of an easement for the operation of the airport on the airport land on which the airport, or its part, existing as of December 31, 2009, is located, for the purpose of operating the airport. The establishment of an easement for airport operation is permissible only if the public interest in its establishment outweighs the preservation of the existing rights of the owner of the airport land and if the airport operator has failed to acquire the right to the airport land by agreement.

(2) For the establishment of an easement for airport operation, the owner of the land to which the easement has been established is entitled to compensation from the airport operator. The court shall determine the compensation for the establishment of an easement for airport operation as an annual payment, in an amount corresponding to the locally customary rent for the land to which the easement is established. The compensation is due no later than December 31 of the relevant calendar year, unless otherwise stipulated in the decision on the establishment of the easement. The owner of the land to which the easement has been established and the airport operator may agree in writing on a different amount of the annual payment and its earlier due date. The court shall, upon the request of the airport operator or the owner of the land to which the easement has been established, decide on a change in the amount of compensation if the circumstances under which the amount of compensation was determined or agreed upon have substantially changed.

(3) The court shall decide on the cancellation of the easement

- a) upon the proposal of the airport operator,

- b) if the airport is not operated for a period of one year,

- c) if the airport ceases to exist.

(4) The easement shall also cease to exist if the airport operator has not paid the compensation within 90 days from the due date of the annual payment.

(5) If it is sufficient for the fulfillment of the purpose of establishing the easement to establish the easement to a part of the airport land, the court shall decide on the establishment of the easement only to that part of the land, which it shall determine on the basis of a geometric plan prepared at the expense of the airport operator. This shall not apply if such a procedure would lead to the devaluation of the part of the land not encumbered by the easement.

p31.text:
(1) The airport operator is obliged to

- a) by the date of commencement of airport operations, demonstrate to the Office that it meets the conditions stipulated for airport operations,

- b) operate the airport in accordance with a valid airport operating permit,

- c) within a period of six months before the intended termination of airport operations, notify the Office of the intention to terminate airport operations and, within the same period, inform the airport owner, if the operator is not simultaneously the airport owner. In the case of an operator of an international public airport, this period shall be twelve months.

(2) The airport operator is authorized to issue instructions to operators of aviation activities, passengers, and other persons involved in airport operations and air traffic to ensure safe airport operations and to coordinate activities at the airport.

p32.text:
(1) The Office shall, upon written request by an airport operator or air carriers operating more than half of the take-offs or landings at the given airport in the preceding calendar year, or on its own initiative, designate an operated airport as a scheduled airport or a coordinated airport under the conditions stipulated by directly applicable European Union regulation^2a). In the event of a request by the European Commission pursuant to directly applicable European Union regulation^2a), the Office shall proceed mutatis mutandis. The particulars of the request by an airport operator or air carriers pursuant to the first sentence shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(2) For a coordinated airport, the Office shall, under the conditions stipulated by directly applicable European Union regulation^2a), entrust the allocation of available airport slots to air carriers and the supervision of their use to a natural person with permanent residence in the Czech Republic or a legal entity with its registered office in the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as the „coordinator“). The coordinator shall ensure, within 3 months of their appointment and under the conditions stipulated by directly applicable European Union regulation^2a), the establishment of a coordination committee as an advisory body to the coordinator.

(3) The coordinator shall allocate available airport slots based on a written request in accordance with directly applicable European Union regulation^2a). The particulars of the request shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(4) The coordinator shall be obliged to be insured against liability for damage caused by the performance of this activity no later than at the commencement of their activity.

(5) For a scheduled airport, the Office shall, under the conditions stipulated by directly applicable European Union regulation^2a), entrust the evaluation of air carriers' flight schedules and the issuance of related recommendations to a natural person with permanent residence in the Czech Republic or a legal entity with its registered office in the Czech Republic.

p33.text:
(1) If the operational condition of an airport or its part does not comply with the safe operation of the airport, the Office may decide on the temporary suspension of the operation of the airport or its part.

(2) The decision may apply only to certain types of aircraft or to certain types of flights.

p34.heading: Operational fitness of an airport
p34.text:
The airport operating license shall expire

- a) by a decision of the Office to revoke the license, if the airport operator has ceased to meet the prerequisites for issuing the license, has seriously violated the provisions of this Act, or has submitted a proposal for the revocation of the airport operating license,

- b) upon the expiry of the period for which the license was issued,

- c) on the day of the dissolution of the legal entity or upon the expiry of 30 days after the death of the natural person who is the holder of the license,

- d) on the day of legal force of the decision on permission to operate an airport issued to an applicant who has demonstrated ownership of more than half of the area of the airport land.

p34a.text:
(1) A public international airport may be operated only if its operational fitness is approved.

(2) The operational fitness of a public international airport shall be approved by the Office upon the request of the airport operator. The request shall be accompanied by a copy of the airport manual prepared by the airport operator, which contains technical and operational data about the airport operator and the airport operated by them.

(3) The airport operator shall continuously record changes to the data contained in the airport manual. The airport operator shall notify the Office of changes to the data specified in the airport manual no later than 5 working days from the day these changes occurred; a copy of the record of changes in the airport manual shall be attached to the notification.

(4) The particulars of the airport manual shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p34b.text:
(1) When approving the operational fitness of an airport, the Office shall ascertain whether all facilities, equipment, operational procedures, and the safety management system at the airport, as well as air services provided at this airport, as stated in the airport manual, are in accordance with aviation regulations. Furthermore, the Office shall ascertain whether the airport operator and airport personnel, as stated in the airport manual, meet the professional competence requirements for airport operation and maintenance stipulated by aviation regulations.

(2) The Office shall issue a decision on the approval of the operational fitness of the airport without undue delay, but no later than 1 year from the commencement of the proceedings.

p34c.text:
(1) If the Office ascertains that a public international airport does not meet the conditions for approval of operational fitness during operation, it shall order the operator of this airport to remedy the identified deficiencies and shall set a reasonable deadline for this, which may not exceed 6 months.

(2) If the airport operator fails to remedy the identified deficiencies within the stipulated deadline, the Office shall revoke the approval of the airport's operational fitness. An appeal against the decision to revoke the approval of the airport's operational fitness shall not have suspensive effect.

p34d.text: Operational fitness may also be approved for a public domestic airport. The provisions of § 34a (2) to (4), § 34b and 34c shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p35.heading: Areas designated for take-offs and landings
p35.text:
(1) Geographically defined areas in territorial planning documentation or in a decision on land use,^3) designated for aircraft take-offs and landings and related activities, may be used for take-offs and landings only under conditions stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) For take-offs and landings of specified types of aircraft during defined aviation activities, any areas may be used, provided they ensure safe take-off or landing.

(3) The characteristics of the areas, the definition of aircraft types and aviation activities for which any areas may be used for take-offs and landings, shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p36.heading: Aeronautical structure
p36.text:
(1) An aeronautical structure is

- a) a runway for aircraft take-offs and landings, regardless of its structural and technical design,

- b) runways and areas designated for aircraft movements and parking related to their take-offs and landings, regardless of their structural and technical design, and

- c) a structure serving to ensure air traffic.

(2) The Transport and Energy Building Authority shall also assess the suitability of the aeronautical structure from the perspective of air traffic safety in the occupancy permit procedure according to the Building Act.

p37.heading: Protection zones
p37.text:
(1) Protective zones shall be established around aeronautical structures. The Office shall establish a protective zone, with the exception of an airport noise protective zone, by means of a measure of a general nature after consultation with the land-use planning authority. An airport noise protective zone shall be established by the building authority by means of a measure of a general nature after consultation with the land-use planning authority and with the Office. In the measure of a general nature, the competent authority shall specify the parameters of the protective zone and individual measures for the protection of aeronautical structures.

(2) Protective zones of aeronautical structures are divided into protective zones of

- a) airports, and

- b) air navigation facilities.

(3) Protective zones of airports are divided into protective zones

- a) with a prohibition of construction,

- b) with height restrictions on construction,

- c) for protection against dangerous and misleading lights,

- d) with a prohibition of laser devices,

- e) with restrictions on the construction of high-voltage and very high-voltage overhead lines,

- f) noise^4a), and

- g) ornithological.

(4) Protective zones of air navigation facilities are divided into protective zones of

- a) radiolocation facilities,

- b) navigation facilities,

- c) communication facilities,

- d) facilities for aeronautical meteorological service,

- e) facilities for aeronautical information service,

- f) lights and radio beacons, and

- g) underground aeronautical structures.

p40.text:
(1) Within the protective zones of aeronautical structures, construction and non-construction projects may only be carried out on the basis of a permit from the competent building authority^38). In cases where a construction or non-construction project is not subject to a permit under the Building Act, the competent building authority shall issue a permit for activities in the protective zone of an aeronautical structure. The competent building authority shall issue a permit under the first or second sentence only with the consent of the Office. The Office shall grant consent if the construction or non-construction project does not impede air traffic or endanger its safety.

(2) Within the protective zones of aeronautical structures, activities may only be carried out and facilities operated with the consent of the Office. The Office shall grant consent if the activity or facility does not impede air traffic or endanger its safety.

p41.text:
(1) For the permitting of constructions and facilities outside protective zones, the consent of the Office and the Ministry of Defence is required if it concerns

- a) constructions or facilities 75 m or more in height above the terrain,

- b) constructions or facilities 30 m or more in height on natural or artificial elevations that protrude 75 m or more above the surrounding landscape,

- c) facilities that may endanger the safety of air traffic or interfere with the function of aircraft on-board instruments and air navigation facilities, in particular facilities of industrial plants, high-voltage and very high-voltage lines, energy facilities, wind power plants, and broadcasting stations.

(2) The operator of the transmission system of lines and facilities of 110 kV and more (hereinafter referred to as „lines“) is obliged, upon request of a person who, in connection with the subject of their business or activity, creates a cartographic representation of the transmission system of lines, to provide in a suitable form the current geographical coordinates of the location of the mast structures of the lines and a drawing of the line route between them.

p42.text: The operator of an airport or other aeronautical structures is entitled, even outside the protective zone, after prior demonstrable notification of the owner, to enter foreign land for the purpose of ensuring the operation of airports and other aeronautical structures, or removing obstacles restricting the operation of the airport or aeronautical structures. In doing so, they are obliged to ensure that the use of the land and structures thereon is disturbed as little as possible and that no preventable damage arises from the entry and activities. The exercise of these entitlements must be limited to the necessary time and extent. The owner of the real estate is obliged to tolerate the restriction of their ownership right to their real estate during the exercise of these entitlements. This provision does not affect the right to compensation for damage under the Civil Code.

p42a.text:
(1) The airport operator, as defined by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing rules and procedures for the introduction of operational restrictions to reduce noise at airports^35), shall prepare an annual report for each calendar year containing data on airport operations and noise situation, and on implemented and planned noise reduction measures and an assessment of their impacts (hereinafter referred to as the „noise situation report“), and shall send it to the Office by the end of March of the following calendar year. For the preparation of the noise situation report, the airport operator shall use data obtained using the method according to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing rules and procedures for the introduction of operational restrictions to reduce noise at airports^35).

(2) If, at an airport under paragraph 1, the hygienic noise limits set by the law governing the protection of public health^36) are exceeded for two consecutive calendar years, the airport operator shall supplement the noise situation report with data on the estimated number of persons exposed to noise for the preceding two calendar years, the planned development of the airport, the anticipated development of the noise situation at the airport, and the possibilities of introducing new noise reduction measures and an assessment of their impacts. The airport operator shall supplement the noise situation report by the end of the calendar year in which it was sent to the Office.

(3) The data contained in the noise situation report and its supplement shall be specified by an implementing legal regulation.

p42b.text:
(1) If, in connection with the operation of an airport designated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing rules and procedures for the introduction of operating restrictions to reduce noise at airports^35), the hygienic noise limits set by the Act regulating public health protection^36) are exceeded for three consecutive calendar years, the Office shall decide ex officio and under the conditions set by this directly applicable regulation on the introduction of operating restrictions to reduce noise at such airport.

(2) The participants in the proceedings for the introduction of operating restrictions to reduce noise at an airport shall only be the interested parties specified by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing rules and procedures for the introduction of operating restrictions to reduce noise at airports^35); this shall not apply to local residents, their representatives, and relevant local authorities (hereinafter referred to as “local residents”). The Office shall notify the participants of the initiation of proceedings by public notice. The proceedings shall commence upon the expiry of the period specified in the notice of initiation of proceedings; the period shall not be shorter than 15 days from the date of posting the notice of initiation of proceedings on the official board. Documents in the proceedings shall be served on participants by public notice; this shall not apply to the airport operator.

(3) The Office shall state in its decision the date from which the operating restrictions to reduce noise at the airport are to be introduced.

(4) Before issuing the decision, the Office shall publish on the official board a draft of the operative part and the reasoning of the decision, stating the period, place, and manner in which the participants in the proceedings may submit objections and propose additions to the proceedings against the draft, and local residents may submit comments. After evaluating the objections and comments submitted to the draft of the operative part and the reasoning of the decision and proposals for additions to the proceedings, the Office shall re-publish the amended draft on the official board and send it, in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing rules and procedures for the introduction of operating restrictions to reduce noise at airports^35), to the relevant authorities of the European Union Member States and the European Commission. No objections or comments may be raised against the re-published draft of the operative part and the reasoning of the decision; this also applies to proposals for additions to the proceedings.

(5) If the noise situation at the airport requires or permits it, the Office shall amend the decision ex officio. Paragraphs 2 to 4 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the proceedings for amending the decision. If the noise situation at the airport permits it, the Office shall revoke the decision. Paragraph 2 and the first sentence of paragraph 4 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the proceedings for revoking the decision.

p42d.text:
(1) The provisions of this Part shall apply to the negotiation of prices for the use of an airport where

- a) more than 5 million passengers are handled in a calendar year, or

- b) the highest number of passengers are handled in a calendar year, if no airport meets the condition under letter a).

(2) The Office for the Protection of Competition shall publish annually, in a manner enabling remote access, a list of airports to which the provisions of this Part apply.

p42e.text: The airport operator must not discriminate against air carriers when negotiating prices for the use of the airport. This does not prevent differentiation of prices for the use of the airport due to public interest, especially with regard to operational safety of the airport, environmental protection, or human health, based on substantial, precise, and objectively measurable criteria related to airport operations.

p42f.text:
The airport operator shall annually invite, in a manner enabling remote access, air carriers using the airport operated by it to register for participation in the discussion of the proposed price for the use of the airport (hereinafter referred to as the “airport price list”); for this purpose, it shall set a reasonable period, which must not be shorter than 60 days. Air carriers who register upon the airport operator's invitation shall provide to the operator, within the period specified in the first sentence, data anticipated for at least the next 2 years, broken down annually, to the extent of:

- a) the number of transported passengers, including transit passengers, and the anticipated number of take-offs and landings at the airport,

- b) the composition and utilization of the aircraft fleet, and

- c) requirements for airport activities and equipment included in the airport price list.

p42g.text:
(1) The airport operator shall annually discuss the "airport price list" with air carriers that have responded to its call and provided the requested data. This shall not apply if the airport operator and air carriers agree on a different frequency of discussion.

(2) For the purpose of discussing the "airport price list", the airport operator shall provide the Office for the Protection of Competition and air carriers that have responded to its published call with data to the extent of:

- a) the "airport price list", its components and the method of their determination,

- b) airport activities and equipment included in the "airport price list",

- c) public budget funds provided for airport activities or equipment that were included in the "airport price list" in the preceding calendar year,

- d) the amount of costs for airport activities and equipment that were included in the "airport price list" in the preceding calendar year,

- e) the amount of revenues associated with the payment of charges for airport use in the preceding calendar year,

- f) the number of passengers handled and the number of take-offs and landings at the airport in the preceding calendar year,

- g) the extent of utilization of airport equipment that was included in the "airport price list" in the preceding calendar year,

- h) the number of passengers handled and the number of take-offs and landings at the airport projected for at least the next 2 years, broken down annually,

- i) the amount of costs for airport activities and equipment that are included in the "airport price list", projected for at least the next 2 years, broken down annually,

- j) the amount of revenues associated with the payment of charges for airport use, projected for at least the next 2 years, broken down annually.

(3) The airport operator shall provide the requested data at least 4 months before the day from which the "airport price list" is to be applied. The airport operator may shorten this period if serious and justified circumstances so require.

(4) Data provided by the airport operator and air carriers for the purpose of discussing the "airport price list" may not be disclosed to third parties without the consent of the provider; this provision shall not affect the obligation to disclose data to authorities competent under this or a special act.

(5) An air carrier may submit its justified comments on the "airport price list" within a reasonable period set by the airport operator. The airport operator shall discuss these comments, proceeding in such a way as to achieve agreement, if possible with regard to airport use.

(6) The airport operator shall publish the "airport price list" after discussing it with air carriers. The "airport price list" may be used for the purpose of negotiating charges for airport use no earlier than 2 months from the date of its publication. If no agreement on the "airport price list" has been reached between the airport operator and air carriers, the airport operator shall publish the specific reasons that led it to reject the air carriers' comments.

p42h.text: The airport operator shall invite, by means enabling remote access, air carriers using the airport it operates to register for participation in the discussion of an airport development plan whose anticipated costs exceed 15% of the airport operator's average annual turnover for the preceding 5 calendar years. For this purpose, it shall set a reasonable period, which must not be shorter than 60 days. The airport operator shall discuss the airport development plan with the air carriers who have responded to its call no later than before the commencement of the implementation of such a plan.

p42i.text:
