(1) The Office for the Protection of Competition shall decide, upon the request of an air carrier, whether the published "airport price list", its components, and the method of their determination are not in conflict with § 42e. The request may be submitted no later than 15 days from the date on which the airport operator published the "airport price list".

(2) The request must contain data on what constitutes the conflict of the published "airport price list", its components, or the method of their determination with § 42e, and an indication of the evidence necessary to prove this conflict. If the request does not contain the specified data, the Office for the Protection of Competition shall invite the applicant to supplement the request within 3 days from the date of delivery of the invitation. If the applicant fails to supplement their request within this period, the Office for the Protection of Competition shall not consider it and shall terminate the proceedings.

(3) The participants in the proceedings are the applicant and the airport operator.

(4) If the Office for the Protection of Competition decides that the published "airport price list", its components, or the method of their determination is in conflict with § 42e, this "airport price list" cannot be used, and the airport operator shall re-discuss the amended "airport price list" without undue delay.

(5) An appeal cannot be filed against a decision under paragraph 4.

p43.text:
(1) The provisions of § 26 and § 35 to 42 shall also apply to military airports, military aviation structures, and their protective zones. The competence of the Office in matters of military airports, military aviation structures, and their protective zones shall be exercised by the Ministry of Defence.

(2) If the conditions stipulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c), under which an airport may be exempted from its scope, are met, the Office shall decide, upon the request of the builder, operator, or owner of the airport, that this directly applicable regulation and directly applicable European Union regulations issued on its basis shall not apply to the design, maintenance, and operation of the airport and safety-related equipment used at this airport. If so stipulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c) or if requested by the airport operator, the Office shall revoke the decision issued pursuant to the first sentence.

(3) If the conditions stipulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the area of aviation fuels^46), under which an airport may be included in its scope, are met, the Office shall decide, upon the request of the airport operator, that this directly applicable regulation shall apply to it in the operation of the airport.

p44.text:
(1) The airspace of the Czech Republic is accessible for flying under the conditions stipulated by this Act, by international treaties by which the Czech Republic is bound, and in compliance with the rules of flight, which stipulate procedures for flying in the airspace. The rules of flight shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) For the purpose of safe conduct of flights and effective provision of air navigation services, the Office shall issue a measure of a general nature pursuant to the Administrative Procedure Code on the division of the airspace of the Czech Republic, in agreement with the Ministry of Defence and after discussion with the person authorised to perform state administration in matters of sport flying equipment.

(3) The Office may, by a measure of a general nature issued pursuant to the Administrative Procedure Code, for a period strictly necessary or permanently, restrict or prohibit the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying over certain areas, for reasons of state defence, security reasons, or reasons of environmental protection^5) and human health. The Office shall issue a measure of a general nature pursuant to the first sentence in agreement with the Ministry of Defence and after discussion with the person authorised to perform state administration in matters of sport flying equipment and, if the measure of a general nature is to be issued for reasons of environmental protection, with the Ministry of the Environment.

(4) If flying in the airspace of the Czech Republic is immediately and seriously endangered or if a particularly significant public interest so requires, and if it is not possible to proceed otherwise due to the danger of delay, the Office shall, by a measure of a general nature pursuant to the Administrative Procedure Code, restrict or prohibit the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic or its part for flying for a period strictly necessary.

(5) Parts of the airspace of the Czech Republic may be temporarily reserved for flying by one user or category of users by:

- a) a civil-military airspace management unit within the framework of providing air navigation services, established by the person authorised to provide air navigation services on the basis of a decision of the Office on stipulating conditions for issuing a certificate pursuant to directly applicable European Union regulation^5b) and the Ministry of Defence, if the reservation period does not exceed 24 hours within three consecutive days from the day of reservation,

- b) a civil-military airspace management unit pursuant to letter a) in areas designated by the procedure pursuant to paragraph 2.

(6) The Office may, by a measure of a general nature issued pursuant to the Administrative Procedure Code, temporarily reserve parts of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying by one user or category of users on the basis of a suggestion from an airspace user, if the reservation period exceeds 24 hours within three consecutive days from the day of reservation and if it does not concern areas pursuant to paragraph 5 letter b). The Office shall issue a measure of a general nature pursuant to the first sentence in agreement with the Ministry of Defence and after discussion with the person authorised to perform state administration in matters of sport flying equipment. If the Office does not find a reason for issuing a measure of a general nature, it shall notify the submitter of the suggestion of this fact within 30 days from the day the suggestion was delivered to it.

(7) The characteristics of parts of the airspace of the Czech Republic in which flying may be restricted or prohibited, the conditions for restriction or prohibition, and the conditions for reserving parts of the airspace of the Czech Republic shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p44a.text:
(1) The Office shall deliver the draft measure of a general nature, after discussion with the affected authorities listed in § 44 (2), (3) and (6) and the person authorised to provide air traffic services, by public notice, which it shall post on its official board.

(2) Air navigation service providers and organisations associating airspace users in the Czech Republic, or, if so determined by the Office, also other persons whose legitimate interests may be directly affected by the measure of a general nature, may submit written reasoned objections against the draft measure of a general nature pursuant to § 44 (2), (3) and (6).

(3) The Office shall announce the measure of a general nature pursuant to § 44 (2), (3) and (6) by public notice, which it shall post on its official board and, after its entry into force, publish in the Aeronautical Information Publication.

p44b.text:
(1) The draft measure of a general nature pursuant to § 44 (4) shall not be published, and no comments or objections shall be submitted thereto.

(2) The Office shall send the draft measure of a general nature pursuant to § 44 (4) with its justification to the Ministry of Defence, the person authorised to perform state administration in matters of sport flying equipment, and the person authorised to provide air traffic services for their opinion and shall set a reasonable deadline for this. The Office is obliged to consider the opinion as a basis for the measure of a general nature and to address it in its justification. If the affected authorities pursuant to the first sentence do not express their opinion within the set deadline, it shall be deemed that they agree with the draft measure of a general nature.

(3) The Office shall announce the measure of a general nature pursuant to § 44 (4) by public notice, which it shall post on its official board. The measure of a general nature shall enter into force on the day of posting the public notice.

(4) Part six of the Administrative Procedure Code shall not apply to the temporary reservation of a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying by one user or category of users pursuant to § 44 (5).

p44c.text: If the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying over certain areas is permanently or temporarily dangerous, without the use of the airspace over these areas being prohibited or restricted, the Office shall publish this fact in the Aeronautical Information Publication or by other suitable means.

p44d.text: For the issuance of rules or instructions for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic exclusively concerning the flying of unmanned aircraft, § 44 (2) to (6) shall not apply. However, if it concerns the temporary reservation of a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic in which the flying of exclusively military unmanned aircraft is to be permitted, an instruction pursuant to § 44 (5) or a measure of a general nature pursuant to § 44 (6) shall be issued instead of a measure of a general nature pursuant to § 44i.

p44e.text:
(1) The Office may, by a measure of a general nature, permanently prohibit, restrict, or stipulate conditions for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying unmanned aircraft over defined areas for reasons of protecting life, health, property, or privacy of persons, protecting civil aviation from unlawful acts, critical infrastructure, or the environment, or for reasons of state security or defense.

(2) The Office shall issue the measure of a general nature in agreement with the Ministry of Defense. The Office shall also discuss the draft measure of a general nature with other affected authorities, which are

- a) the Ministry of the Environment, if the measure of a general nature is to be issued for reasons of environmental protection, and

- b) the person authorized to perform state administration in matters of sport flying equipment, if the draft measure of a general nature concerns the flying of unmanned aircraft

- 1. at an airport or within its protective zone,

- 2. at an altitude exceeding 120 m above terrain, or

- 3. in an area permanently used for take-offs and landings of sport flying equipment and within a radius of 5.5 km from its center.

(3) Objections to the draft measure of a general nature may be submitted by organizations associating unmanned aircraft system operators in the Czech Republic, as well as by unmanned aircraft system operators, if they are associations pursuant to § 54n, and, if so determined by the Office, also by other persons whose legitimate interests may be directly affected by the measure of a general nature. Objections to the draft measure of a general nature concerning the flying of unmanned aircraft pursuant to paragraph 2 letter b) point 1 may also be submitted by the operator of the affected airport.

(4) The measure of a general nature and its draft shall be published on its official board only by the Office.

p44f.text:
(1) The Office may, by a measure of a general nature, permanently define a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic in which the flying of unmanned aircraft will not be subject to some of the requirements stipulated for the open category of operation by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39).

(2) The Office shall issue the measure of a general nature in agreement with the Ministry of Defense. The Office shall also discuss the draft measure of a general nature with other affected authorities, which are the person authorized to perform state administration in matters of sport flying equipment and the municipality over whose administrative district the defined part of the airspace is to be located.

(3) The Office shall publish the measure of a general nature in the Aeronautical Information Publication after it enters into force.

p44g.text:
(1) The Office may, by a measure of a general nature, permanently or temporarily define a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic in which services for the controlled operation of unmanned aircraft systems will be provided to ensure safe, efficient, smooth, and coordinated operation of unmanned aircraft systems pursuant to a directly applicable European Union regulation governing controlled airspace for unmanned aircraft systems^40).

(2) The Office shall issue the measure of a general nature in agreement with the Ministry of Defense. The Office shall also discuss the draft measure of a general nature with other affected authorities, which are the person authorized to perform state administration in matters of sport flying equipment and, if the defined part of the airspace is to extend to an altitude lower than 300 m above terrain, the municipality over whose administrative district this part of the airspace is to be located.

(3) Objections to the draft measure of a general nature may be submitted by the common information service provider and providers of services for the controlled operation of unmanned aircraft systems pursuant to a directly applicable European Union regulation governing controlled airspace for unmanned aircraft systems^40), air navigation service providers, organizations associating unmanned aircraft system operators in the Czech Republic, as well as unmanned aircraft system operators, if they are associations pursuant to § 54n, and, if so determined by the Office, also by other persons whose legitimate interests may be directly affected by the measure of a general nature.

(4) In the measure of a general nature, the Office shall also stipulate, in accordance with a directly applicable European Union regulation governing controlled airspace for unmanned aircraft systems^40),

- a) conditions for the use of the defined part of the airspace of the Czech Republic,

- b) technical requirements that must be met by an unmanned aircraft system operated in the defined part of the airspace of the Czech Republic,

- c) conditions for the separation of unmanned and other aircraft operations in the defined part of the airspace of the Czech Republic,

- d) the definition of services for the controlled operation of unmanned aircraft systems provided in the defined part of the airspace of the Czech Republic and the requirements placed on these services,

- e) the common information service provider,

- f) rules for the provision of operational data by the common information service provider, providers of services for the controlled operation of unmanned aircraft systems, and air traffic service providers, and

- g) rules for access to data provided by the common information service provider.

(5) The Office shall publish the measure of a general nature in the Aeronautical Information Publication after it enters into force.

p44h.text:
(1) If public order, the safety of persons or property, or the defense of the state are seriously endangered, or if it is necessary due to the fulfillment of a task of the Police of the Czech Republic and it is not possible to proceed otherwise due to the danger in delay, the Office shall, by a measure of a general nature, for the period absolutely necessary, prohibit, restrict, or set conditions for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying unmanned aircraft over defined areas; this does not apply if it is necessary to immediately prohibit, restrict, or set conditions for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying unmanned aircraft due to the fulfillment of a task of the Police of the Czech Republic.

(2) The measure of a general nature shall be issued by the Office in agreement with the Ministry of Defense, which is the affected body in the proceedings concerning its proposal.

(3) The proposal for a measure of a general nature shall not be published, and no comments or objections shall be submitted thereto.

(4) The measure of a general nature shall be published only by the Office on its official board. The measure of a general nature shall become effective at the moment of posting of the public notice.

(5) A member of the Police of the Czech Republic is authorized to issue an instruction which, to the necessary extent and for the period absolutely necessary, prohibits, restricts, or sets conditions for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying unmanned aircraft over defined areas, if such an instruction must be issued immediately due to the fulfillment of a task of the Police of the Czech Republic.

p44i.text:
(1) The Office may, by a measure of a general nature, based on a stimulus from a person who demonstrates that their rights are to be protected by such a measure or that they have a legitimate interest in such a measure, temporarily, but for a maximum period of 5 consecutive days, define a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic in which

- a) it prohibits, restricts, or sets conditions for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying unmanned aircraft, or

- b) it allows unmanned aircraft to fly for one unmanned system operator or a category of unmanned system operators.

(2) If the Office does not find a reason for issuing a measure of a general nature, it shall communicate this fact to the submitter of the stimulus within 30 days from the day the stimulus was delivered to it.

(3) In the proceedings concerning the proposal for a measure of a general nature, the Office shall consider whether

- a) the protection of rights or legitimate interests pursued by the submitter of the stimulus cannot be achieved otherwise,

- b) the interest in maintaining the accessibility of the airspace of the Czech Republic does not outweigh the interest in protecting the rights or legitimate interests of the submitter of the stimulus, and

- c) the duration and scope of the requested definition of a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic are not disproportionate.

(4) The measure of a general nature shall be issued by the Office in agreement with the Ministry of Defense. The Office shall also discuss the proposal for a measure of a general nature with another affected body, which is the municipality over whose administrative district the defined part of the airspace is to be located. The period for publishing the proposal for a measure of a general nature is at least 5 days.

(5) Objections to the proposal for a measure of a general nature may be submitted within 10 days from the day of its publication by organizations associating unmanned system operators in the Czech Republic, as well as by unmanned system operators, if they are associations according to § 54n, and, if the Office so determines, also by other persons whose legitimate interests may be directly affected by the measure of a general nature.

(6) The measure of a general nature shall become effective on the fifth day from the day of posting of the public notice.

p44j.text:
(1) The digital map for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying unmanned aircraft (hereinafter referred to as the „digital map“) is a public administration information system, the administrator of which is the Office.

(2) The digital map indicates parts of the airspace of the Czech Republic and related

- a) rules for flying unmanned aircraft defined by measures of a general nature issued under this Act,

- b) instructions issued under § 44 (5) or § 44h (5), and

- c) rules for flying unmanned aircraft stipulated in a special legal regulation^41).

(3) The parts of the airspace of the Czech Republic and related instructions issued under § 44h (5) shall be indicated in the digital map immediately after their issuance by the Police of the Czech Republic.

(4) Instructions issued under § 44 (5) or § 44h (5) shall acquire legal effects no earlier than the moment of their publication in the digital map.

(5) The data maintained in the digital map are publicly accessible in an exchange format in a manner enabling remote access.

(6) The data maintained in the digital map, the method of their maintenance, and the exchange format shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p45.heading: Air Navigation Services
p45.text:
(1) The safety and regularity of flying in the airspace of the Czech Republic shall be ensured through air navigation services.

(2) Air navigation services include

- a) air traffic services, including aerodrome services,

- b) aeronautical telecommunication service,

- c) aeronautical meteorological service,

- d) aeronautical search and rescue service,

- e) aeronautical information service,

- f) pre-flight preparation and flight monitoring services,

- g) airport handling process services,

- h) flight procedure design service.

(3) When using airspace, everyone is obliged to follow the instructions of persons providing air traffic services, including aerodrome services.

(4) The characteristics of individual types of air navigation services and the activities by which their provision is ensured shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

Part 2

Air Traffic Services

p46.text:
(1) Air traffic services for civil aviation may be provided by a legal entity with its registered office in the Czech Republic, authorized by the Office. An aerodrome service may also be entrusted to a natural person with permanent residence in the Czech Republic.

(2) The Office may authorize a legal or natural person according to paragraph 1 to provide air traffic services, if such person

- a) has the technical equipment necessary for providing air traffic services,

- b) ensures the provision of air traffic services through professionally and medically fit ground aeronautical personnel,

- c) has liability insurance for damages arranged and the premium paid.

p47.text:
(1) The authorized person is obliged to comply with the rules and procedures for providing air traffic services when providing such services.

(2) The technical equipment necessary for providing air traffic services, the rules and procedures for their provision shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p48.heading: Part 3
p48.text:
The Office may withdraw the authorization if the person

- a) does not provide air traffic services by means of the prescribed technical equipment and professionally qualified ground aeronautical personnel,

- b) fails to fulfil the obligations stipulated in the rules and procedures for the provision of air traffic services,

- c) has requested the withdrawal of the authorization.

Aeronautical telecommunication service, aeronautical meteorological service, aeronautical information service, pre-flight briefing and flight monitoring service, aeronautical search and rescue service and flight procedure design service

p49.heading: Part 4
p49.text:
(1) The aeronautical telecommunication service, aeronautical meteorological service, aeronautical information service, pre-flight briefing and flight monitoring service, and flight procedure design service may be provided by a legal or natural person with the prior consent of the Office. The Office shall grant consent provided that the legal or natural person demonstrates that it possesses the technical equipment necessary for the provision of these services.

(2) The aeronautical search and rescue service is ensured by the Ministry of Transport in cooperation with the Ministry of Defence and the Ministry of the Interior. Details of mutual cooperation in ensuring the aeronautical search and rescue service shall be laid down in an agreement concluded between the Ministry of Transport, the Ministry of Defence, and the Ministry of the Interior.


Air navigation services

p49a.text:
(1) A person providing air navigation services may refuse to provide them to a recipient who is demonstrably in arrears with payment of the price for air navigation services previously provided by that person to that person or to the European Organisation for the Safety of Air Navigation (EUROCONTROL), if

- a) the total amount of their unpaid due receivables is at least CZK 100,000, and

- b) the period of delay is at least 3 months.

(2) The provision of air navigation services may not be refused if this would jeopardize the safety or regularity of air traffic, especially if it concerns an aircraft operated by the recipient of these services in flight.

(3) Unless otherwise agreed with the recipient, the person providing air navigation services shall commence their provision immediately after the recipient has paid all due receivables related to previously provided air navigation services.

(4) The person providing air navigation services shall immediately notify the refusal to provide them to

- a) the Office,

- b) the airport operator at which the provision of these services was refused,

- c) the European Organisation for the Safety of Air Navigation (EUROCONTROL).

Part 5

Ground handling services at a public airport
p49a.heading: Refusal to provide air navigation services

p49b.text:
(1) Ground handling services at a public airport^5f) (hereinafter referred to as „ground handling services“) may be provided for the needs of others by a legal entity, a natural person, including an air carrier and an airport operator; only an air carrier may provide these services for its own needs.

(2) Ground handling services may be provided on the basis of consent granted by the Office.

p49c.heading: Conditions for granting consent
p49c.text:
(1) The Office shall grant consent to provide „ground handling services“ on the basis of a written application, if the applicant proves that

- a) they are a natural person with permanent residence in the Czech Republic or a legal entity with its registered office in the Czech Republic,

- b) the natural person and their responsible representative, if appointed, have reached the age of 18, are fully capable of legal acts, are of good character and professionally competent, if the applicant is a natural person,

- c) the statutory body or a member of the statutory body have reached the age of 18, are fully capable of legal acts, are of good character and at least one member of the statutory body is professionally competent, if the applicant is a legal entity,

- d) they have technical equipment for providing „ground handling services“,

- e) they have liability insurance for damages arising from the provision of „ground handling services“ and the insurance premium has been paid,

- f) they have financial security for providing „ground handling services“.

(2) The particulars of the written application for granting consent, details on the method of proving financial security for „ground handling services“, and the particulars of the decision on granting consent shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p49d.heading: Good character
p49d.text:
(1) A person shall not be considered of good character according to § 49c if they have been finally convicted of a criminal offense whose factual basis is related to the provision of ground handling services, or if they have been finally convicted of another criminal offense committed intentionally, if, given the nature of the provision of ground handling services and the person of the applicant for consent to provide ground handling services, there is a concern that they will commit the same or a similar act when providing ground handling services, unless they are considered as not having been convicted.

(2) For the purpose of ascertaining whether a person according to § 49c para. 1 letter b) and c) meets the condition of good character, the Office shall request an extract from the Criminal Register according to a special legal regulation^1m). The request for an extract from the Criminal Register and the extract from the Criminal Register shall be submitted electronically, in a manner enabling remote access.

p49e.text: The applicant proves professional competence by a document of completed university education in the field of economics, in the field of technical sciences and technologies or in the field of law and a document of three years of practice in management activities in the field of civil aviation or a document of secondary or higher vocational education in the field of economics, in the field of technical sciences and technologies or in the field of law and a document of at least five years of practice in management activities in the field of civil aviation.

p49f.heading: Measures for regulating ground handling services for safety reasons
p49f.text:
(1) For the purpose of ensuring the safety of civil aviation and the safety of public airport operations, the Office may issue measures for regulating ground handling services consisting of baggage, cargo and mail handling, technical and operational aircraft handling on the apron, and handling of aviation fuels and oils. By means of measures for regulating ground handling services, the Office may, for a public airport,

- a) where at least three million passengers or 75 thousand tons of cargo are handled in a calendar year, limit the number of providers of ground handling services for the needs of others, to at least two providers, one of whom may be the airport operator,

- b) where at least one million passengers or 25 thousand tons of cargo are handled in a calendar year, reserve the provision of ground handling services for own needs to a limited number of air carriers, to at least two air carriers.

(2) The definition of the reason for ensuring safety shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p49g.heading: Measures for regulating ground handling services due to airport operational congestion
p49g.text:
(1) In the event of operational congestion at a public airport, which results in a limitation of the usable space for the provision of ground handling services, the Office may issue a measure to regulate ground handling services. By a measure to regulate ground handling services due to operational congestion at an airport, the Office may, for a public airport,

- a) where at least three million passengers or 75 thousand tonnes of cargo are handled in a calendar year,

- 1. limit the number of ground handling service providers for third-party needs consisting of passenger handling and catering of aircraft with food and beverages,

- 2. reserve the provision of ground handling services for third-party needs consisting of baggage, cargo, and mail handling, technical and operational handling of aircraft on the apron, and handling of aviation fuels and oils to a single ground handling service provider,

- b) where at least one million passengers or 25 thousand tonnes of cargo are handled in a calendar year,

- 1. reserve the provision of ground handling services for own needs consisting of passenger handling services and catering of aircraft with food and beverages to a limited number of air carriers,

- 2. prohibit an air carrier from providing ground handling services for own needs consisting of baggage, cargo, and mail handling services, technical and operational handling of aircraft on the apron, and handling of aviation fuels and oils, or reserve their provision to a single air carrier.

(2) The definition of reasons for operational congestion at an airport shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p49h.text:
(1) In the general measure by which a measure to regulate ground handling services pursuant to § 49f and 49g is introduced, the Office shall state

- a) the type of ground handling services to which the measure applies,

- b) the area of the public airport to which the measure applies,

- c) the procedure aimed at overcoming the introduced restrictions,

- d) the date on which the measure is introduced, and

- e) the period for which the measure is introduced.

(2) After initiating the proceedings, the Office shall notify the European Commission of the proposed measure, including the reasons for its introduction and the date on which the measure is to be introduced; it must do so at least 4 months before the date on which the measure is to be introduced. The proceedings shall be suspended for a period of 3 months from the date of notification.

(3) If the European Commission disagrees with the proposed measure, the Office shall terminate the proceedings. If the European Commission requests changes to the proposed measure, the Office shall be bound by them when issuing the measure.

p49i.heading: Air Carrier Committee
p49i.text: The Office shall ensure that an air carrier committee, composed of representatives of air carriers using the public airport, or representatives of organizations representing these air carriers, if the air carriers reserve this option, is established at a public airport where the provision of ground handling services may be regulated by a measure pursuant to § 49f (1) and § 49g (1).

p49j.heading: Selection Procedure
p49j.text:
(1) The Office shall announce a selection procedure for granting consent in cases where the provision of ground handling services will be regulated by a measure pursuant to § 49f (1) (a) or § 49g (1) (a).

(2) The provisions on commercial public tenders under the Commercial Code shall apply mutatis mutandis to the selection procedure. The conditions and procedure of the selection procedure and the method of evaluating applications for the provision of ground handling services shall be determined by the Office after consultation with the public airport operator and the air carrier committee and published in the Aeronautical Information Publication. The conditions stipulated in § 49c for granting consent must be part of the selection procedure conditions. The Office shall submit the conditions and procedure of the selection procedure for publication in the Official Journal of the EU.

(3) Based on the assessment and evaluation of applications for granting consent in the selection procedure and after consultation with the public airport operator and the air carrier committee, the Office shall grant consent for the provision of ground handling services to the applicant who best met the conditions stipulated in the selection procedure.

(4) Consent for the provision of ground handling services based on a selection procedure may be granted for a maximum period of

- a) 7 years, if granted based on a regulatory measure pursuant to § 49f (1),

- b) 3 years, if granted based on a regulatory measure pursuant to § 49g (1) (a) point 1,

- c) 2 years, if granted based on a regulatory measure pursuant to § 49g (1) (a) point 2.

(5) If the provision of ground handling services is regulated pursuant to § 49f (1) or § 49g (1), the public airport operator may provide ground handling services at the airport it operates without a selection procedure, based on consent granted pursuant to § 49b.

p49k.heading: Consent
p49k.text:
(1) The Office shall grant consent based on a selection procedure in cases where the provision of ground handling services is regulated pursuant to § 49f (1) (a) or § 49g (1) (a) point 1, so that at least one of the ground handling service providers for third-party needs is not controlled by

- a) the airport operator, or

- b) an air carrier whose performance exceeded 25% of passengers and cargo recorded at the airport during the calendar year preceding the year in which consent for the provision of ground handling services for third-party needs was granted, or

- c) the Ministry of Transport or the Office.

(2) The Office shall decide on the granting of consent based on a selection procedure within 120 days from the announcement of the commercial public tender.

p49l.heading: Change of Data
p49l.text: If the conditions stipulated in § 49c (1) change, the holder of consent for the provision of ground handling services is obliged to notify the Office of changes concerning the data on the basis of which the consent was granted, and to submit to the Office documents proving these changes within 30 days from the date of the changes.

p49m.heading: Withdrawal of Consent
p49m.text:
The Office shall withdraw consent granted for the provision of ground handling services if the ground handling service provider

- a) violates the conditions stipulated for the provision of ground handling services in the decision on granting consent,

- b) ceased to meet any of the conditions on the basis of which consent was granted to him,

- c) requested the withdrawal of consent,

- d) fails to fulfill the obligation imposed in § 49l.

p49n.heading: Access to Airport Facilities
p49n.text: The operator of a public aerodrome is obliged to enable a legal or natural person providing ground handling services for third parties, and an air carrier providing ground handling services for its own needs, to access aerodrome facilities to the extent necessary to provide these services. The operator of a public aerodrome shall negotiate the price for access to and use of aerodrome facilities with the ground handling service provider in accordance with price regulations.^9)

p49o.heading: Account Management
p49o.text: The ground handling service provider must maintain separate accounting for costs, revenues, and proceeds for the provision of these services. The aerodrome operator must not transfer financial resources obtained from aerodrome operations to ground handling services. The Office supervises the maintenance of separate accounting.

p50.heading: Flight of military, police, and customs aircraft
p50.text:
(1) The flight of military, police, and customs aircraft shall be governed by this Act, with the exception of flights for defence, training, and state security purposes.

(2) For flights in the same part of the airspace, uniform flight rules shall apply to military, police, and customs aircraft and civil aviation.

(3) If the safety of persons, property, or public order cannot be ensured otherwise, police aircraft may exceptionally deviate from flight rules to the extent absolutely necessary when flying.

p50a.heading: Flight of fire brigade unmanned aircraft
p50a.text: If the fulfilment of the primary task of the Fire and Rescue Service of the Czech Republic according to the Fire and Rescue Service Act cannot be ensured otherwise, fire brigade unmanned aircraft may exceptionally deviate from flight rules to the extent absolutely necessary when flying. In such a case, increased caution must be exercised to avoid endangering flight safety, and coordination of the flight of fire brigade unmanned aircraft with the flight of military, police, and customs aircraft must be ensured.

p51.heading: Air navigation services for military purposes
p51.text:
(1) Air navigation services for military purposes may be merged with air navigation services for civil aviation.

(2) Air traffic services for civil aviation and for military purposes must be mutually coordinated and provided in mutual cooperation.

(3) Details on mutual coordination and cooperation in the provision of air traffic services or merged air traffic services shall be stipulated by an agreement concluded between the Office and the Ministry of Defence.

p51a.heading: Terrain and Obstacle Data Database
p51a.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport maintains an electronic database of geographical, topographical, and geodetic data on terrain and obstacles in the territory of the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as the „database“).

(2) The Ministry of Transport may, with the consent of the Ministry of Defence, decide to entrust a legal entity with the maintenance of the database based on its request, if this entity

- a) provides air navigation information service,

- b) has an administrative system for database maintenance established,

- c) has equipment for database maintenance and ensures that data stored in the database are not lost or altered, and

- d) ensures that the database is maintained by professionally qualified persons who have completed at least secondary education with a maturita examination or another equivalent examination proving the same level of attained education.

(3) The Ministry of Transport shall specify the conditions for database maintenance and the period of its validity in the entrustment.

(4) The Ministry of Transport shall withdraw the entrustment if the person entrusted with database maintenance

- a) ceased to meet the conditions for issuing the entrustment,

- b) seriously violated the provisions of this Act,

- c) seriously violated the conditions stipulated in the entrustment, or

- d) requested the withdrawal of the entrustment.

(5) The entrustment shall expire

- a) upon the expiry of the period for which it was issued,

- b) on the day of the dissolution of the legal entity that is its holder.

(6) The Ministry of Transport or the person entrusted with database maintenance shall ensure the protection of the database against misuse and interference by unauthorized persons.

(7) The Ministry of Defence and the operator of an aerodrome capable of accepting instrument flights shall provide the data specified in paragraph 1 to the Ministry of Transport or the person entrusted with database maintenance free of charge.

(8) The Ministry of Defence may, upon request of the operator of an aerodrome capable of accepting instrument flights, who operates an aerodrome owned by the Czech Republic, which the Ministry of Defence is entitled to manage, provide the Ministry of Transport or the person entrusted with database maintenance, free of charge, the results of geodetic activities processed for the needs of state defence, for the purpose of processing data according to paragraph 1.

(9) The Ministry of Transport or the person entrusted with database maintenance shall make the database accessible to the Ministry of Defence and the Office free of charge. The person entrusted with database maintenance shall also make the database accessible to the Ministry of Transport free of charge.

(10) The Ministry of Transport or the person entrusted with database maintenance shall publish data from the database in the Aeronautical Information Publication according to § 99 (2) whenever they change, but at least once a year.

(11) The definition of data stored in the database, their scope and properties, the method of database maintenance, the documents to be submitted with the request for entrustment with database maintenance, the requirements for the method of database protection, the data provided by the Ministry of Defence and the operator of an aerodrome capable of accepting instrument flights, their scope, properties, form, and frequency of provision, and the scope of data published in the Aeronautical Information Publication shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p53.heading: Mandatory Airspace Exit
p53.text:
(1) An aircraft that has entered a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic where flying is prohibited or restricted must, as soon as it ascertains this, leave this area. If the aircraft does not leave this area, it may be forced to leave the area for reasons of ensuring flight safety in the airspace.

(2) Procedures for forcing an exit from prohibited or restricted airspace shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p54.heading: Aircraft Commander
p54.text: The aircraft commander is responsible for the safe conduct of the flight. Within the scope of this responsibility, the aircraft commander is authorized to issue commands to all persons present on board the aircraft. All persons present on board the aircraft are obliged to comply with the commands of the aircraft commander.

p54a.heading: Register of Unmanned Aircraft System Operators
p54a.text:
(1) The register of unmanned aircraft system operators is an information system of public administration, the administrator of which is the Office. Data shall be kept in the register in the manner and to the extent specified by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39).

(2) An unmanned aircraft system operator who is obliged to register in the register of unmanned aircraft system operators according to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) may also submit an application for the purpose of fulfilling this obligation in a manner enabling remote access via a user interface specified by the Office; in this case, the unmanned aircraft system operator must be identified by means of electronic identification according to legal regulations governing electronic identification^42).

(3) The Office shall ensure the provision of data kept in the register of unmanned aircraft system operators in a manner enabling remote and continuous access

- a) to the Ministry of Transport, the Ministry of Defence and the Police of the Czech Republic,

- b) to the competent authority of another Member State of the European Union, the Agency and the European Commission, and

- c) to air traffic service providers, common information service providers and unmanned aircraft system controlled airspace service providers according to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing unmanned aircraft system controlled airspace^40), if they provide services in the Czech Republic's airspace designated for unmanned aircraft system controlled airspace according to § 44g.

(4) The Office shall also provide data kept in the register upon request to a person who demonstrates a legal interest in their provision.

(5) The unmanned aircraft system operator shall notify the Office of a change in data recorded in the register of unmanned aircraft system operators within 15 days from the date on which it occurred; the notification may also be made in the manner specified in paragraph 2. The Office shall record the change in data in the register based on the notification.

(6) The Office shall record in the register of unmanned aircraft system operators the information that the unmanned aircraft system operator has ceased activities consisting of operating unmanned aircraft systems, if the unmanned aircraft system operator has notified the Office of this fact or if the Office has learned that the unmanned aircraft system operator has died or ceased to exist. The Office shall delete the entry of the unmanned aircraft system operator from the register after 5 years from the date on which the information about the cessation of activities consisting of operating unmanned aircraft systems was recorded in the register.

p54b.heading: Submission via user interface
p54b.text:
(1) An applicant for an authorisation for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems in the specific category of operation according to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) may also submit an application for this authorisation in a manner enabling remote access via a user interface specified by the Office; in this case, the applicant must be identified by means of electronic identification according to legal regulations governing electronic identification^42).

(2) A person who intends to operate an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation in accordance with a standard scenario according to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) may also make a declaration of conformity with the standard scenario in a manner enabling remote access via a user interface specified by the Office; in this case, this person must be identified by means of electronic identification according to legal regulations governing electronic identification^42).

p54c.heading: Restriction and withdrawal of authorisations of unmanned aircraft system operators
p54c.text:
(1) The Office shall withdraw an authorisation for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or light unmanned aircraft systems in the specific category of operation if its holder has requested it. If the holder of an authorisation for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or light unmanned aircraft systems has ceased to meet the conditions for its granting specified by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) or has seriously violated an obligation specified by this directly applicable regulation, the Office shall restrict or withdraw the authorisation according to the nature and extent of the identified deficiencies. The restriction of the authorisation shall be carried out by restricting the purpose or method of operating unmanned aircraft systems or by restricting a part of the airspace of the Czech Republic that may be used for flying unmanned aircraft.

(2) If the Office has decided to restrict an authorisation for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or light unmanned aircraft systems, it shall issue the unmanned aircraft system operator with a new document certifying the authorisation for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems with a validity period corresponding to the validity period of the original document or a new certificate of the light unmanned aircraft system operator. The unmanned aircraft system operator shall hand over the original document or certificate to the Office within 5 days from the date of issue of the new document or certificate.

(3) If the Office has decided to withdraw an authorisation for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or light unmanned aircraft systems, the unmanned aircraft system operator shall hand over the document certifying the authorisation for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or the certificate of the light unmanned aircraft system operator to the Office within 5 days from the date of entry into force of the decision.

p54d.heading: Insurance
p54d.text:
(1) The operator of an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A2 of the open operational category, the operator of an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A3 of the open operational category, if the maximum take-off mass of the unmanned aircraft exceeds 4 kg, and the operator of an unmanned aircraft system in the specific operational category must have liability insurance for damages from the operation of the unmanned aircraft system concluded and the insurance premium paid at all times during the operation of the unmanned aircraft system; this does not apply if the operator of the unmanned aircraft system is an association under § 54n or a member of such an association.

(2) Unless otherwise stipulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the insurance of air carriers and aircraft operators^10a), liability insurance for damages from the operation of an unmanned aircraft system shall be concluded

- a) in the open operational category in relation to all unmanned aircraft systems operated by their operator, and

- b) in the specific operational category in relation to each unmanned aircraft system separately.

(3) The minimum amount of the insurance benefit limit for individual operational categories and subcategories shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation, unless the amount of the insurance benefit limit is stipulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the insurance of air carriers and aircraft operators^10a).

Part 2

Remote pilots

p54e.text:
(1) The Office shall issue a certificate of competency for piloting an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open operational category upon request by a person who has completed training and successfully passed an examination of theoretical knowledge required for piloting an unmanned aircraft system in the open operational category pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39). The Office shall issue the certificate instead of a written decision.

(2) The training and examination shall be conducted by the Office or a person authorized by it. The applicant for their completion shall undergo the training and examination

- a) by means of remote access after their identification through electronic identification means pursuant to legal regulations governing electronic identification^42), or

- b) using computer technology at a location designated by the Office or a person authorized by it after the Office or the person authorized by it verifies their identity.

(3) The examination shall be conducted in the form of a test pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39). The examination shall be evaluated and its result shall be received by the Office immediately after its completion in an automated manner.

(4) A person who has completed training and successfully passed the examination shall submit an application for the issuance of a certificate of competency for piloting an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open operational category immediately after its successful completion, namely

- a) by means of remote access, or

- b) using computer technology at the place of their conduct.

(5) The Office shall stop the proceedings on an application that was not submitted immediately after the successful completion of the examination by a resolution.

(6) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the application must state whether the issuance of the certificate is requested in paper or electronic form. If the applicant requests the issuance of the certificate in electronic form and does not have a data box accessible, they shall also state in the application the electronic address to which it is requested to be sent. The Office shall send the certificate in electronic form via a public data network to the data box or to the stated electronic address. If the certificate is sent to the electronic address stated in the application, the decision on its issuance shall become final and legally effective on the day of dispatch.

(7) The holder of a certificate of competency for piloting an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open operational category shall notify the Office of a change in the data contained in this certificate within 15 days from the date on which it occurred, and at the same time submit documents proving the change. The Office shall issue a new certificate with the changed data and with a period of validity corresponding to the period of validity of the original certificate. Paragraph 6 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the content of the notification and the procedure of the Office.

(8) If the holder of a certificate of competency for piloting an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open operational category has seriously violated the remote pilot's obligation stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39), the Office shall decide on their disqualification from piloting an unmanned aircraft system in this subcategory. On the day the decision becomes final, the certificate shall lose its validity. If the certificate was issued in paper form, the person whose disqualification from piloting an unmanned aircraft system has been decided shall hand it over to the Office within 15 days from the day the decision becomes final. A person whose disqualification from piloting an unmanned aircraft system has been decided may submit an application for the issuance of a new certificate no earlier than 6 months after the day this decision becomes final.

(9) The model of the certificate of competency for piloting an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open operational category shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p54f.text:
(1) The Office shall issue a certificate of competency to operate an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A2 of the open category of operation pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) upon request by a person who, no later than 3 months before submitting the request, has successfully passed an additional theoretical knowledge examination pursuant to this directly applicable regulation. The Office shall issue the certificate instead of a written decision.

(2) The additional examination shall be conducted by the Office or a person authorized by it.

(3) A person may take the additional examination if they

- a) prove their identity,

- b) hold a valid certificate of competency to operate an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open category of operation, and

- c) declare under oath that they have acquired practical skills for operating an unmanned aircraft system pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39).

(4) Before taking the additional examination, the Office or the authorized person shall verify the fulfillment of the conditions under paragraph 3. The additional examination shall be conducted using computer technology at a location designated by the Office or a person authorized by it and shall take the form of a test pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39). The examination shall be evaluated, and its result shall be automatically received by the Office immediately after its completion. If the additional examination is conducted by an authorized person, the costs of its conduct shall be borne by the applicant.

(5) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the request must state whether the issuance of the certificate is requested in paper or electronic form. If the applicant requests the issuance of the certificate in electronic form and does not have an accessible data box, they shall also state in the request the electronic address to which it is to be sent. The Office shall send the certificate in electronic form via the public data network to the data box or to the specified electronic address. If the certificate is sent to the electronic address specified in the request, the decision on its issuance shall become final and legally effective on the date of dispatch.

(6) The holder of a certificate of competency to operate an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A2 of the open category of operation shall notify the Office of any change in the data contained in this certificate within 15 days from the date on which it occurred, and at the same time submit documents proving the change. The Office shall issue a new certificate with the changed data and with a period of validity corresponding to the period of validity of the original certificate. Paragraph 5 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the content of the notification and the procedure of the Office.

(7) If the holder of a certificate of competency to operate an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A2 of the open category of operation has seriously violated the remote pilot's duty established by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39), the Office shall decide on their disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system in this subcategory. On the date the decision becomes final, the certificate shall cease to be valid. If the certificate was issued in paper form, the person whose disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system has been decided shall surrender it to the Office within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final. A person whose disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system has been decided may submit a request for the issuance of a new certificate no earlier than 6 months after the date this decision becomes final.

p54g.text:
(1) The Office shall issue a certificate of theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) upon request by a person who, no later than 3 months before submitting the request, successfully passed an additional theoretical knowledge examination pursuant to this directly applicable regulation. The Office shall issue the certificate instead of a written decision.

(2) The additional examination shall be conducted by the Office or a person authorized by it.

(3) A person may take the additional examination if they

- a) prove their identity and

- b) hold a valid certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategories A1 and A3 of the open category of operation.

(4) Before taking the additional examination, the Office or the authorized person shall verify compliance with the conditions under paragraph 3. The additional examination shall be conducted using computer technology at a location designated by the Office or a person authorized by it and shall take the form of a test pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39). The examination shall be evaluated, and its result shall be automatically received by the Office immediately after its completion. If the additional examination is conducted by an authorized person, the costs of its execution shall be borne by the applicant.

(5) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the request must state whether the certificate is requested in paper or electronic form. If the applicant requests the certificate in electronic form and does not have a data box enabled, they shall also provide an email address in the request to which it should be sent. The Office shall send the certificate in electronic form via the public data network to the data box or to the specified email address. If the certificate is sent to the email address specified in the request, the decision on its issuance shall become final and legally effective on the date of dispatch.

(6) The holder of a certificate of theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation shall notify the Office of any change in the data contained in this certificate within 15 days from the date it occurred, and at the same time submit documents proving the change. The Office shall issue a new certificate with the changed data and with a validity period corresponding to the validity period of the original certificate. Paragraph 5 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the content of the notification and the procedure of the Office.

(7) If the holder of a certificate of theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation has seriously violated the remote pilot's obligation stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39), the Office shall decide on their disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario. On the date the decision becomes final, the certificate shall cease to be valid. If the certificate was issued in paper form, the person whose disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system has been decided shall surrender it to the Office within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final. A person whose disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system has been decided may submit a request for a new certificate no earlier than 6 months after the date this decision becomes final.

p54h.text:
(1) The Office may authorize a person to conduct instruction and examinations using computer technology pursuant to § 54e (2) (b) and additional examinations pursuant to § 54f and 54g, provided that such person has the right to use non-residential premises and equipment necessary for carrying out these activities and ensures supervision over their conduct, either personally or through an employee. In the authorization, the Office shall stipulate the conditions for ensuring the proper conduct of these activities.

(2) The Office shall revoke the authorization under paragraph 1 if the authorized person

- a) has requested it,

- b) has ceased to meet any of the conditions for authorization, or

- c) has seriously violated an obligation stipulated by this Act or a condition stipulated in the authorization.

p54i.text:
(1) Upon request of the holder of a valid certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 or A2 of the open category of operation, or the holder of a valid certificate of theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation, the Authority shall issue a new certificate if the applicant has completed training to maintain theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system for the relevant subcategory of the open category of operation or in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation. The Authority shall issue a new certificate in lieu of a written decision.

(2) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the application must state whether the issuance of the certificate is requested in paper or electronic form. If the applicant requests the issuance of the certificate in electronic form and does not have an activated data box, the application must also include the electronic address to which it is requested to be sent. The Authority shall send the certificate in electronic form via a public data network to the data box or to the specified electronic address. If the certificate is sent to the electronic address specified in the application, the decision on its issuance shall become final and legally effective on the date of dispatch.

(3) The training shall be conducted by the Authority or a person authorized by it.

(4) The Authority may authorize a person to conduct the training if that person

- a) is professionally qualified to conduct such training or ensures its conduct through its professionally qualified employee, and

- b) has a developed training plan ensuring the conduct of training within the scope of topics stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39).

(5) A person is professionally qualified to conduct training if they

- a) have completed at least secondary education with a maturita examination,

- b) have at least 3 years of experience in operating or managing unmanned aircraft systems, and

- c) have successfully passed an examination before a three-member expert commission, whose members are appointed and dismissed by the Director General of the Authority.

(6) In the authorization, the Authority shall set conditions for ensuring the proper conduct of the training.

(7) The training shall be conducted at a location designated by the Authority or a person authorized by it. The Authority or the authorized person shall verify the identity of the training participant before its commencement. The authorized person shall conduct the training in accordance with the training plan ensuring the conduct of training within the scope of topics stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39). The authorized person shall electronically send confirmation of the participant's completion of the training to the Authority immediately after its conclusion. The costs of attending the training shall be borne by its participant.

(8) The Authority shall withdraw the authorization if the authorized person

- a) has requested it,

- b) has ceased to meet any of the conditions for authorization, or

- c) has seriously violated an obligation stipulated by this Act or a condition stipulated in the authorization.

(9) The length of training to maintain theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system for subcategories of the open category of operation or in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation, and the content, form, and course of the examination proving professional qualification to conduct the training, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p54j.text:
The Authority shall withdraw the authorization to conduct practical skills training required for operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) granted under this directly applicable regulation, if the authorized person

- a) has requested it,

- b) has ceased to meet any of the conditions for authorization stipulated by this directly applicable regulation, or

- c) has seriously violated an obligation stipulated by this directly applicable regulation.

p54k.text:
(1) Operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation, unless it is operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario or operating an unmanned aircraft system pursuant to § 54n, may only be performed by a person who holds a certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation. The certificate shall be issued by the unmanned aircraft system operator who holds an authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or light unmanned aircraft systems, to a person who

- a) holds a valid certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategories A1 and A3 of the open category of operation,

- b) has completed training to acquire theoretical knowledge and practical skills necessary for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation, and

- c) is in a contractual relationship with this unmanned aircraft system operator, the subject of which is the operation of an unmanned aircraft system for this operator; this does not apply if the certificate is issued to this unmanned aircraft system operator.

(2) The provision of training shall be ensured by the unmanned aircraft system operator who holds an authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or light unmanned aircraft systems,

- a) to the extent and in the manner stipulated in the document certifying the authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or in the certificate of the light unmanned aircraft system operator, and

- b) in cooperation with a person authorized by the Office on the basis of a contract concluded with them by the unmanned aircraft system operator.

(3) The Office shall authorize a person who is professionally competent to cooperate with the unmanned aircraft system operator in providing training or shall ensure such cooperation through its professionally competent employee. A person is professionally competent if they

- a) have completed at least secondary education with a maturita examination,

- b) have at least 3 years of experience in operating or managing unmanned aircraft systems, and

- c) have successfully passed an examination before a three-member expert commission, whose members are appointed and dismissed by the Director General of the Office.

(4) In the authorization, the Office shall stipulate the conditions for proper cooperation with the unmanned aircraft system operator in providing training. Cooperation must not be carried out by a person whose relationship with the unmanned aircraft system operator or the activities performed by them, or with the remote pilot, jeopardizes its proper execution.

(5) The Office shall withdraw the authorization if the authorized person

- a) has requested it, or

- b) has seriously violated an obligation stipulated by this Act or a condition stipulated in the authorization.

(6) If the holder of a certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation has seriously violated the remote pilot's obligation stipulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39), the Office shall decide on their disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system in this category. The participant in the proceedings shall also be the unmanned aircraft system operator who issued the certificate. The unmanned aircraft system operator may issue a new certificate to a person whose disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system has been decided, no earlier than 6 months from the date on which this decision became final. If the Office ascertains that a certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation was issued to a person before the expiry of this period by another unmanned aircraft system operator, it shall immediately inform them of this fact and request the holder of such issued certificate to return it to the unmanned aircraft system operator.

(7) The holder of a certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation shall return this certificate to the unmanned aircraft system operator who issued it, immediately after

- a) they ceased to be the holder of a valid certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategories A1 and A3 of the open category of operation,

- b) a decision was made on their disqualification from operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation,

- c) their contractual relationship with the unmanned aircraft system operator, the subject of which was the operation of an unmanned aircraft system for this operator, ceased to exist, or

- d) they were requested by the Office pursuant to paragraph 6 to return it.

(8) If, in the case of the holder of a certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation, who issued this certificate, a fact according to paragraph 7 letter a) or b) occurred, they must not operate an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation until the re-issuance of the certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategories A1 and A3 of the open category of operation or until the issuance of a new certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation, unless it is operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario or operating an unmanned aircraft system pursuant to § 54n.

(9) The unmanned aircraft system operator shall immediately after

- a) issuing a certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation, send a copy thereof to the Office, and

- b) a reason for its return according to paragraph 7 letter a) or c) arose for the holder of the certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation issued by them, communicate this fact to the Office.

(10) The template for the certificate of competency for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation, the topics that the training for acquiring theoretical knowledge and practical skills necessary for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation must cover, and the content, form, and course of the examination proving professional competence for cooperation with the unmanned aircraft system operator in providing training, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p54l.text:
(1) The register of remote pilots is a public administration information system, the administrator of which is the Office. The register contains data on the holder of a document certifying competence to operate an unmanned system, which are:

- a) first name(s) and surname,

- b) date of birth,

- c) address of residence, and

- d) data on competence to operate an unmanned system.

(2) The Office shall ensure, in a manner enabling remote and continuous access, the provision of data kept in the register of remote pilots to the Ministry of Transport, the Ministry of Defence, and the Police of the Czech Republic. The Office shall also provide data kept in the register upon request to a person who demonstrates a legal interest in their provision.

(3) Data kept in the register of remote pilots shall be retained for the period of validity of the document certifying competence to operate an unmanned system and for 5 years after its expiry.

(4) The data on competence to operate an unmanned system, which are kept in the register of remote pilots, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p54m.heading: Part 3
p54m.text:
(1) The Police of the Czech Republic and the Office may call upon a remote pilot to:

- a) prove their identity,

- b) present a document certifying their competence to operate an unmanned system,

- c) undergo an indicative examination or a specialized medical examination pursuant to the Act on the Protection of Health from the Harmful Effects of Addictive Substances, or

- d) interrupt the flight of an unmanned aircraft for the purpose of exercising control or ending a threat to flight safety.

(2) The remote pilot is obliged to comply with the call pursuant to paragraph 1.


Associations

p54n.heading: Operation and control of unmanned systems within an association
p54n.text:
(1) An unmanned system may be operated or controlled by a member of an association, if it is an association that has been granted authorization to carry out recreational, sports, or competitive flights or public air displays (hereinafter referred to as „association authorization“). An unmanned system may also be operated by an association that has been granted an association authorization (hereinafter referred to as „authorized association“).

(2) An unmanned system may also be controlled by a person who does not have permanent or other residence in the Czech Republic, is not a member of an authorized association, and is a member of a similar organization with its registered office in the territory of another state, if they participate in a competition or public air display organized by an authorized association (hereinafter referred to as „international event participant“) at the place and time of their occurrence; the authorized association shall be considered the operator of such an unmanned system.

p54o.heading: Association authorization

p54p.heading: Duties of a member of an authorized association and a participant in an international event, and duties related to the termination of membership in an authorized association
p54p.text:
(1) A member of an authorized association operating or controlling an unmanned aircraft system and a participant in an international event shall comply with the rules of the association.
