(2) If the rules of the association or a directly applicable European Union regulation governing insurance for air carriers and aircraft operators^10a) so stipulate, a member of an authorized association, if they are an unmanned aircraft system operator, must have liability insurance for damages arising from the operation of an unmanned aircraft system and have paid the insurance premium with a minimum limit of insurance coverage set by the rules of the association or by this directly applicable regulation.

(3) A person whose membership in an authorized association has ceased shall return the association member's unmanned aircraft system pilot card to the association within 15 days from the date on which they became aware of this fact. If the association member's unmanned aircraft system pilot card was issued in electronic form, a person whose membership in the association has ceased must not use this card.

Part 4

Common information service and services for controlled unmanned aircraft system operations

p54q.heading: Publication of data on service providers
p54q.text: The Office shall publish, in a manner enabling remote access, data on providers of the common information service and providers of services for controlled unmanned aircraft system operations pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the controlled unmanned aircraft system airspace^40), to whom it has issued a certificate pursuant to this directly applicable regulation. The published data shall include the business name, name or names, and surname, registered office address, and identification number of the provider, if assigned, and a description of the services provided by them.

p54r.heading: Restriction and withdrawal of authorization to provide services
p54r.text:
(1) If a provider of the common information service or a provider of services for controlled unmanned aircraft system operations pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the controlled unmanned aircraft system airspace^40) ceases to meet the conditions for granting authorization set by this directly applicable regulation, or does not provide the service to the extent or in the manner specified by this directly applicable regulation, or has seriously breached another obligation stipulated by it, the Office shall, depending on the nature and extent of the deficiencies found, restrict or withdraw the authorization for the provision of services by this provider. The Office shall also restrict or withdraw the authorization for the provision of services by a provider of the common information service or a provider of services for controlled unmanned aircraft system operations pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the controlled unmanned aircraft system airspace^40)

- a) if the provider requests it, or

- b) if the provider has not commenced the provision of these services within 6 months of the granting of authorization for their provision, or has not provided these services for a period longer than 12 consecutive months.

(2) The restriction of authorization for the provision of services by a provider of the common information service or a provider of services for controlled unmanned aircraft system operations pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the controlled unmanned aircraft system airspace^40) shall be carried out by restricting the scope of services provided or the manner of their provision, or by restricting a part of the Czech Republic's airspace in which these services may be provided. If the Office has decided to restrict the authorization, it shall issue a new certificate evidencing this authorization to the provider of the common information service or the provider of services for controlled unmanned aircraft system operations pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the controlled unmanned aircraft system airspace^40); the provider shall surrender the original certificate to the Office within 15 days from the date of issuance of the new certificate.

(3) If the Office has decided to withdraw the authorization for the provision of services by a provider of the common information service or a provider of services for controlled unmanned aircraft system operations pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the controlled unmanned aircraft system airspace^40), the provider shall surrender the certificate evidencing this authorization to the Office within 15 days from the date the decision becomes final.

Part 5

Application of the provisions of the Act and European Union regulations to unmanned aircraft systems

p54s.text:
(1) The following shall not apply to unmanned aircraft systems, their registration and airworthiness, persons performing activities related to ensuring their safe and smooth operation, the flight of unmanned aircraft in the airspace of the Czech Republic, the reporting of incidents other than serious incidents related to the operation of unmanned aircraft systems, and the flight of unmanned aircraft for recreational, individual, sports, or pilot training purposes:

- a) Parts Two and Three,

- b) Part Five, Chapters II and III, with the exception of § 50, 50a, and 53,

- c) § 55b para. 1, and

- d) Part Seven.

(2) The directly applicable European Union regulation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c) and directly applicable European Union regulations issued thereunder shall apply to the design, production, maintenance, operation, and control of unmanned aircraft systems in the open or specific category of operation for customs, police, rescue, or fire-fighting purposes.

p55.heading: Institute for Professional Investigation of Air Accidents
p55.text:
(1) The Air Accident Investigation Institute (hereinafter referred to as the „Institute“) is an administrative authority with its registered office in Prague, whose budget is part of the budgetary chapter of the Ministry of Transport.

(2) The Institute is headed by a director, who is appointed and dismissed by the Government on the proposal of the Minister of Transport. An irreproachable citizen of the Czech Republic may be appointed as the Director of the Institute; an irreproachable citizen is considered to be a citizen who has not been convicted of an intentionally committed criminal offence, unless they are deemed not to have been convicted. The Director of the Institute is considered to be a service authority and is authorised to issue orders to state employees for the performance of state service in accordance with the State Service Act. In addition to the Director of the Institute, the employees of the Institute also include inspectors.

(3) The Director of the Institute and the inspectors of the Institute may not, for the duration of their term of office, perform any activity related to the type approval of products, the airworthiness of aircraft parts and equipment and aeronautical ground equipment, the granting of licences for the operation of commercial air transport, air traffic control or airport operations, and may not serve as advisors or members of the statutory bodies of entities whose interests could be in conflict with the activities of the Institute.

p55a.heading: Activities of the Institute
p55a.text:
(1) The Institute

- a) investigates the causes of air accidents and incidents, and

- b) fulfils the tasks of the competent authority according to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the reporting of occurrences in civil aviation^37), including the tasks of the contact point and the publication of the safety report.

(2) The inspectors of the Institute are obliged to

- a) maintain confidentiality regarding facts of which they became aware in connection with the performance of their duties, and

- b) identify themselves with an Institute inspector's ID card when performing their duties.

(3) In extraordinary or factually complex cases, the Institute's inspectors may use the assistance of an expert consultant who has knowledge of a special field (hereinafter referred to as „consultant“). The Institute's inspector shall make a record in the file regarding the engagement of a consultant. With the consent of the Institute's inspector, the consultant may, to the extent necessary for the performance of their duties, inspect the file on an air accident or incident and be present during the exercise of the Institute's inspectors' powers. The consultant may not interfere with the exercise of the Institute's inspectors' powers. The consultant is obliged to maintain confidentiality regarding all facts of which they became aware in connection with the performance of their duties. The Institute may release them from this obligation. Special regulations on experts and interpreters shall apply mutatis mutandis to the exclusion of a consultant.

(4) When investigating the causes of air accidents and remedying their consequences, the Institute cooperates with the standing bodies for the coordination of the components of the integrated rescue system. When carrying out rescue and liquidation work at the site of an air accident, the Institute's inspectors cooperate with the incident commander, especially for the purpose of preserving evidence necessary for the expert determination of the cause of the air accident.

(5) When investigating the causes of air accidents and incidents involving civil and military aviation, the Institute cooperates with the Ministry of Defence to the necessary extent. When investigating the causes of air accidents and incidents involving the operation of civil and police aircraft, the Institute cooperates with the Ministry of the Interior to the necessary extent.

(6) The specimen of the Institute inspector's ID card shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p55b.heading: Investigation of causes of incidents other than serious incidents
p55b.text:
(1) Any incident other than a serious incident that occurred in the territory of the Czech Republic must be reported without undue delay to the Institute by the operator or pilot of the aircraft, or the air navigation service provider.

(2) When investigating the causes of incidents other than serious incidents, the Institute's inspectors are authorised to secure parts of the aircraft for the purpose of their further examination and to require

- a) free access to the site of an incident other than a serious incident, to the aircraft, its parts and contents,

- b) immediate preparation of a list of evidence,

- c) free access to flight recorders and all other records and to use them,

- d) free access to the results of interviews with aviation personnel and pilots of sport flying equipment,

- e) free access to the results of tests used to detect the presence of alcohol or other addictive substances in aviation personnel and pilots of sport flying equipment,

- f) explanations from persons, and

- g) free access to all other information related to an incident other than a serious incident.

(3) For every incident other than a serious incident whose causes were investigated by the Institute, the Institute's inspector shall prepare a report without undue delay, which, if appropriate, contains safety recommendations. The Institute shall publish the report in a manner enabling remote access within 12 months from the date on which the incident other than a serious incident occurred; at the same time, it shall submit it to the Ministry of Transport, the Office, aircraft operators or air navigation service providers involved in the incident other than a serious incident, and other persons who could benefit from its conclusions with regard to the operational safety of civil aviation.

p55c.heading: Authorisation
p55c.text:
(1) The Institute may, upon request, entrust a legal entity with the investigation of the causes of aircraft accidents in which no fatalities occurred, and of incidents, provided that the legal entity is capable of ensuring the proper performance of these activities through professionally qualified natural persons and with the assistance of appropriate technical equipment. In the performance of the activities with which it has been entrusted, the legal entity is subordinate to the Institute.

(2) A legal entity entrusted pursuant to paragraph 1 shall proceed with the investigation of the causes of aircraft accidents and serious incidents in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the investigation of accidents and incidents in civil aviation^19). The provisions of § 55a para. 2 letter a), § 55a para. 3 and 4, and § 55b para. 2 and 3 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the performance of activities by a legal entity entrusted pursuant to paragraph 1. A natural person, through whom a legal entity entrusted pursuant to paragraph 1 ensures the investigation of the causes of aircraft accidents and incidents, is obliged to identify themselves with an identification card issued by the entrusted legal entity when performing this activity.

(3) A legal entity entrusted pursuant to paragraph 1 is obliged to immediately notify the Institute of

- a) all results of the activities with which it has been entrusted, and

- b) all changes concerning the facts decisive for the granting of the entrustment.

(4) The Institute shall withdraw the granted entrustment if its holder

- a) ceases to meet the conditions for granting the entrustment,

- b) seriously violates the obligations stipulated by this Act, or

- c) has requested the withdrawal of the entrustment.

(5) The specimen of the identification card for a natural person pursuant to paragraph 2 shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p56.text:
(1) Commercial air transport may be operated only on the basis of a license issued by the Office.

(2) Commercial air transport is divided into scheduled and non-scheduled according to the method of operation, and into domestic and international according to its scope.

p58.text:
(1) The Office shall issue, in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation^20), a license for the operation of commercial air transport based on an application under the following conditions:

- a) the natural person and their responsible representative, if appointed, have reached the age of 18, are fully capable of legal acts, are of good character, and at least one of them is professionally qualified,

- b) all natural persons who are the statutory body of the legal entity or members of the statutory body have reached the age of 18, are capable of legal acts, are of good character, and at least one member of the statutory body or the responsible representative of the legal entity meets the condition of professional qualification,

- c) the applicant demonstrates that they meet the conditions stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation^20).

(2) The particulars of the application for the issuance of a license and the documents that must be attached to the application shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p59.heading: Good Character
p59.text:
(1) A person shall not be considered of good character if they have been finally convicted of a criminal offense whose factual basis is related to commercial air transport, or if they have been finally convicted of another criminal offense committed intentionally, if, given the nature of commercial air transport and the person of the applicant for the license, there is a concern that they will commit the same or a similar act during the operation of commercial air transport, unless they are considered as not having been convicted.

(2) For the purpose of ascertaining whether a person pursuant to § 58 para. 1 letter a) and b) meets the condition of good character, the Office shall request, in accordance with a special legal regulation^1m), an extract from the Criminal Register. The request for the issuance of an extract from the Criminal Register and the extract from the Criminal Register shall be submitted electronically, in a manner allowing remote access.

p60.heading: Professional Qualification
p60.text: Professional qualification is demonstrated by a document of completed secondary or university education in economics, transport, technical, or legal fields and at least five years of professional experience in civil aviation.

p62.text:
The Office shall state in the decision on the issuance of the license

- a) the designation of the domestic air carrier, including the identification number of the person provided by the administrator of the basic register of legal entities, entrepreneurial natural persons, and public authorities^9a),

- b) the type of commercial air transport,

- c) the frequency and scope of commercial air transport within a time period,

- d) the number and types of aircraft operated,

- e) the date of commencement of commercial air transport operations,

- f) the period for which the license is granted,

- g) the conditions for operating commercial air transport.

p63.heading: Amendment of License
p63.text:
The Office may decide on an amendment to the license

- a) if there has been a change in the facts on the basis of which the decision on the issuance of the license was made,

- b) if required by public interest in satisfying transport needs or the safety of commercial air transport,

- c) based on a reasoned request from the license holder.

p64.heading: Restriction of Commercial Air Transport
p64.text:
The Office may decide to restrict commercial air transport for the absolutely necessary period for reasons of

- a) flight safety,

- b) public interest in other extraordinary aviation activities,

- c) if it is necessary to comply with obligations arising from an international treaty that is part of the legal order.

p65.heading: Withdrawal of License
p65.text:
The Office shall withdraw the license if the license holder

- a) seriously violates the obligations stipulated by this Act or the directly applicable European Union regulation^5j),

- b) fails to comply with the conditions stipulated in the license, or

- c) has requested the withdrawal of the license.

p66.text:
The license shall cease to be valid

- a) on the day of the dissolution of the legal entity that is the license holder,

- b) by a decision of the Office on the withdrawal of the license.

p67.heading: Air Operator Certificate
p67.text:
(1) The Air Operator Certificate is issued by the Office based on the application of a domestic air carrier.

(2) The Air Operator Certificate certifies that the domestic air carrier

- a) meets the conditions stipulated by this Act for aviation personnel, air traffic safety, and the operation of aircraft necessary for commercial air transport,

- b) has an internal organizational structure and management system for ensuring commercial air transport.

(3) The Office shall withdraw the Air Operator Certificate if the domestic air carrier ceases to meet the conditions under paragraph 2.

(4) The particulars of the application shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p68.heading: Obligations of a domestic air carrier
p68.text:
(1) A domestic air carrier shall be obliged to

- a) operate commercial air transport under the conditions stipulated in the licence,

- b) notify the Authority of all changes concerning the facts on the basis of which the decision on granting the licence was made, and the identification number of the legal entity, if it was not included in the application for granting the licence, or changes concerning the fulfilment of the conditions stipulated in the licence within 10 days of the occurrence of such changes,

- c) notify the Authority at least 20 days in advance of the commencement of regular commercial air transport for which it holds a valid licence,

- d) notify the Authority at least 3 days in advance of the commencement of irregular commercial air transport for which it holds a valid licence,

- e) notify the Authority within 24 hours of the suspension or termination of commercial air transport for which it holds a valid licence,

- f) in the event of licence revocation, ensure at its own expense the transport of persons, baggage, animals, goods and mail to which it has committed,

- g) publish the conditions of carriage by the date of commencement of commercial air transport.

(2) A domestic air carrier shall further be obliged to draw up a plan for assisting victims of air accidents and their relatives^19) and to proceed in accordance with this plan. The plan for assisting victims of air accidents and their relatives shall include

- a) work procedures for confirming a passenger's involvement in an air accident and their personnel and material provision, and

- b) work procedures for providing care to victims of air accidents and their relatives, including informational, psychological, legal and financial assistance, and their personnel and material provision.

p69.heading: Transmission of passenger data
p69.text:
(1) For the purpose of improving border controls and combating illegal immigration, an air carrier shall be obliged to transmit, upon request, electronically, and in the event of a malfunction by other suitable means, to the unit of the Police of the Czech Republic which is competent, according to a special legal regulation^5j), to carry out border control at the airport, data on passengers who cross the external border^1n), immediately after receiving the request, but not earlier than after the completion of boarding of all passengers onto the aircraft.

(2) The data transmitted according to paragraph 1 shall be

- a) given name(s) and surname,

- b) day, month and year of birth,

- c) nationality,

- d) number and type of travel document presented by the passenger,

- e) place of entry into the territory of the Czech Republic,

- f) flight number,

- g) date and time of departure and arrival,

- h) initial place of embarkation for transport, and

- i) total number of passengers transported by the respective flight.

(3) An air carrier shall be obliged to inform passengers, in accordance with a special legal regulation^5k), about the collection and processing of data according to paragraph 1.

(4) An air carrier shall dispose of personal data of passengers collected for the purpose of fulfilling the obligations under paragraph 1 within 24 hours after the landing of the aircraft by which these passengers were transported to the territory of the Czech Republic.

(5) The rights and obligations of the air carrier and passengers concerning the protection of personal data stipulated by a special legal regulation are not affected by the provisions of paragraphs 1 to 4.

(6) The Police of the Czech Republic shall dispose of personal data transmitted according to paragraph 1 within 24 hours of their receipt, unless these data are used for fulfilling a task assigned to it.

(7) The rights and obligations of the Police of the Czech Republic concerning the protection of personal data stipulated by a special legal regulation^5l) are not affected by the provisions of paragraphs 1 and 6.

p69a.heading: Transmission of passenger name record data
p69a.text:
(1) An air carrier shall provide the Police of the Czech Republic with passenger name record data, which it collects in the course of its usual activities and which relate to a flight operated by it with take-off, intermediate landing or landing in the territory of the Czech Republic. The air carrier shall provide these data electronically or, if technically not possible, by other suitable means, within the period of

- a) 24 to 48 hours before the planned time of departure, and

- b) immediately after passengers have boarded the aircraft ready for departure.

(2) Passenger name record data relating to a flight operated by an air carrier from the territory of an European Union Member State with a landing in the territory of another European Union Member State without an intermediate landing outside the European Union Member States shall be provided by the air carrier in accordance with paragraph 1 only if it results from information on the provision of such data published in the Official Journal of the European Union.

(3) Passenger name record data provided in accordance with paragraph 1 letter b) may also be provided by the air carrier in the form of an update of the data provided in accordance with paragraph 1 letter a).

(4) Passenger name record data that the air carrier provides in accordance with paragraphs 1 and 2 shall also be provided by the air carrier to the Police of the Czech Republic upon its request, immediately after receipt of the request.

(5) Passenger name record data means

- a) the reservation code for an individual passenger name record,

- b) the date of reservation and the date of ticket issuance,

- c) the date of the planned journey,

- d) the given name(s) and surname of the passenger,

- e) the address and contact information of the passenger,

- f) all data on the payment made for the services provided,

- g) all data on the flight itinerary for an individual passenger name record,

- h) all data on the passenger's loyalty program,

- i) the identification code of the travel agency or tour operator,

- j) all data on the passenger's check-in status, confirmation of their departure, and cases where the passenger did not show up for departure or where the passenger arrived at the airport without a reservation,

- k) all data on the passenger's removal from the reservation,

- l) all data on unaccompanied minors,

- m) all data contained in the „general remarks“ field in the air carrier's reservation system,

- n) the ticket number issued, the date and place of its issuance, the identification code of the issuing travel agency or tour operator, and all data from the air carrier's automated pricing and reservation conditions system,

- o) the seat number and all other seat information,

- p) the air carrier code used in code-sharing arrangements,

- q) all data on baggage,

- r) the number and given names and surnames of passengers within an individual passenger name record,

- s) passenger data specified in § 69 paragraph 2, and

- t) any changes to the data specified in letters a) to s).

p70.heading: International commercial air transport
p70.text:
(1) An air carrier of the European Union^20) shall inform the Ministry of Transport without undue delay of its intention to operate regular international commercial air transport from the Czech Republic to a state that is not a Member State of the European Union.

(2) An air carrier of the European Union^20) intending to operate regular international commercial air transport on the basis of an international agreement which is part of the legal order and which contains restrictions on the access of air carriers to the transport market between the Czech Republic and the other contracting party must be granted the right to operate regular international commercial air transport on a designated route within the given scope (hereinafter referred to as „traffic right“) for such commercial air transport.

p70a.heading: Granting of a traffic right
p70a.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport shall decide on the granting of a traffic right to an air carrier of the European Union^20) based on its application.

(2) An application for the granting of a traffic right shall, in addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, contain

- a) the name and registered office address of the authority that issued the license for commercial air transport operations and the air operator certificate to the air carrier of the European Union^20) and that exercises control over it,

- b) the address of the air carrier's establishment in the territory of the Czech Republic, if it is an air carrier of another Member State,

- c) a definition of the commercial air transport services offered, including the period and type of operation,

- d) a proposed flight schedule, including the organizational and geographical definition of the planned operation and the anticipated start date of operation,

- e) a definition of the method of technical support for operations, including the type and internal arrangement of the aircraft used and the legal relationship thereto,

- f) a definition of the planned pricing policy, including offered fares and final prices for transport,

- g) a definition of the anticipated development of traffic volume and anticipated aircraft capacity utilization over 3 years from the anticipated start date of operation,

- h) a definition of the method of offering commercial air transport services, including the distribution of transport documents,

- i) a definition of the method and extent of integration of the route corresponding to the requested traffic right into the network of routes of the air carrier of the European Union^20) and its connections to the networks of other air carriers, and

- j) a definition of the intended cooperation with other air carriers, including the method and extent of such cooperation, on the route corresponding to the requested traffic right.

p70b.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport shall publish information on the receipt of the first application for the grant of a traffic right on its official board within 7 days of its delivery. Other air carriers of the European Union^20) may submit an application for the grant of a traffic right in the same matter within 21 days of the publication of information on the first application. Applications submitted later shall not be considered. The Ministry of Transport shall publish information on the receipt of applications from other air carriers of the European Union^20) on its official board within 7 days of their delivery.

(2) The Ministry of Transport shall decide on applications submitted pursuant to paragraph 1 in joint proceedings.

(3) In proceedings for the grant of a traffic right, the file may be inspected only after the expiry of the deadline for submitting applications pursuant to paragraph 1.

p70c.text:
(1) When deciding on the grant of a traffic right, the Ministry of Transport shall take into account:

- a) the type, level, and scope of commercial air transport services offered, the extent to which the needs of individual categories of users are considered, and the ratio of price to the level of commercial air transport services,

- b) the frequency of commercial air transport services offered, the offered capacity, and the distribution of flights throughout the calendar year,

- c) the anticipated start date of operation and the period of operation,

- d) the availability of the offered commercial air transport services to users,

- e) the pricing policy of the air carrier of the European Union^20), including offered tariffs and final prices for transport,

- f) the provision of transport services, including connections and integration into the existing route network,

- g) the technical security of operation, including the use of own aircraft, leased aircraft without crew, and leased aircraft with crew,

- h) the method and extent of cooperation with other air carriers,

- i) the conditions of operation on the designated route and the adequacy of the offer, and

- j) the previous use of allocated traffic rights by the air carrier of the European Union^20).

(2) The Ministry of Transport may grant a traffic right to multiple applicants if an international treaty, which is part of the legal order, allows it.

(3) The Ministry of Transport shall decide on an application for the grant of a traffic right within 60 days of the delivery of the first application. The decision on the grant of a traffic right shall be issued for an indefinite period.

(4) The Ministry of Transport may stipulate conditions for its exercise in the decision on the grant of a traffic right. The Ministry of Transport may decide on a change to the conditions for the exercise of a traffic right based on an application from an air carrier of the European Union^20) to whom the traffic right was granted, or ex officio, provided that there has been a change in the facts decisive for the grant of the traffic right.

(5) The Ministry of Transport shall publish the decision on the grant of a traffic right on its official board.

(6) An air carrier of the European Union^20) to whom a traffic right has been granted is obliged to notify the Ministry of Transport of:

- a) a change in the facts stated in the application, if it may have a substantial impact on the exercise of the granted traffic right, no later than 14 days from the date the change occurred, and

- b) the fact that the competent authority has not issued or has revoked its authorization to provide commercial air transport services on the designated route, without undue delay.

(7) An air carrier of the European Union^20) may not transfer the granted traffic right to another person.

p70d.text:
(1) An air carrier of the European Union^20) may apply to the Ministry of Transport for the grant of a traffic right that was granted to another air carrier, but not earlier than 5 years from the date the decision on the grant of the traffic right became final. In proceedings on an application pursuant to the first sentence, the provisions of § 70a para. 2, § 70b, and § 70c para. 1 to 5 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(2) Upon receipt of an application pursuant to paragraph 1, the Ministry of Transport shall assess the previous manner of exercising the traffic right by the air carrier of the European Union^20) to whom it was granted, and by its decision shall:

- a) leave the traffic right with this air carrier and reject the application,

- b) withdraw the traffic right from this air carrier and grant it to the applicant, or

- c) leave the traffic right with this air carrier and simultaneously grant it to the applicant.

p70e.heading: Withdrawal of a traffic right
p70e.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport shall withdraw a granted traffic right if the air carrier of the European Union^20):

- a) does not commence operation on the designated route within 6 months from the date the decision on the grant of the traffic right became final,

- b) does not utilize the granted traffic right for a period longer than 6 consecutive months, unless this is due to extraordinary circumstances beyond its control,

- c) violates the conditions stipulated in the decision on the grant of the traffic right,

- d) notifies that it no longer intends to utilize the granted traffic right, or

- e) ceases to operate commercial air transport.

(2) The Ministry of Transport shall also withdraw a granted traffic right if:

- a) there is a substantial change in the facts stated in the application of the air carrier of the European Union^20) to whom the traffic right was granted, and this change has an undesirable effect on the exercise of the granted traffic right, or

- b) the competent authority does not issue or revokes the authorization for the air carrier of the European Union^20) to provide commercial air transport services on the designated route.

p70f.heading: Commercial air transport by an air carrier of another Member State
p70f.text:
(1) An air carrier of another Member State shall notify the Ministry of Transport of the commencement of scheduled commercial air transport to the Czech Republic, from the Czech Republic, or within the territory of the Czech Republic. The notification shall be submitted at least 10 days before the planned date of commencement of scheduled commercial air transport and, in addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, shall include the name and address of the registered office of the authority that issued the license for operating commercial air transport and the air operator certificate to the air carrier of another Member State. If an air carrier of another Member State intends to operate scheduled commercial air transport from the Czech Republic to a state that is not a Member State of the European Union, it shall also state the address of its establishment in the Czech Republic in the notification.

(2) An air carrier of another Member State shall attach to the notification a flight schedule containing:

- a) the code designation of the air carrier of another Member State assigned by an international organization under an international treaty that is part of the legal order,

- b) the flight number, day, estimated times, and routing,

- c) the type and registration mark of the aircraft,

- d) the offered capacity of the aircraft,

- e) the organizational and geographical scope of the planned operation, and

- f) the period of validity of the flight schedule.

(3) If an air carrier of another Member State intends to operate scheduled commercial air transport for which traffic rights are granted, from the Czech Republic to a state that is not a Member State of the European Union, it shall not submit a notification.

(4) Notwithstanding the obligation to submit a notification, an air carrier of another Member State shall always submit the flight schedule to the Ministry of Transport at least 10 days before the start of each operating period.

(5) An air carrier of another Member State shall notify the Ministry of Transport of the performance of an irregular flight or a series of irregular flights to the Czech Republic, from the Czech Republic, or within the territory of the Czech Republic with an aircraft with a total number of passenger seats greater than 9 or with a maximum take-off weight greater than 5.7 tons. The notification shall be submitted at least 3 days before the planned date of the flight, or in the case of a series of irregular flights, at least 7 days before the planned date of the first flight.

(6) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the notification shall contain:

- a) the code designation of the air carrier of another Member State assigned by an international organization under an international treaty that is part of the legal order,

- b) the flight number, day, estimated times, routing, and purpose of the flight,

- c) the type and registration mark of the aircraft, and

- d) the name and address of the registered office of the authority that issued the license for operating commercial air transport and the air operator certificate to the air carrier of another Member State.

p71.heading: Scheduled commercial air transport by an air carrier from a third country
p71.text:
(1) An air carrier from a third country may operate scheduled commercial air transport to the Czech Republic, from the Czech Republic, within the territory of the Czech Republic, or across the territory of the Czech Republic on the basis of a permit issued by the Ministry of Transport. The Ministry of Transport shall issue a permit upon the request of an air carrier from a third country if it is in accordance with an international air transport agreement that is part of the legal order, or if it does not pose an economic or safety risk or an environmental risk.

(2) The request shall be submitted at least 30 days before the planned date of commencement of scheduled commercial air transport operations. The air carrier from a third country shall attach to the request proof of liability insurance for damage caused by aircraft operations, and furthermore, the following documents issued by the competent authority:

- a) a license for operating commercial air transport, if issued,

- b) an air operator certificate,

- c) a certificate of airworthiness,

- d) a certificate of noise suitability.

(3) The air carrier from a third country shall further attach to the request a flight schedule containing:

- a) the code designation of the air carrier from a third country assigned by an international organization under an international treaty that is part of the legal order,

- b) the flight number, day, estimated times, and routing,

- c) the type and registration mark of the aircraft,

- d) the offered capacity of the aircraft,

- e) the organizational and geographical scope of the planned operation, and

- f) the period of validity of the flight schedule.

(4) If an air carrier from a third country requests the issuance of a permit to operate scheduled commercial air transport across the territory of the Czech Republic, the documents specified in paragraph 2 letters c) and d) and the data specified in paragraph 3 letters d) and e) are not required.

(5) The permit is issued for an indefinite period. If the Ministry of Transport grants the request for the issuance of a permit in full, no appeal may be filed against such a decision.

(6) The permit holder shall immediately inform the Ministry of Transport of any change in the facts stated in the request for its issuance and in the documents attached thereto.

p71a.text:
The Ministry of Transport shall revoke a permit issued pursuant to § 71 paragraph 1 if the air carrier from a third country:

- a) does not use the issued permit for a period longer than 12 consecutive months, unless this is due to extraordinary circumstances beyond its control,

- b) notifies that it no longer intends to use the issued permit,

- c) seriously violates the provisions of this Act, a directly applicable European Union regulation, or an international treaty that is part of the legal order,

- d) ceases to operate commercial air transport, or

- e) is subject to an operating ban under a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the list of air carriers subject to an operating ban in the European Union^21) or a decision of an international organization issued on the basis of an international treaty that is part of the legal order.

p71b.heading: Notification obligation of an air carrier from a third country
p71b.text:
(1) A third-country air carrier shall notify the Ministry of Transport of the commencement of direct or indirect sale of scheduled commercial air transport services in the territory of the Czech Republic. The notification shall be submitted at least 20 days before the planned date of commencement of sale.

(2) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the notification shall contain:

- a) the business name or designation and the address of the registered office of the organizational unit of the third-country air carrier established in the territory of the Czech Republic,

- b) the business name or designation and the address of the registered office of the person mediating the sale of commercial air transport services for the third-country air carrier in the territory of the Czech Republic, if no organizational unit is established, and

- c) the type of commercial air transport services offered in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(3) A third-country air carrier shall inform the Ministry of Transport of any change in the facts stated in the notification within 15 days from the date on which such change occurred.

p71c.heading: Non-scheduled commercial air transport by a third-country air carrier
p71c.text:
(1) A third-country air carrier may operate non-scheduled commercial air transport to the Czech Republic, from the Czech Republic, within the territory of the Czech Republic, or across the territory of the Czech Republic on the basis of a permit issued by the Ministry of Transport. The Ministry of Transport shall issue a permit upon the request of a third-country air carrier if it is in accordance with an international air transport agreement which is part of the legal order, or if it does not pose an economic or safety risk or an environmental risk.

(2) The request shall be submitted at least

- a) 3 working days before the planned date of the flight, if it is a request for a permit for a single flight,

- b) 7 working days before the planned date of the flight, if it concerns the transport of dangerous goods^22), or

- c) 15 working days before the planned date of the first flight, if it concerns a series of non-scheduled flights.

(3) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the request shall contain:

- a) the code designation of the third-country air carrier assigned by an international organization under an international agreement which is part of the legal order,

- b) the flight number, date, estimated times, routing, and purpose,

- c) the type and registration mark of the aircraft,

- d) the organizational and geographical scope of the planned operation,

- e) the number of passengers or the type and quantity of cargo,

- f) the name, classification, quantity, and method of packaging of dangerous goods^22), if transported,

- g) the numerical code assigned to dangerous goods^22) by the United Nations, if transported,

- h) the first name, surname, and address of permanent residence or the business name or designation and the address of the registered office of the transport ordering party,

- i) the first name, surname, and address of permanent residence or the business name or designation and the address of the registered office of the sender and recipient of the goods, if transported, and

- j) the business name or designation of the chosen handling services provider in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(4) The third-country air carrier shall attach to the request proof of liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of the aircraft, and also the following documents issued by the competent authority:

- a) a license for operating commercial air transport, if issued,

- b) a license or other similar document for the transport of dangerous goods^22), if transported,

- c) an air operator certificate,

- d) a certificate of airworthiness,

- e) a noise certificate.

(5) If a third-country air carrier requests the issuance of a permit for operating non-scheduled commercial air transport across the territory of the Czech Republic, the data specified in paragraph 3 letters d), e), h) to j) and the documents specified in paragraph 4 letters d) and e) are not required.

(6) If the Ministry of Transport grants the request for the issuance of a permit in full, no appeal may be filed against such a decision.

(7) The permit holder shall immediately inform the Ministry of Transport of any change in the facts stated in the request for its issuance and in the documents attached thereto.

p71d.text: The Ministry of Transport shall revoke a permit issued under § 71c paragraph 1 if any of the reasons specified in § 71a letters b) to e) occur.

p72.heading: Special provisions on proceedings in matters of commercial air transport
p72.text:
(1) In proceedings under § 70a to 70e, 71, 71a, 71c and 71d, a participant may act and submit documents also in English. Documents originally drawn up in a language other than Czech or English shall be submitted by the participant in officially verified translation into Czech or English, unless the Ministry of Transport informs the participant that an officially unverified translation is sufficient. The Ministry of Transport may make such a statement on its official board for all proceedings under the first sentence. The provisions of this paragraph shall apply mutatis mutandis to submissions made under § 70f and 71b.

(2) In proceedings under § 71, 71a, 71c and 71d, the Ministry of Transport shall simultaneously perform acts in Czech and English. This shall not apply if the participant does not agree, or if the course of the proceedings would be jeopardized, or if such a procedure would involve disproportionate costs for the Ministry of Transport; in such a case, acts shall be performed only in Czech, and the Ministry of Transport shall issue a resolution to that effect, which shall be notified to the participant. In case of a discrepancy between the Czech and English versions of an act, the Czech version shall be decisive.

(3) In proceedings under § 71, 71a, 71c and 71d, submissions may also be made electronically without the use of a signature or by telegraph or telefax. In such a case, the submission shall not be confirmed or supplemented. If the submission was made by a person other than the one stated therein, the submission shall not have the effects associated with it by law; the Ministry of Transport shall issue a resolution to that effect, which shall be noted in the file. The provisions of this paragraph shall apply mutatis mutandis to submissions made under § 70f and 71b.

(4) In proceedings under § 71, 71a, 71c and 71d, the Ministry of Transport shall deliver documents via a public data network to the electronic address provided by the participant, or via telefax; in these cases, the document shall be deemed delivered on the day of dispatch, unless the message is returned as undeliverable. Confirmation of receipt of the delivered document by the addressee via a signed data message is not required.

p73.text: „Aerial work“ means aerial activities where an air operator uses an aircraft for work for remuneration. „Aerial work“ also means sightseeing flights, the use of an aircraft by an air operator for training in flight schools, and the activity of flight schools.

p74.text:
(1) Aerial work may be operated by a natural person with permanent residence and a legal person with registered office in the Czech Republic only on the basis of a permit. The Office may issue a permit on the basis of an application.

(2) A permit may be issued if the applicant meets the conditions stipulated by this Act for applicants for a license to operate commercial air transport pursuant to § 58 (1), for air personnel, and for the operation of aircraft used for aerial work.

(3) An implementing regulation shall stipulate the individual types of aerial work, the technical and operational conditions for their operation, the particulars of the application for a permit, and the particulars of the decision on issuing a permit.

p74a.text:
(1) A national of another Member State of the European Union or a legal person with its registered office, central administration, or principal place of business in another Member State of the European Union, who are authorized to operate aerial work in that Member State, may temporarily operate such aerial work also in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(2) The provisions of § 74 shall not apply to the operation of aerial work pursuant to paragraph 1.

(3) The person pursuant to paragraph 1 shall notify the Office of the commencement of aerial work operations in the territory of the Czech Republic no later than 3 days in advance.

(4) A person operating aerial work in the territory of the Czech Republic pursuant to paragraph 1 is obliged to prove, during an inspection, the authorization to operate aerial work in another Member State of the European Union.

(5) The provisions of the preceding paragraphs shall also apply to a national of another state or a legal person with its registered office, central administration, or principal place of business in another state, if this results from an international treaty that is part of the legal order.

p75.heading: Aerial activities for state needs
p75.text:
(1) „Aerial activity for state needs“ means flights carried out by an air operator for the purpose of

- a) transport of constitutional officials,

- b) performance of state administration.

(2) Flights for the purpose of transporting constitutional officials may be carried out with the consent of the state represented by the government. Flights for the purpose of performing state administration may be carried out with the consent of the Ministry of Transport.

p76.heading: Aerial activities for own needs
p76.text:
(1) „Aerial activity for own needs“ means flights by which a legal or natural person ensures business or other activity for which it is authorized according to special regulations.

(2) Aerial activity for own needs may be operated by a natural person with permanent residence or a legal person with registered office in the Czech Republic on the basis of a permit issued by the Office. The Office may issue a permit on the basis of an application if the applicant meets the conditions stipulated by this Act for air personnel and for the operation of aircraft necessary for aerial activity for own needs.

(3) An implementing regulation shall stipulate the particulars of the application for a permit to operate aerial activity for own needs.

p77.heading: Recreational and sport flying
p77.text: „Recreational and sport flying“ means the use of an aircraft for one's own needs or the needs of other persons for the purpose of recreation, personal transport, or sport, which is not carried out for profit. Recreational and sport flying may only be operated with aircraft with a maximum take-off mass of less than 5.7 t and with a total number of passenger seats less than 9.

p78.heading: Public air display
p78.text: A public air display may only be operated with the consent of the Office; this does not apply to the performance of a public air display by unmanned systems in the open category of operation or by an authorized association. The Office shall grant consent if flight safety in the airspace is not endangered.

p79.text:
The operator of aerial activities and the user of an aircraft is obliged to

- a) operate aerial activity with an airworthy aircraft,

- b) ensure aerial activity with professionally and medically fit air personnel,

- c) use airports or designated areas for aircraft take-offs and landings and related aircraft movements,

- d) perform flights according to flight rules,

- e) comply with the instructions of persons providing air traffic services.

p80.text:
(1) The operator of an aerial activity may exclude from transport persons, animals, and cargo whose transport would endanger the safety of air traffic or whose transport would lead to a violation of regulations valid in the state of departure, state of arrival, or state of overflight.

(2) Objects and substances whose nature may endanger the safety of air traffic may only be transported if the conditions stipulated by implementing and special regulations are observed.

p80a.text:
(1) A directly applicable European Union regulation governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the area of aviation fuels^46) shall apply to an air carrier to whom it does not apply, if that air carrier notifies the Authority.

(2) A directly applicable European Union regulation governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the area of aviation fuels^46) shall also apply to an air carrier to whom it applies, when operating aviation activities other than commercial air transport, if that air carrier notifies the Authority.

(3) A directly applicable European Union regulation governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the area of aviation fuels^46) shall apply to an operator of aviation activities other than commercial air transport, when operating such activities, if that operator notifies the Authority.

(4) The effects of a notification pursuant to paragraph 1, 2 or 3, which has been communicated by the Authority to the European Commission and the Agency

- a) by 30 June, shall take effect from the first day of the calendar year following such notification, or

- b) after 30 June, shall take effect from the first day of the second calendar year following such notification.

(5) The Authority shall, immediately after communicating a notification pursuant to paragraph 1, 2 or 3 to the European Commission and the Agency, inform the notifier of when such communication was made.

p81.heading: Administration of Sport Flying Devices
p81.text:
(1) A sport flying device is a maximum two-seat aircraft or a sport parachute, intended for flying for one's own use or the use of other persons for recreation, individual personal transport, sport or pilot training, which is not carried out for profit, with the exception of pilot training, flights of hang gliders and paragliders with a passenger, and sport parachute jumps with a passenger.

(2) Types of sport flying devices include, in particular,

- a) ultralight glider,

- b) ultralight aeroplane,

- c) powered hang glider,

- d) ultralight helicopter,

- e) ultralight powered gyrocopter,

- f) powered paraglider,

- g) hang glider,

- h) paraglider,

- i) sport parachute.

(3) A pilot of a sport flying device is a person who holds a valid pilot's licence and operates the sport flying device. For the purposes of this Act, a parachutist is also considered a pilot.

(4) A student pilot of a sport flying device is a person who is learning to operate it according to a training syllabus.

(5) A builder of a sport flying device is a natural person who builds a sport flying device for their own use.

(6) A manufacturer of a sport flying device is a natural or legal person who manufactures sport flying devices and related other products, aircraft parts and equipment for sale.

(7) An operations inspector or a technical inspector is a professionally qualified natural person who, within the scope of authority arising from this Act, supervises the fulfilment of obligations by builders, manufacturers, pilots and other persons involved in the operation of sport flying devices.

(8) The characteristics of individual types of sport flying devices shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p82.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport may, by its decision, entrust a legal entity (hereinafter referred to as the „authorised person“) with the performance of state administration in matters of sport flying devices, based on its written request. The Ministry of Transport shall specify the conditions and procedures for such performance and define its scope in the decision on authorisation to perform state administration.

(2) The Ministry of Transport shall issue the decision if the applicant

- a) has its registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic,

- b) has established an administrative and technical system for the administration of sport flying devices in accordance with this Act,

- c) has qualified persons and professional background for the performance of state administration of sport flying devices in accordance with this Act and for the creation of operational and technical regulations,

- d) has established and secured an information system for maintaining the register of pilots and sport flying devices,

- e) has established procedures for verifying and approving the airworthiness of sport flying devices,

- f) has established procedures for the instruction and verification of the competence of applicants for pilot licences and the granting of qualifications,

- g) has no financial or other interests that could affect the impartiality of the authorised person's activities,

- h) is capable of ensuring the protection of information and data in accordance with a special legal regulation^5k).

(3) The Ministry of Transport shall withdraw the authorisation

- a) if the authorised person fails to fulfil the obligations or conditions stipulated by this Act or the decision on authorisation,

- b) if the authorised person requests the withdrawal of the authorisation.

(4) The decision on authorisation and on the withdrawal of such authorisation shall be published by the Ministry of Transport by announcement in the Collection of Laws and International Treaties.

(5) If the performance of state administration in matters of sport flying devices is not ensured by an authorised person, it shall be ensured by the Authority.

(6) The particulars of the request for authorisation and the documents that must be attached to the request shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p84.text: Supervision over the design, construction, manufacture, repair, and maintenance of sport flying equipment and related products, aircraft parts and equipment, and supervision over the activities of operators, pilots, and other persons involved in the operation of sport flying equipment shall be carried out by an operations inspector or a technical inspector.

p84b.heading: Register of Sport Flying Equipment

p84c.heading: Pilots and Persons in Training
p84c.text:
(1) A sport flying device may be operated by

- a) a pilot who holds a valid pilot's license with the relevant qualification, or

- b) a student pilot under the conditions stipulated by the training syllabus.

(2) A condition for issuing a pilot's license is demonstrating the applicant's medical fitness, completing theoretical instruction, practical training, and passing the relevant examination in accordance with the training syllabus.

(3) A medical report^1n) on the medical fitness of applicants and pilots of sport flying devices is issued, based on a medical examination and other necessary specialized examinations, by a healthcare service provider through an assessing physician authorized by the Office. An assessing physician means

- a) a physician of a healthcare service provider who has been authorized pursuant to § 22 para. 3, for sport flying devices listed in § 81 para. 2 letter a) to e) and i) and two-seat sport flying devices listed in § 81 para. 2 letter f) to h),

- b) a physician who is a registered healthcare service provider in the field of general practical medicine for single-seat sport flying devices listed in § 81 para. 2 letter f) to h).

(4) The period of validity of the medical report on medical fitness is

- a) for pilots of sport flying devices pursuant to paragraph 3 letter a)

- 1. 60 months for persons under 40 years of age, unless the relevant physician stipulates a shorter period with regard to the assessed person's health status,

- 2. 24 months for persons from 40 to 75 years of age, unless the relevant physician stipulates a shorter period with regard to the assessed person's health status,

- 3. 12 months for persons from 75 years of age, unless the relevant physician stipulates a shorter period with regard to the assessed person's health status,

- b) for pilots of sport flying devices pursuant to paragraph 3 letter b)

- 1. unlimited for persons under 75 years of age, unless the physician stipulates a limitation on its validity period with regard to the assessed person's health status,

- 2. 12 months for persons from 75 years of age, unless the relevant physician stipulates a shorter period with regard to the assessed person's health status.

(5) "Qualification" means fulfilling the conditions stipulated for obtaining authorization to perform activities associated with the operation of sport flying devices. If practical experience is a condition for entering the relevant qualification into the pilot's license, the applicant is obliged to provide proof thereof.

(6) Immediately before a flight, during a flight, and immediately after the completion of a flight, the pilot or student pilot is obliged to carry the following valid documents:

- a) identity card,

- b) pilot's license or student's document,

- c) certificate of airworthiness of the sport flying device,

- d) document of liability insurance for damages caused by the operation of the sport flying device.

(7) The pilot or student pilot is obliged, during the operation of a sport flying device, to comply with the instructions of the operations inspector, technical inspector, or persons exercising state supervision under this Act.

(8) The particulars of the application for a pilot's license, the documents that must be attached to the application, the conditions for commencing practical training and solo flights, the method of determining qualification conditions for issuing a pilot's license, and the method of determining qualification conditions for persons conducting theoretical instruction and practical training shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p84d.heading: Areas for take-offs and landings of sport flying devices
p84d.text:
(1) For take-offs and landings during regular operation of sport flying devices, the following may be used:

- a) an aerodrome, if the aeronautical information publication stipulates the operation of a certain type of sport flying device at this aerodrome, or with the consent of the operator of this aerodrome,

- b) areas permanently used for take-offs and landings of aircraft and designated for this purpose in the land-use planning documentation or in the decision on permission for the intention, provided that the owner or operator of the area and the municipality in whose territory the area is located give their consent.

(2) For take-offs and landings during irregular operation of motorized sport flying devices and regular and irregular operation of non-motorized sport flying devices, any other area may be used, if the owner of the area has given consent to its use for this purpose, provided the following conditions are met:

- a) the area is located outside the residential area of the municipality at a distance of at least 100 m from residential buildings, and during operation, persons not involved in the operation will not be within a distance of less than 50 m from the sport flying devices,

- b) the area is located outside the territory of a national park, protected landscape area, national nature reserve, nature reserve, national natural monument, and natural monument, unless the relevant nature protection authority has given consent to the use of the territory for this purpose, and

- c) the area is located outside the territory of hygienic protection zones of water sources and protected areas of natural water accumulation, unless the relevant water management authority has given consent to the use of the territory of hygienic protection zones of water sources and protected areas of natural water accumulation for this purpose.

p85.heading: Competent Authority
p85.text: The tasks of the competent authority, including the information obligation of a Member State of the European Union to the European Commission, under the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) and regulations issued thereunder, shall be performed by the Office.

p85a.heading: National Programmes
p85a.text:
(1) The Office shall issue and update national programmes in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) or in accordance with a decision of an international organization issued on the basis of an international treaty that is part of the legal order. The national programmes shall contain a description of how the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference is ensured.

(2) The Office shall publish the national programmes in a manner enabling remote access, unless the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) stipulates otherwise.

p85b.heading: General obligation to protect civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference
p85b.text: Everyone entering areas serving for the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference pursuant to directly applicable European Union law governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) is obliged, upon entering and during their stay in such areas, to proceed with due caution so as not to expose civil aviation to the danger of an act of unlawful interference by their conduct, or to enable such conduct by others.

p85c.heading: Orders to ensure the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference
p85c.text:
(1) Natural and legal persons ensuring the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference are, for this purpose, authorized to issue orders to natural persons entering or staying in areas serving for the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference pursuant to directly applicable European Union law governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23); in doing so, they are obliged to ensure that the orders are proportionate to their purpose. An „order“ means an oral or written instruction calling for the person to whom the instruction is directed to behave in a manner stipulated by this Act or by directly applicable European Union law governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), or to refrain from an act prohibited by this Act or by directly applicable European Union law governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), or to immediately leave the area serving for the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference.

(2) Natural persons to whom an order is directed are obliged to comply with it.

p85d.heading: General provision on screening of persons
p85d.text: Everyone carrying out screening of natural persons pursuant to this Act and directly applicable European Union law governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) is obliged to carry it out using such methods and means that exclusively pursue the purpose of the screening and respect the dignity of every person subjected to the screening. For this purpose, a manual search of natural persons shall be carried out by a person of the same sex.

p85e.heading: Verification of reliability
p85e.text:
(1) Everyone for whom it is required by directly applicable European Union law governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) must be reliable. The condition for the reliability of a natural person is their integrity and trustworthiness.

(2) The Office shall verify the reliability of a natural person upon their request. This does not apply to a person who performs service or dependent work for the Ministry of Defence, the Police of the Czech Republic, the Fire and Rescue Service of the Czech Republic, the Customs Administration of the Czech Republic, the Prison Service of the Czech Republic, the General Inspectorate of Security Forces, the Security Information Service, and the Office for Foreign Relations and Information, or who holds a valid document on security clearance of a natural person or a valid certificate of a natural person pursuant to the Act governing the protection of classified information; such a person is considered reliable under this Act. If such a person ceases to meet the condition under the previous sentence, they shall immediately notify this fact to the person who allowed them unescorted access to a restricted security area.

(3) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the request shall contain

- a) the applicant's place of birth; if the applicant was born abroad, also the state in whose territory they were born,

- b) the applicant's citizenship,

- c) the name of the applicant's current employer, the designation of the position held, and a description of the work performed,

- d) a definition of the activity the applicant intends to perform and for the performance of which they must be reliable, and

- e) other data for the period of 5 years preceding the date of submission of the request or, if a reliability document was issued to the applicant by the Office within 5 years before the submission of the request, for the period from the date of issue of the previous reliability document to the date of submission of the request, which are:

- 1. the applicant's previous citizenship,

- 2. the names of the applicant's previous employers with an indication of the date of commencement and termination of the employment relationship, the designation of the position held, and a description of the work performed; this applies mutatis mutandis to service relationships,

- 3. the names of the persons with whom the applicant obtained education, the content and duration of this education with an indication of the date and manner of its completion,

- 4. the applicant's continuous stay in a foreign state longer than 6 months and its reason, and

- f) a solemn declaration of the truthfulness and completeness of the data provided in the request.

(4) The request shall be accompanied by a document similar to an extract from the Criminal Register of the state in which the applicant resided continuously for more than 6 months within the period of 5 years preceding the date of submission of the request, or an extract from the Criminal Register, the appendix of which contains this information. If the applicant is a foreign national, they shall also attach to the request a document similar to an extract from the Criminal Register of the state of which they are a national, or an extract from the Criminal Register, the appendix of which contains this information. If such a state does not issue a document similar to an extract from the Criminal Register, the applicant shall attach a solemn declaration of integrity made before a notary or an authority of the state of which they are a national, or before a notary or an authority of the state of last residence. These documents must not be older than 3 months.

p85f.heading: Integrity and trustworthiness
p85f.text:
(1) For the purposes of reliability verification, a natural person is considered to be „of good character“ if they have not been finally convicted of an intentional criminal offense, nor finally convicted of a criminal offense committed through negligence in connection with the performance of aviation personnel activities, airport operation, provision of air navigation services, or operation of aviation activities, unless they are regarded as not having been convicted.

(2) For the purposes of reliability verification, a natural person is considered to be „trustworthy“ if, in connection with the activity they intend to perform and for the performance of which they must be reliable, no specific risk can be identified. A specific risk is a reasonable suspicion that the natural person's past behavior, their professional or social contacts significantly indicate that they could expose civil aviation to the danger of an unlawful act.

p85g.heading: Verification of Identity and Good Character
p85g.text:
(1) The only participant in the reliability verification proceedings is the applicant. After the initiation of the proceedings, the Office shall verify the applicant's identity and good character.

(2) For the purpose of verifying good character, the Office shall request an extract from the Criminal Register. The request for the extract and the extract from the Criminal Register shall be submitted electronically, in a manner enabling remote access. For the purpose of verifying good character, the Office is also authorized to request a certified copy of a final decision from the court. If the decision does not contain facts relevant for verifying good character, the Office is authorized to inspect those parts of the criminal file that contain such facts.

(3) If the applicant has provided false information relevant for verifying identity or is not of good character, the Office shall issue a decision on their unreliability. Otherwise, the Office shall request the Police of the Czech Republic to assess their trustworthiness.

p85h.heading: Assessment of Trustworthiness
p85h.text: The Police of the Czech Republic shall assess the trustworthiness of the natural person no later than 15 days from the date of delivery of the Office's request and shall issue a binding opinion thereon. For the purpose of assessing trustworthiness, the Police of the Czech Republic may request necessary information from the relevant intelligence service of the Czech Republic. In particularly complex cases where trustworthiness cannot be assessed within the period specified in the first sentence, the period for issuing the binding opinion shall be 25 days from the date of delivery of the Office's request. If the binding opinion or its supporting documents contain classified information, these documents may not be inspected and shall be stored separately outside the file. In the event that the supporting documents for issuing the binding opinion contain classified information, the justification of the binding opinion shall refer to these supporting documents in such a way as not to frustrate the purpose of their classification; if this is not possible, only the facts resulting from these supporting documents shall be stated in the justification of the binding opinion in general terms.

p85i.text: If, according to the binding opinion of the Police of the Czech Republic, the applicant is not trustworthy, the Office shall issue a decision on their unreliability. This also applies if, during the proceedings, it becomes apparent that the applicant intentionally provided false information relevant for verifying reliability or intentionally concealed such information. Otherwise, the Office shall issue a reliability document instead of a written decision.

p85j.heading: Reliability Document
p85j.text:
(1) The reliability document is a public document. Its holder shall immediately report the theft or loss of the reliability document to the Office and to the person for whom they perform the activity for which they must be reliable, and to the airport operator, if the latter issued them an airport identification card. The reliability document is valid for a period of 1 year from its issuance.

(2) The reliability document contains

- a) first name(s) and surname,

- b) day, month, and year of birth,

- c) citizenship,

- d) date of issue and period of validity, and

- e) impression of the official seal and signature of the authorized official.

(3) The validity of the reliability document expires

- a) upon the expiration of its period of validity,

- b) on the date of entry into other legal effect of the Office's decision on the unreliability of its holder issued in the reliability review proceedings,

- c) upon reporting its theft or loss,

- d) upon such damage that the entries contained therein are illegible or its integrity is compromised,

- e) upon a change in any of the data contained therein,

- f) upon return of the document by its holder to the Office,

- g) on the date of delivery of a new document, or

- h) upon the death of the document holder, or their declaration of death.

(4) The holder of a reliability document shall surrender to the Office within 5 days a document whose validity has expired according to paragraph 3 letter a), b), d), e) and g), and shall immediately notify this fact to the person for whom they perform the activity for which they must be reliable, and to the airport operator, if the latter issued them an airport identification card. Everyone is obliged to immediately surrender a found reliability document to the Office or the Police of the Czech Republic.

(5) The holder of a reliability document whose validity has expired according to paragraph 3 letter c) to e) shall apply to the Office for a new document within 5 days from the date of expiration of validity. In such a case, the Office shall issue a new reliability document within 5 days of receipt of the application, which replaces the original document.

(6) The specimen of the reliability document shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p85k.heading: Reliability Review
p85k.text:
(1) The Office shall initiate ex officio proceedings for the review of reliability if, during the period of validity of the reliability certificate, it ascertains facts indicating that the holder of the certificate:

- a) is not irreproachable,

- b) is not trustworthy, or
