- c) intentionally provided false information material to the verification of reliability or intentionally concealed such information.

(2) The only participant in the reliability review proceedings shall be the person whose reliability is being reviewed. If the Office ascertains facts indicating that the holder of the reliability certificate is not trustworthy, it shall request the Police of the Czech Republic to assess their trustworthiness. The provisions of § 85f, § 85g para 2, and § 85h shall apply mutatis mutandis to the reliability review proceedings.

(3) If the Office ascertains that the holder of the reliability certificate is not irreproachable or trustworthy, it shall issue a decision on their unreliability. This shall also apply if the holder of the reliability certificate intentionally provided false information material to the verification of reliability or intentionally concealed such information. Otherwise, the Office shall discontinue the proceedings by resolution. An appeal against a decision on unreliability shall not have suspensive effect.

p85l.heading: Judicial Review
p85l.text:
(1) Evidence in judicial proceedings shall be taken in such a way as to observe the duty to maintain confidentiality regarding classified information contained in data from the records of the Police of the Czech Republic or intelligence services of the Czech Republic or in the results of investigations by the Police of the Czech Republic. Evidence of these circumstances may be taken by examination only if the person bound by the duty of confidentiality has been released from this duty by the competent authority; release from confidentiality is not possible only in cases where the activities of the Police of the Czech Republic or intelligence services of the Czech Republic could be endangered or seriously disrupted. If evidence is taken otherwise than by examination, this paragraph shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(2) The presiding judge shall decide that parts of the file containing classified information shall be separated if, in connection with such information, the activities of the Police of the Czech Republic or intelligence services of the Czech Republic could be endangered or seriously disrupted; the participant in the proceedings, their representative, and persons involved in the proceedings may not inspect the separated parts of the file.

p85m.heading: Airport Security Programme
p85m.text:
(1) An airport operator must not operate an airport without an approved airport security programme in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23). The airport security programme shall be approved by the Office upon the request of the airport operator, to which the airport security programme is appended; this request may also be submitted by an applicant for an airport operating licence concurrently with the application for an airport operating licence.

(2) The Office shall approve the airport security programme if it contains measures ensuring a sufficient level of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference at the airport in accordance with this Act and the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23). The only participant in the proceedings for the approval of the airport security programme shall be the applicant. In the proceedings for the approval of the airport security programme, the Police of the Czech Republic shall be the affected authority.

(3) A change to the airport security programme affecting the provision of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference shall be approved by the Office upon the request of the airport operator. Paragraph 2 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the approval of a change to the airport security programme. The airport operator shall immediately notify the Office of any changes to the airport security programme other than those specified in the first sentence.

(4) The substantive requirements and structure of the airport security programme shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p85n.heading: Obligations of the Airport Operator
p85n.text:
(1) In accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), the airport operator shall divide the airport area according to the type of security measures applied into individual zones, visibly mark them, and, using appropriate security elements, ensure that passage between them is possible only upon fulfillment of the conditions stipulated by this Act and the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

(2) In accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), the airport operator shall further ensure:

- a) the performance of screening of persons and vehicles entering and driving into the airport area,

- b) the issuance of airport identification cards and entry permits,

- c) the performance of detection screening of persons other than passengers and their carry-on items,

- d) the security of the airport area.

p85o.heading: Information on Aircraft Security Search
p85o.text: The air carrier shall ensure that information on the aircraft security search in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) is also kept on board the aircraft for the period stipulated by this directly applicable regulation.

p85p.heading: Detection Screening of Passengers, Cabin Baggage, and Hold Baggage
p85p.text:
(1) The airport operator and the air carrier shall ensure the performance of screening of passengers, cabin baggage and hold baggage in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

(2) An air carrier may permit an item, the bringing of which is prohibited by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), to be brought on board an aircraft only with the prior consent of the Office and under the conditions stipulated by this European Union regulation. If an air carrier permits such an item to be brought on board an aircraft, it shall immediately notify the affected airport operator thereof.

(3) The Office shall, upon request of a natural or legal person, grant consent for an item, the bringing of which on board an aircraft is prohibited by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), to be brought on board, if, taking into account the physical and chemical properties of the item and the purpose of its use, it can be considered that its bringing does not pose a threat to civil aviation.

(4) The only participant in the proceedings for granting consent is the applicant. In the proceedings for granting consent, the Office shall inspect the item for which permission to bring on board an aircraft is requested. In the decision on granting consent for bringing an item, the Office shall stipulate the conditions for safe handling of the item after it has been brought into the airport area and on board the aircraft, and the period for which the consent is granted.

(5) If the Office has granted consent for bringing an item, the authorized official shall, immediately after the decision is issued, place the item in a sealable opaque package, which shall be visibly marked with a seal or other visible securing device with a unique numerical identifier, stated in the decision, in such a way that the item cannot be removed from it without breaking the securing device. Anyone handling the item must ensure that the item is placed in the package equipped with the securing device during its bringing into the airport area and on board the aircraft and for the entire period of its storage in the airport area and on board the aircraft. The air carrier shall ensure that the item is safely accompanied from its bringing into the airport area and safely stored in the airport area and on board the aircraft.

p85q.heading: Subsequent protection of passengers, cabin baggage and hold baggage
p85q.text: The airport operator and the air carrier shall ensure the subsequent protection of passengers, cabin baggage and hold baggage after their screening has been carried out in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

p85r.heading: Security Programme of the Air Navigation Service Provider
p85r.text:
(1) The air navigation service provider shall develop and adhere to a security programme that contains measures ensuring, in accordance with this Act and a decision of an international organization issued on the basis of an international treaty that is part of the legal order, a sufficient level of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) when providing air navigation services.

(2) The air navigation service provider shall, without undue delay, make its security programme available to the Office upon its request.

(3) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the content requirements and structure of the security programme of the air navigation service provider.

p85s.heading: Person performing security control in connection with cargo and mail instead of an approved agent
p85s.text:
(1) A natural or legal person in the position of an approved agent according to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) may entrust another natural or legal person with the performance of security control of cargo and mail outside its premises, if it stipulates their duties ensuring a sufficient level of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference in its security programme and ensures their proper fulfillment.

(2) A natural or legal person may perform security control of cargo and mail instead of an approved agent outside its premises with the consent of the Office granted upon its request. In addition to the general requirements for submissions according to the Administrative Procedure Code, the request shall contain a definition of the type and scope of security control that the applicant intends to perform.

(3) The Office shall grant consent if the applicant

- a) meets the conditions stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) for performing the security control specified in the request and

- b) has implemented measures for this activity ensuring its proper performance.

(4) The person performing security control in connection with cargo and mail instead of an approved agent shall immediately notify the Office of any change in facts affecting the granting of consent.

(5) The Office shall withdraw consent if the person to whom it was granted

- a) ceased to meet the conditions for granting consent,

- b) seriously violates the obligations stipulated by this Act or the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), or

- c) requested the withdrawal of consent.

p85t.heading: Register of persons authorized to perform security control in connection with cargo and mail instead of an approved agent
p85t.text:
(1) A non-public register of persons authorized to carry out security screening in relation to cargo and mail in place of a regulated agent (hereinafter referred to as the „register of authorized persons“) is hereby established, the purpose of which is, in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), to make available an overview of these persons and their services to regulated agents.

(2) The administrator of the register of authorized persons is the Office. Access to it is granted only to regulated agents, for whom the Office will provide remote access.

(3) The Office shall enter a person to whom it has granted consent to carry out security screening in relation to cargo and mail in place of a regulated agent into the register of authorized persons within 3 days from the date on which the decision to grant such consent becomes final.

(4) The following types of personal data concerning natural persons who are not entrepreneurs are processed in the register of authorized persons:

- a) first name(s),

- b) surname,

- c) date of birth, and

- d) address of permanent residence, or other residence in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(5) In the case of a natural person who is an entrepreneur, the following types of personal data are processed in the register of authorized persons:

- a) business name or first name(s) and surname,

- b) address of registered office, and

- c) identification number, if assigned.

(6) The following types of data concerning legal entities are processed in the register of authorized persons:

- a) business name or designation,

- b) address of registered office, and

- c) identification number, if assigned.

(7) The following types of data are further processed in the register of authorized persons:

- a) date of entry, and

- b) date of deletion.

(8) Personal data are processed in the register of authorized persons for the duration of the consent to carry out security screening in relation to cargo and mail in place of a regulated agent; after the withdrawal of consent, the Office shall immediately dispose of such data.

p85u.heading: Screening and subsequent protection of air carrier items
p85u.text: The air carrier shall ensure the screening of its items and their subsequent protection after screening in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

p85w.heading: Training
p85w.text: Everyone for whom persons perform duties related to the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference shall ensure that the work activities stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) are carried out by natural persons who have successfully completed training.

p85x.heading: Provision of training
p85x.text:
(1) A natural person may provide training in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) based on a permit issued upon their request by the Office.

(2) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the request shall contain a proposal for the type of training in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) that the applicant intends to provide. The request shall be accompanied by

- a) a document proving the highest attained education in original or certified copy,

- b) an affidavit regarding previous experience in the field of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference, containing a description of all performed activities related to the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference, including their duration, and

- c) a proposal for the content, scope, and form of the training that the applicant intends to provide.

(3) The Office shall issue a permit for the provision of training if

- a) the applicant

- 1. has reached the age of 18,

- 2. has at least a complete secondary education with a maturita examination,

- 3. has performed activities in the field of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference for at least 1 year,

- 4. has demonstrated professional knowledge and practical skills necessary for the provision of the proposed type of training within the administrative proceedings, and

- b) the proposal for the content, scope, and form of the training that the applicant intends to provide complies with the conditions for the provision of training under the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

(4) The only participant in the proceedings for issuing a permit is the applicant. The applicant's professional knowledge and practical skills shall be demonstrated in the proceedings by a theoretical and practical examination before a three-member expert commission composed of employees of the Office, whose members are appointed and dismissed by the Director General of the Office.

(5) In the decision on issuing the permit, the Office shall state the type of training in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) that the applicant is authorized to provide, and the period of validity of the decision.

(6) The Office shall revoke the issued permit if the natural person providing the training (hereinafter referred to as the „trainer“)

- a) fails to comply with the conditions for providing training stipulated by this Act or by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), or

- b) has requested the revocation of the permit.

(7) The content and form of the examination, during which the professional knowledge and practical skills of the applicant for a permit to provide training are demonstrated, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p85y.heading: Completion of training
p85y.text:
(1) Professional training pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) shall conclude with an examination. The trainer shall issue a written document to the person who has successfully completed the professional training, attesting to this fact. If a natural person does not hold a valid document of completed professional training, they shall not perform activities directly related to such professional training.

(2) The period of validity of the document of completed professional training for natural persons performing activities directly related to the performance of detection screening, for whom directly applicable European Union legislation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) requires successful completion of professional training at least once every 6 months, shall be 6 months. In other cases, the validity of the document of completed professional training shall be 2 years.

(3) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the template for the document of completed professional training.

p85z.heading: Specialised training for dog handlers and dogs intended for the detection of explosives
p85z.text:
(1) A natural or legal person may conduct specialised training for dog handlers and dogs intended for the detection of explosives pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) on the basis of a permit issued by the Office upon their application.

(2) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the application shall contain the first name(s) and surname of the trainer designated by the applicant to provide the specialised training (hereinafter referred to as the „training guarantor“). The application shall be accompanied by

- a) a document certifying the applicant's authorisation to handle explosives in the territory of the Czech Republic,

- b) a document certifying the training guarantor's authorisation to handle explosives in the territory of the Czech Republic,

- c) an affidavit from the training guarantor regarding their previous experience in the field of dog training, including a description of all activities performed, including their duration, and

- d) a proposal for the content, scope, and form of the specialised training that the applicant intends to conduct.

(3) The Office shall issue a permit for conducting specialised training if

- a) the applicant performs detection screening pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23),

- b) the training guarantor has performed activities in the field of dog training for at least 5 years and has demonstrated, within the administrative proceedings, the professional knowledge and practical skills necessary for the detection of explosives and the proper provision of specialised training, and

- c) the proposal for the content, scope, and form of the specialised training that the applicant intends to conduct includes the use of means ensuring the achievement of sufficient detection capability of dogs and complies with the conditions for conducting specialised training pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

(4) The only participant in the proceedings for issuing the permit shall be the applicant. The professional knowledge and practical skills of the training guarantor shall be demonstrated in the proceedings by a theoretical examination and a practical test before a three-member expert committee, whose members are appointed and dismissed by the Director General of the Office. The majority of the members of the expert committee shall be members or employees of the Police of the Czech Republic or the Army of the Czech Republic, whose activities include conducting training of dogs intended for the detection of explosives. The actual costs incurred in connection with the proceedings for issuing the permit shall be borne by the applicant. The permit shall be valid for 5 years.

(5) The Office shall revoke the issued permit if the natural or legal person conducting the specialised training

- a) fails to comply with the conditions for conducting specialised training stipulated by this Act or directly applicable European Union legislation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), or

- b) has requested the revocation of the permit.

(6) The content and form of the theoretical examination and practical test, by which the professional knowledge and practical skills of the training guarantor and the means ensuring the achievement of sufficient detection capability of dogs are demonstrated, shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

p86.heading: Use of means serving to protect civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference
p86.text: A natural or legal person who ensures the performance of detection screening pursuant to this Act or directly applicable European Union legislation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) shall ensure that the means serving to protect civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference (hereinafter referred to as „security means“), used in the performance of detection screening, meet the requirements for detection capabilities, technical settings, and operational security stipulated by this directly applicable European Union legislation.

p86a.heading: Dogs intended for the detection of explosives
p86a.text:
(1) A natural or legal person that ensures the performance of detection screening may, in fulfilling this obligation, permit the use of a dog designated for the detection of explosives only if the person performing the duties of a dog handler has been issued a permit by the Office for the performance of this activity. This shall not apply if the dog used for the detection of explosives is used by the Police of the Czech Republic or the Army of the Czech Republic.

(2) The Office shall, upon request by a natural person performing the duties of a dog handler, issue a permit for the use of a dog designated for the detection of explosives if this person, together with the dog equipped with an electronic chip, has been successfully trained in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23) and has demonstrated, within the administrative proceedings, the professional knowledge and practical skills necessary for the detection of explosives as stipulated by this directly applicable European Union regulation.

(3) In addition to the general requirements for submissions under the Administrative Procedure Code, the request shall contain the designation of the subject of the detection screening for which the dog designated for the detection of explosives will be used by the applicant. The request shall further contain the following data identifying the dog that the applicant intends to use for detection screening:

- a) name, or names,

- b) date of birth,

- c) breed,

- d) sex,

- e) identification data stored on the electronic chip with which the dog is equipped.

(4) The request shall be accompanied by a document proving successful completion of professional training for dog handlers in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

(5) The only participant in the proceedings for issuing the permit is the applicant. The professional knowledge and practical skills of the person performing the duties of a dog handler necessary for the detection of explosives shall be demonstrated in the proceedings by a theoretical examination and a practical test in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23). The theoretical examination and practical test shall be conducted before a three-member expert commission, whose members are appointed and dismissed by the Director General of the Office. The majority of the members of the expert commission shall be members or employees of the Police of the Czech Republic or the Army of the Czech Republic, whose activity includes the training of dogs designated for the detection of explosives. The practical test shall also include verification of the detection capabilities of the dog that the applicant intends to use. The actual costs incurred in connection with the proceedings for issuing the permit shall be borne by the applicant. In the decision on issuing the permit, the Office shall state its period of validity and the data identifying the dog that the applicant intends to use for detection screening.

(6) The Office shall revoke the issued permit if the permit holder

- a) fails to comply with the conditions for the use of a dog designated for the detection of explosives in detection screening stipulated by this Act or by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), or

- b) has requested the revocation of the permit.

(7) An appeal against a decision on the revocation of a permit shall not have suspensive effect.

p86b.heading: Different method of performing detection screening
p86b.text:
(1) The Office may, upon request by a natural or legal person that ensures the performance of detection screening, permit that person to ensure the performance of detection screening by a different method than that stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), including the use of a security measure utilizing the latest scientific knowledge, if the conditions stipulated by this directly applicable European Union regulation are met.

(2) The only participant in the proceedings for issuing the permit is the applicant. In the decision on issuing the permit, the Office shall state the conditions for the permitted method of performing detection screening and the period of validity of the permit.

(3) The Office shall revoke the issued permit if the permit holder

- a) fails to comply with the conditions for the permitted method of performing detection screening stipulated by this Act, the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), or the decision on issuing the permit, or

- b) has requested the revocation of the permit.

(4) An appeal against a decision on the revocation of a permit shall not have suspensive effect.

p86c.heading: Notification obligation
p86c.text:
(1) A natural or legal person that intends to operate an airport or commercial air transport under this Act or to carry out the activity of an approved agent, known consignor, approved supplier of in-flight supplies, or instructor in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), shall notify the Office of this fact at least 45 days before submitting an application for a decision of the administrative authority establishing the right to perform this activity. For a natural or legal person that intends to carry out professional training of dog handlers and dogs designated for the detection of explosives, and for a natural or legal person that intends to perform security screening in connection with cargo and mail instead of an approved agent outside its premises, the first sentence shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(2) A natural or legal person that intends to carry out the activity of a permanent consignor, known supplier of in-flight supplies, or known supplier of airport supplies in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), shall notify the Office of this fact at least 45 days before the date of commencement of this activity. For any other natural or legal person that intends to transport cargo or mail subjected to security screening in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), the first sentence shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(3) A natural or legal person shall promptly inform the Office of any change in the facts stated in its notification; this shall not apply if the decision establishing the right to perform its activity has become final.

p86d.heading: Basic measures
p86d.text:
(1) If the Office receives a notification pursuant to § 86c, it shall without undue delay, ex officio, on the basis of and within the limits of a European Commission decision regulating the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference, determine for the natural or legal person who made this notification the basic measures necessary to ensure a sufficient level of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference in the performance of its activities (hereinafter referred to as „basic measures“). This shall not apply if the Office has reasonable suspicion that the notifier could misuse their knowledge of the basic measures and expose civil aviation to the danger of an act of unlawful interference. In such a case, the Office shall without undue delay inform them of this fact. The procedure under this paragraph shall not apply to access to classified information under the Act regulating the protection of classified information.

(2) The only party to the proceedings for the determination of basic measures shall be the person for whom the basic measures are determined (hereinafter referred to as „recipient of basic measures“). The decision determining the basic measures shall be the first act in the proceedings. An appeal against the decision shall not have suspensive effect.

(3) If it is necessary for the control of compliance with the basic measures, the Office may, in its decision, impose on the recipient of basic measures the obligation to inform it of facts significant for assessing the content and scope of the performance of their activities.

(4) Everyone is obliged to maintain confidentiality regarding the facts contained in the decision by which basic measures were determined; this shall not apply if these facts are made available to a natural or legal person who necessarily needs them to fulfil their obligations imposed by law or by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23).

(5) Until the date of enforceability of the decision by which basic measures were determined, the recipient of basic measures may not carry out the activity with which these measures are directly related.

(6) If the Office ascertains facts on the basis of which it is necessary to change the determined basic measures, in particular if the European Commission decision regulating the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference changes, it shall amend the decision by which such measures were determined. In this case, the Office shall also set a deadline for fulfilling the obligations arising from the change of basic measures. If the reason for determining the basic measures ceases to exist, the Office shall revoke the decision by which such measures were determined. The provisions of paragraph 2 shall apply mutatis mutandis to proceedings for the amendment or revocation of a decision.

p86e.heading: Special Measures
p86e.text:
(1) If permitted by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), the Office may, after assessing local conditions, determine special measures for an airport operator or air carrier at their request, containing derogations from the provisions of this directly applicable European Union regulation, provided that a sufficient level of protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference is still ensured by the special measure.

(2) The only party to the proceedings for the determination of a special measure shall be the person for whom the special measure is determined.

(3) If the Office ascertains facts on the basis of which it is necessary to change the determined special measure, it shall amend the decision by which such measure was determined. In this case, the Office shall also set a deadline for fulfilling the obligations arising from the change of the special measure. If the reason for determining the special measure ceases to exist, or if the airport operator or air carrier for whom the special measure was determined requests it, the Office shall revoke the decision by which such measure was determined. The provisions of paragraph 2 shall apply mutatis mutandis to proceedings for the amendment or revocation of a decision. An appeal against a decision on the amendment or revocation of a decision by which a special measure was determined shall not have suspensive effect.

p86f.heading: Extraordinary Measures
p86f.text:
(1) If an airport operator ascertains that civil aviation is immediately threatened by a particularly serious act of unlawful interference, they shall immediately adopt, for the period strictly necessary, the following extraordinary measures:

- a) a stricter method of conducting checks at the airport,

- b) stricter conditions for entry and access to the airport area,

- c) a stricter procedure for ensuring airport security, or

- d) restriction or suspension of airport operations.

(2) If an air carrier ascertains that civil aviation is immediately threatened by a particularly serious act of unlawful interference, they shall immediately adopt, for the period strictly necessary, a stricter method of conducting checks or a stricter procedure for ensuring the security of commercial air transport operations.

(3) If an airport operator or air carrier ascertains new facts on the basis of which it is necessary to change an adopted extraordinary measure, they shall amend such measure. If the reason for adopting an extraordinary measure ceases to exist, the airport operator or air carrier shall revoke such measure.

(4) The airport operator or air carrier shall immediately notify the Office of the adoption, amendment, or revocation of an extraordinary measure.

(5) If civil aviation is immediately threatened by a particularly serious act of unlawful interference and the airport operator or air carrier has not adopted an extraordinary measure, or if such measure is not sufficient, the Office shall order an extraordinary measure ex officio.

(6) The only party to the proceedings for ordering an extraordinary measure shall be the person for whom the extraordinary measure is ordered. The decision ordering the extraordinary measure shall be the first act in the proceedings. An appeal against the decision shall not have suspensive effect. The Office shall notify the European Commission of the issuance of the decision without undue delay.

(7) If the Office ascertains new facts on the basis of which it is necessary to change an ordered extraordinary measure, it shall amend the decision by which such measure was ordered. If the reason for ordering an extraordinary measure ceases to exist, the Office shall revoke the decision by which such measure was ordered. The provisions of paragraph 6 shall apply mutatis mutandis to proceedings for the amendment or revocation of a decision.

p87.text:
(1) State administration in civil aviation matters shall be exercised, in accordance with and to the extent defined by this Act and in accordance with international treaties that form part of the legal order, by the Ministry of Transport, the Office, the Institute, and the Office for the Protection of Competition.

(2) State administration in military aviation matters shall be exercised, in accordance with and to the extent defined by this Act, by the Ministry of Defence.

p88.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport, in accordance with this Act,

- a) administers aircraft addresses allocated to the Czech Republic by an international organization,

- b) ensures the operation of an airport for the period during which the airport owner is unable to ensure its operation by its own means or through another operator,

- c) in cooperation with the Ministry of Defence and the Ministry of the Interior, ensures aeronautical search and rescue services,

- d) decides on the granting and withdrawal of traffic rights to air carriers of the European Union,

- e) decides on the authorization and revocation of authorization for the operation of scheduled commercial air transport and non-scheduled commercial air transport by an air carrier from a third country,

- f) approves operational and technical regulations for the operation of sport flying equipment and training syllabi,

- g) grants consent for flights for the purpose of exercising state administration,

- h) entrusts a legal entity with the exercise of state administration in matters of sport flying equipment and withdraws the granted entrustment,

- i) deals with offenses committed by natural and legal persons in the field of operation of sport flying equipment,

- j) appoints representatives of the Czech Republic to the administrative board of the Agency and to the committee established under the directly applicable European Union regulation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c),

- k) submits a statement on spatial planning documentation and spatial measures on building moratoriums from the perspective of air transport interests,

- l) maintains a database, grants and withdraws authorization for a legal entity to maintain the database, protects and makes the database accessible in accordance with § 51a,

- m) submits a report to the European Commission on the application of the just culture approach in accordance with the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the reporting of occurrences in civil aviation^37).

(2) The Ministry of Transport is the appellate authority in administrative proceedings against decisions of the Office issued under this Act. When deciding on an appeal against a decision to withdraw a certificate of competency for the performance of duties of an aircrew member due to medical unfitness, the Ministry of Transport is bound by the opinion of the Ministry of Health.

(3) The Ministry of Transport is the appellate body in administrative proceedings against decisions of the authorized person issued under this Act.

(4) The Ministry of Transport is the appellate authority in administrative proceedings against decisions of the Institute issued under this Act.

(5) The Ministry of Transport is authorized, in extraordinary situations that immediately and seriously threaten civil aviation, to issue orders for the performance of flights for the absolutely necessary period.

p89.text:
(1) The Office

- a) cooperates with the Agency and concludes an agreement with it on the terms of cooperation,

- b) performs the tasks of the national supervisory authority pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation^1e),

- c) authorises

- 1. a legal entity to perform tasks in the field of conformity or suitability for use assessment of components and verification of European Air Traffic Management Network systems and withdraws the granted authorisation,

- 2. a legal or natural person to assess and verify product property conformity, assess and verify airworthiness or inspect airworthiness and suspends or withdraws the granted authorisation,

- 3. a medical doctor of a healthcare provider and, in agreement with the Ministry of Defence and the Ministry of Health, designates healthcare providers,

- 4. a natural or legal person to evaluate airline flight schedules and issue related recommendations,

- d) supervises the fulfilment of airline obligations regarding compensation and assistance to passengers in the event of denied boarding, cancellation or significant delay of a flight and handles complaints about violations of these obligations,

- e) decides

- 1. on the approval of a product type and assesses and verifies product property conformity,

- 2. on airworthiness and issues airworthiness certificates and verifies airworthiness,

- 3. on the approval of airworthiness of an individually manufactured product and issues airworthiness certificates,

- 4. on the unairworthiness of an aircraft and withdraws airworthiness certificates and recognises as valid airworthiness certificates issued by another state,

- 5. on the determination of the type of airport and its change,

- 6. on the temporary suspension of operation of an airport or part thereof,

- f) performs airworthiness inspections,

- g) withholds airworthiness certificates until the airworthiness defect is remedied,

- h) grants consent for experimental flying,

- i) approves, recognises and inspects the suitability of aircraft parts and equipment and aeronautical ground equipment for their use in civil aviation,

- j) issues, suspends the validity of and withdraws authorisations for the development, design, manufacture, testing, installation, maintenance, repair, modification and structural changes of products, aircraft parts and equipment and aeronautical ground equipment,

- k) assigns and withdraws aircraft addresses and maintains a list of all assigned aircraft addresses,

- l) maintains records of aviation personnel, issues aviation personnel licences and recognises the validity of aviation personnel licences issued by another state, withholds or withdraws aviation personnel licences, orders and performs verification of their professional and flight fitness, verification of professional competence or review of medical fitness of an aviation personnel member and restricts or prohibits the activity for which the aviation personnel member is authorised,

- m) approves the operator's safety flight standard and a deviation from the operator's safety flight standard for an individual flight,

- n) issues airport operating permits and decides on their cancellation,

- o) maintains records of airports,

- p) designates an operating airport as a scheduled airport or a coordinated airport and authorises a coordinator to allocate available airport slots to air carriers,

- q) supervises the fulfilment of airport operator and air carrier obligations regarding compliance with the rights of persons with disabilities and persons with reduced mobility and orientation,

- r) supervises the fulfilment of air carrier obligations regarding informing air transport passengers pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation^1i),

- s) is the competent authority pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing rules for the issuance of air traffic controller licences and certain certificates^16),

- t) deals with infringements by natural and legal persons in the field of operation of sport flying equipment,

- u) inspects the fulfilment of the domestic air carrier's obligation to draw up a plan for assisting victims of air accidents and their relatives,

- v) monitors the implementation of measures adopted in response to safety recommendations issued by the Institute pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the investigation of accidents and incidents in civil aviation^19),

- w) deals with infringements by natural and legal persons in the field of implementing procedures for monitoring the implementation of measures adopted in response to safety recommendations issued by the Institute pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the investigation of accidents and incidents in civil aviation^19) and

- x) maintains the Aircraft Register and decides on

- 1. the entry of an aircraft into the Aircraft Register and the deletion of an aircraft from the Aircraft Register,

- 2. the entry of a pledge right to an aircraft into the Aircraft Register and the deletion of a pledge right from the Aircraft Register,

- 3. the provisional assignment of a registration mark or aircraft address,

- 4. the assignment of an aircraft address to ground coding equipment and sport flying equipment and

- 5. the change of data entered in the Aircraft Register.

(2) The Office further

- a) approves and cancels the operational suitability of an airport,

- b) establishes protection zones for the protection of aeronautical structures, with the exception of the airport noise protection zone,

- c) grants consent

- 1. for the construction of buildings, non-building projects, activities and operation of facilities in the protection zones of aeronautical structures and outside the protection zones for the placement of buildings and facilities exceeding a specified height, or buildings and facilities that may endanger the safety of air traffic or have a disruptive effect on specified aeronautical facilities,

- 2. to a legal or natural person for the provision of aeronautical telecommunication services, aeronautical meteorological services, aeronautical information services, pre-flight preparation and flight monitoring services, flight procedure design services,

- 3. to a legal or natural person for the provision of ground handling services and decides on its withdrawal,

- 4. for the operation of public air displays,

- d) in agreement with the Ministry of Defence, divides the airspace of the Czech Republic,

- e) issues a general measure defining the rules for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic,

- f) authorises a legal or natural person to provide airport services and withdraws the granted authorisation,

- g) issues certificates pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the provision of air navigation services in the single European sky^18),

- h) designates a legal entity and cancels the designation of a legal entity for the provision of air traffic and meteorological services pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the creation of the single European sky and its functioning^17),

- i) approves a service use agreement concluded pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the provision of air navigation services in the single European sky^18),

- j) cancels the authorisation of a legal entity to provide air traffic services or meteorological services based on a service use agreement concluded pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the provision of air navigation services in the single European sky^18),

- k) in agreement with the Ministry of Defence, reserves parts of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying,

- l) publishes a notice in the Aeronautical Information Publication or by other suitable means that the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic for flying over certain areas is permanently or temporarily dangerous,

- m) issues

- 1. a general measure introducing measures to regulate ground handling services due to safety or operational congestion at the airport,

- 2. a licence for the operation of commercial air transport and decides on its amendment or withdrawal,

- 3. an air operator certificate and decides on its withdrawal,

- 4. a permit for the operation of aerial work,

- 5. a permit for the operation of aerial activities for own use,

- 6. a restriction or prohibition of flying for certain sport flying equipment,

- n) decides on the restriction of commercial air transport, unless this decision-making is entrusted to another authority on the basis of a special legal regulation,

- o) ensures the exercise of state administration in matters of sport flying equipment, unless ensured by an authorised person,

- p) authorises a legal entity to exercise state supervision over the provision of air navigation services and withdraws the granted authorisation,

- q) performs other tasks stipulated by special legal regulations^6b) and directly applicable European Union regulations,

- r) ensures the coordination of civil air traffic with military air traffic,

- s) issues, amends and cancels decisions on the introduction of operational restrictions to reduce noise at an airport and performs other tasks of the competent authority pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing rules and procedures for the introduction of operational restrictions to reduce noise at airports^35),

- t) supervises the fulfilment of obligations of employers and organisations in applying a just culture approach pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the reporting of occurrences in civil aviation^37),

- u) is the competent authority for the certification of persons and organisations and the oversight thereof pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c),

- v) performs the tasks of the competent authority pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39) and directly applicable European Union legislation governing the U-space airspace^40), with the exception of tasks performed by the Institute,

- w) is the administrator of the register of remote pilots,

- x) performs the tasks of the competent authority pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the area of aviation fuels^46) in relation to airport operators, air carriers and operators of aviation activities other than commercial air transport, and

- y) in cooperation with the State Energy Inspectorate, prepares and publishes a report on the use of revenues from fines pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the area of aviation fuels^46).

(3) In the field of civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference, the Office

- a) performs the tasks of the competent authority, including the information obligation of the European Union Member State to the European Commission, pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23) and regulations issued thereunder,

- b) issues, updates and publishes national programmes pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23) or pursuant to a decision of an international organisation issued on the basis of an international treaty that is part of the legal order,

- c) carries out verification and reliability review of persons entering the security restricted area pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23),

- d) approves the airport security programme and its amendments,

- e) grants consent to allow the introduction of an item whose introduction into the airport area and on board an aircraft is prohibited by directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23),

- f) grants and withdraws consent for performing security screening in connection with cargo and mail instead of an approved agent outside its premises,

- g) is the administrator of the register of persons authorised to perform security screening related to cargo and mail instead of an approved agent,

- h) issues and cancels permits for conducting professional training pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23),

- i) issues and cancels permits for conducting professional training of dog handlers and dogs designated for detecting explosives pursuant to directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23),

- j) issues and cancels permits for the use of a dog designated for detecting explosives when performing detection screening,

- k) issues and cancels permits for ensuring the performance of detection screening in a different manner than stipulated by directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23),

- l) determines, amends and cancels basic measures necessary to ensure a sufficient level of civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference pursuant to § 86d,

- m) determines, amends and cancels special measures containing derogations from the provisions of directly applicable European Union legislation governing civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference^23) pursuant to § 86e and

- n) decides on the order, amendment and cancellation of an extraordinary measure pursuant to § 86f.

p89a.text:
The Office for the Protection of Competition

- a) issues decisions on whether the published airport price list, its components, and the method of their determination are not in conflict with § 42e,

- b) publishes, in a manner enabling remote access, a list of airports to which the provisions of Part Four, Chapter VI apply, and

- c) monitors the fulfilment of obligations of the airport operator and air carrier pursuant to Part Four, Chapter VI.

p90.text:
(1) State supervision in civil aviation shall be exercised, within the scope of their competence, by the Ministry of Transport, the Office, and the Institute.

(2) The authorisation to perform state supervision shall be in the form of an identification card.

(3) When performing state supervision during flight, the inspected person shall be obliged to transport the inspecting person by aircraft to the necessary extent and free of charge.

(4) The particulars and specimen of the identification card for the performance of state supervision shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p91.text:
(1) If the inspecting person, during the exercise of state supervision, ascertains a breach of obligations stipulated by legal regulations in civil aviation, they shall, as necessary and according to the nature of the ascertained deficiencies, impose the method and deadline for the removal of these deficiencies and their causes.

(2) If the life or health of persons or the safety of air traffic could be immediately endangered, the inspecting person shall prohibit the performance of the activity, or seize documents related to this activity. The Ministry of Transport or the Office shall initiate administrative proceedings related to the prohibition of the performance of the activity or the seizure of documents within 10 days of the issuance of the decision. The decision on the prohibition of the performance of the activity shall cease to be effective on the day of the termination of the administrative proceedings related to this prohibition by a final decision.

(3) The inspected person must enable persons authorised by an international organisation or the European Commission to perform control activities in accordance with an international treaty that is part of the legal order, or in accordance with a directly applicable regulation of the European Union^7a).

p91a.text:
(1) In exercising state supervision over the technical condition of aircraft, the Office shall carry out ramp inspections of aircraft landing in the territory of the Czech Republic and registered in the aircraft register of a state that is not a Member State of the European Union, particularly if

- a) it finds that aircraft maintenance is inadequate or that the aircraft is damaged or has technical or operational defects (hereinafter referred to as „technical defects“),

- b) after the aircraft's entry into the airspace of the Czech Republic, flight rules have been violated in a manner that raises concerns about flight safety,

- c) it suspects that previously identified technical defects have not been rectified,

- d) it finds that the competent authorities of the state in whose aircraft register the aircraft is registered do not exercise proper supervision over the technical condition of aircraft,

- e) it concludes from the data pursuant to § 12a para 1 that the aircraft operator endangers flight safety,

- f) a previous ramp inspection revealed technical defects in an aircraft of the same operator.

(2) The provisions of paragraph 1 shall not apply to state aircraft under an international treaty which is part of the legal order^1j), and to aircraft not used for commercial air transport and whose maximum take-off weight is less than 5,700 kg.

(3) The inspecting person shall draw up a report on the result of the inspection pursuant to paragraph 1, which shall be handed over to the aircraft commander immediately after the inspection is completed.

(4) Based on the results of the inspection pursuant to paragraph 1, the Office shall draw up a report, which it shall submit to the European Commission within 15 working days from the date of the inspection. Upon request, the Office shall submit the report to the competent authorities of the Member States of the European Union and to the Agency. If the inspection proves that the aircraft does not meet the conditions stipulated by aviation regulations pursuant to § 102 para 2 letter a) and may endanger flight safety, the Office shall immediately submit the report to the aircraft operator, the competent authorities of the Member States of the European Union, the European Commission, and the competent authorities of the state in whose register the aircraft is registered. The Office shall regularly inform the Agency about the adoption and implementation of measures to rectify deficiencies identified during the inspection pursuant to paragraph 1.

(5) The report pursuant to paragraph 3 and the report pursuant to paragraph 4 shall not include the identification of persons who provided data on the technical condition of the aircraft without being requested to do so.

(6) A decision imposing the method of rectifying deficiencies endangering flight safety and their causes during the performance of aircraft inspections on the airport ramp shall have the effect of a flight ban until these deficiencies are rectified. The decision shall include a statement designating the rectified deficiencies as deficiencies endangering flight safety.

(7) Upon request of the inspected person, the Office may, in agreement with the authority of the state in whose aircraft register the aircraft is registered, or the state exercising supervision over the technical condition of the aircraft, by decision exclude the effect of the flight ban pursuant to paragraph 6; the decision shall include the conditions for the flight to an airport where deficiencies endangering flight safety can be rectified. If the deficiencies endangering flight safety consist of non-compliance with airworthiness conditions, the Office may exclude the effect of the flight ban only if it receives permission from the authorities of the state whose airspace is to be used for the flight to the airport where the deficiencies can be rectified.

(8) Employees of the Office are obliged to maintain confidentiality regarding the results of technical condition inspections of aircraft carried out on the airport ramp, which were provided to them by the competent authorities of the Member States of the European Union. The obligation to maintain confidentiality does not apply to the mutual provision of information between administrative authorities in ensuring flight safety.

(9) Upon request, the Office shall provide the competent authority of a Member State of the European Union with a list of international airports, together with the number of take-offs and landings of aircraft specified in paragraph 1 and the number of inspections carried out pursuant to paragraph 1 at each of these airports for a calendar year.

(10) Upon request of the inspected person, the aircraft inspection on the airport ramp shall be conducted in English; this shall not apply if another inspected person, with whom the inspection is being carried out, does not agree, or if the purpose of the inspection would be jeopardized, or if such a procedure would entail disproportionate costs for the Office. A record shall be made of the manner in which the request under the first sentence is handled; if the inspection is conducted in English, the record shall be made in Czech and English. If the inspection is conducted in English, the decision issued in the on-site proceedings following the inspection shall be drawn up in Czech and English. In case of a discrepancy between the Czech and English versions of the record or decision, the Czech version shall prevail.

(11) The model of the report pursuant to paragraph 3 and the model of the report pursuant to paragraph 4 shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p91b.text:
(1) In exercising state supervision over the provision of air navigation services pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the establishment and functioning of the single European sky^17) or an international treaty that is part of the legal order, the Office shall cooperate with the competent authorities of other Member States of the European Union or another state that is a party to such an international treaty (hereinafter referred to as “another Member or third state”). For this purpose, the Office may conclude a written cooperation agreement with the competent authority of another Member or third state, which must contain:

- a) the designation of the contracting parties,

- b) the definition of the subject matter of the agreement,

- c) the rights and obligations of the contracting parties, and

- d) the method and conditions for withdrawal from the agreement.

(2) If an international treaty that is part of the legal order so stipulates, the Office shall also exercise state supervision over the fulfillment of obligations in the provision of air navigation services in the territory of another Member or third state. In such a case, it shall verify whether the air navigation service provider, to whom it has issued a certificate or whom it has designated pursuant to the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the provision of air navigation services in the single European sky^18) and who provides air navigation services in the territory of another Member or third state, fulfills the obligations arising for it from the legal regulations and decisions of the competent authority of another Member or third state.

(3) In exercising state supervision over the fulfillment of obligations in the provision of air navigation services in the territory of another Member or third state, the Office shall only take into account those legal regulations and decisions of the competent authority of another Member or third state that have been notified to it by that authority.

(4) The Office shall notify the competent authority of another Member or third state without undue delay of all results of the exercise of state supervision over the fulfillment of obligations in the provision of air navigation services in the territory of another Member or third state.

(5) If, in connection with the exercise of state supervision over the fulfillment of obligations in the provision of air navigation services in the territory of another Member or third state, the competent authority of another Member or third state requests the Office to perform acts that it could only perform with difficulty or at disproportionate cost, or that it could not perform at all, the Office shall immediately perform the requested acts or inform the competent authority of another Member or third state of the reasons why it cannot comply with the request. If the purpose of the request can be achieved by performing acts other than those requested, the Office shall immediately perform such acts and communicate their results to the competent authority of another Member or third state.

p91c.text:
(1) When the European Commission conducts an inspection in the field of civil aviation security against acts of unlawful interference, the Office shall cooperate with the European Commission to the extent stipulated by the directly applicable European Union regulation governing the procedures for conducting such an inspection^24) and shall provide it with all necessary assistance for this purpose. The Office shall also assign at least one of its employees to participate in inspections conducted by the European Commission and shall communicate their name, surname, and contact details to the Commission.

(2) During the inspection, the European Commission's inspectors are authorized to carry items necessary for the proper conduct of the inspection, including items whose introduction into the airport area and onto aircraft is prohibited.

p92a.heading: Offences in the field of ensuring the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference
p92a.text:
(1) A natural person commits an offence by

- a) failing to exercise due diligence when entering or remaining in areas serving to ensure the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference, in contravention of § 85b,

- b) failing to comply with an order of a natural or legal person ensuring the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference, in contravention of § 85c (2),

- c) not being reliable, despite being required to be so by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the protection of civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference^23), in contravention of § 85e (1),

- d) failing to surrender a found reliability document or a reliability document whose validity has expired, in contravention of § 85j (4),

- e) violating a condition for the safe handling of an item after its introduction into a restricted security area of an airport or on board an aircraft, as stipulated in the consent to introduce the item issued pursuant to § 85p (3),

- f) performing activities within the scope of protecting civil aviation against acts of unlawful interference without a valid certificate of completion of professional training, in contravention of § 85y (1),

- g) failing to maintain confidentiality regarding information contained in a decision establishing basic measures, in contravention of § 86d (4), or

- h) in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the performance of detection and security screening^27)

- 1. entering an airside area or a restricted security area of an airport without escort and without a valid entry authorisation or vehicle permit,

- 2. failing to immediately notify the airport operator of the loss or theft of an airport identification card or vehicle permit,

- 3. failing to immediately return an airport identification card or vehicle permit to the airport operator, or

- 4. unlawfully using an airport identification card or vehicle permit.

(2) A natural person commits an offence by, as

- a) a person performing detection or security screening, performing such screening in contravention of § 85d or in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the performance of detection and security screening^27),

- b) a passenger, in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the performance of detection and security screening^27)

- 1. bringing an item on board an aircraft whose introduction on board an aircraft is prohibited,

- 2. bringing an item into a restricted security area of an airport whose introduction into this area is prohibited, or

- 3. submitting for carriage a checked baggage containing an item whose introduction in checked baggage into a restricted security area of an airport is prohibited,

- c) a person handling an item for which the Office has granted consent for its introduction on board an aircraft, failing to ensure that the item is placed in packaging fitted with a security device pursuant to § 85p (5),

- d) a person ensuring the performance of detection screening, in contravention of § 86a (1), allowing the use of a dog designated for the detection of explosives without the person performing the duties of a dog handler having been issued a permit to perform this activity,

- e) in contravention of § 86c (2), failing to notify that they intend to perform the activity of a „regulated agent“, „known supplier of in-flight supplies“ or „known supplier of airport supplies“, or in contravention of § 86c (3), failing to inform the Office of a change in the facts stated in the notification, or

- f) a dog handler of a dog designated for the detection of explosives, performing this activity without a permit, or performing it in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the performance of detection and security screening^27).

(3) For an offence, a fine may be imposed

- a) up to CZK 100,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (d) or (h) point 2 or 3,

- b) up to CZK 500,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (a), (b), (c), (e), (f) or (h) point 1, or under paragraph 2, or

- c) from CZK 50,000 to CZK 1,000,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (g) or (h) point 4.

(4) For an offence, a prohibition of activity for up to 1 year may be imposed, in the case of an offence under paragraph 2 (f).

p92b.heading: Offences in the field of airspace use
p92b.text:
(1) A natural person commits an offence by

- a) in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the licensing of air traffic controllers and the conditions for the exercise of their activity^28), performing the activity of an air traffic controller, student air traffic controller, instructor or assessor

- 1. despite knowing about an illness or such changes in health status that limit or preclude the performance of this activity,

- 2. without notifying the air navigation service provider of their health indisposition, or

- 3. without a valid licence,

- b) in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing air traffic flow management^29), failing to ensure the update or cancellation of a flight plan in the event of its suspension, or

- c) providing air services without authorisation or consent.

(2) For an offence, a fine may be imposed

- a) up to CZK 100,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (b),

- b) up to CZK 500,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (a), or

- c) from CZK 100,000 to CZK 5,000,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (c).

(3) For an offence, a prohibition of activity may be imposed

- a) for up to 6 months, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (a) point 1 or 2, or

- b) for up to 1 year, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1 (a) point 3.

p92c.heading: Offences in the field of management and operation of unmanned aircraft systems
p92c.text:
(1) A natural person commits an administrative offense by, in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the operation of unmanned aircraft^39):

- a) as a remote pilot,

- 1. operating an unmanned aircraft system without holding a valid document certifying their competence to operate an unmanned aircraft system or at a time when they are prohibited from operating an unmanned aircraft system for a reason under § 54k para. 8,

- 2. operating an unmanned aircraft system under the influence of alcohol,

- 3. operating an unmanned aircraft system in a state of incapacity caused by the consumption of an alcoholic beverage or under the influence of another addictive substance,

- 4. failing to maintain the unmanned aircraft in visual line of sight, failing to perform an adequate visual inspection of the airspace around the unmanned aircraft, or failing to interrupt the flight of an unmanned aircraft that endangers flight safety,

- 5. operating an unmanned aircraft system in violation of the rules for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic defined by a general measure, an instruction issued under § 44 para. 5 or § 44h para. 5, or operational restrictions established by this directly applicable regulation, a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the conduct of flights in airspace^26), a document certifying authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems, a certificate of a light unmanned aircraft system operator, or the rules of an association,

- 6. operating an unmanned aircraft system in or near an intervention area of integrated rescue system components,

- 7. failing to fulfill any of the obligations established by this directly applicable regulation and thereby causing an accident, or

- 8. failing to fulfill another obligation of a remote pilot established by this directly applicable regulation before or during the flight of an unmanned aircraft,

- b) operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation without authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or a declaration of compliance with a standard scenario,

- c) as an unmanned aircraft system operator,

- 1. operating an unmanned aircraft system that does not meet the technical requirements established by this directly applicable regulation, a directly applicable European Union regulation governing unmanned aircraft systems^43), or the authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems,

- 2. failing to ensure that the unmanned aircraft system operated by them is operated by a pilot who has reached the age of 16,

- 3. not being registered in the register of unmanned aircraft system operators,

- 4. failing to display their registration number on the unmanned aircraft,

- 5. failing to report a safety-related event concerning the unmanned aircraft system operated by them to the Institute within the prescribed period or in the prescribed manner, or

- 6. failing to fulfill any of the obligations established by this directly applicable regulation and thereby causing an accident,

- d) as an unmanned aircraft system operator in the specific category of operation,

- 1. operating an unmanned aircraft system in contravention of the conditions established by the document certifying authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems,

- 2. failing to ensure compliance with operational restrictions established by this directly applicable regulation, a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the conduct of flights in airspace^26), a document certifying authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems, or the rules of an association,

- 3. failing to regularly evaluate the adequacy of adopted risk mitigation measures or failing to update them,

- 4. failing to apply for a change to the authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems, or

- 5. failing to notify the Office of a change in the data contained in the declaration of compliance with a standard scenario, or

- e) as an unmanned aircraft system operator, failing to fulfill another obligation of an unmanned aircraft system operator established by this directly applicable regulation.

(2) A natural person commits an administrative offense by, in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing controlled airspace for unmanned aircraft systems^40), as an unmanned aircraft system operator,

- a) operating an unmanned aircraft system without complying with the rules for the use of the airspace of the Czech Republic defined by a general measure issued under § 44g or without utilizing controlled unmanned aircraft system services provided in the part of the airspace of the Czech Republic defined by this general measure,

- b) failing to fulfill any of the obligations established by this directly applicable regulation before or during the flight of an unmanned aircraft, or

- c) failing to adopt measures or procedures for unforeseen situations or failing to make these measures or procedures available to providers of controlled unmanned aircraft system services.

(3) A natural person commits an administrative offense by:

- a) as a holder of

- 1. a certificate of competence for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open category of operation, in contravention of § 54e para. 7, failing to notify a change in the data contained in this certificate within the prescribed period or failing to submit documents proving this change,

- 2. a certificate of competence for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A1 and A3 of the open category of operation, whose incapacity to operate an unmanned aircraft system has been decided, in contravention of § 54e para. 8, failing to surrender this certificate to the Office within the prescribed period,

- 3. a certificate of competence for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A2 of the open category of operation, in contravention of § 54f para. 6, failing to notify a change in the data contained in this certificate within the prescribed period or failing to submit documents proving this change,
