- 4. a certificate of competence for operating an unmanned aircraft system in subcategory A2 of the open category of operation, whose incapacity to operate an unmanned aircraft system has been decided, in contravention of § 54f para. 7, failing to surrender this certificate to the Office within the prescribed period,

- 5. a certificate of theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation, in contravention of § 54g para. 6, failing to notify a change in the data contained in this certificate within the prescribed period or failing to submit documents proving this change,

- 6. a certificate of theoretical knowledge required for operating an unmanned aircraft system in accordance with a standard scenario in the specific category of operation, whose incapacity to operate an unmanned aircraft system has been decided, in contravention of § 54g para. 7, failing to surrender this certificate to the Office within the prescribed period, or

- 7. a confirmation of competence for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation, in contravention of § 54k para. 7, failing to return this confirmation to the unmanned aircraft system operator within the prescribed period,

- b) as a remote pilot, in contravention of § 54m para. 2, failing to comply with a request from the Police of the Czech Republic or the Office to

- 1. prove their identity,

- 2. present a document certifying their competence to operate an unmanned aircraft system,

- 3. undergo an orientation examination or a specialized medical examination, or

- 4. interrupt the flight of an unmanned aircraft,

- c) as an unmanned aircraft system operator,

- 1. in contravention of § 54a para. 5, failing to notify the Office of a change in the data recorded in the register of unmanned aircraft system operators within the prescribed period,

- 2. in contravention of § 54c para. 2 or 3, failing to surrender a document certifying authorization for the operation of unmanned aircraft systems or a certificate of a light unmanned aircraft system operator within the prescribed period,

- 3. in contravention of § 54d or a directly applicable European Union regulation governing insurance for air carriers and aircraft operators^10a), failing to have liability insurance for damages from the operation of an unmanned aircraft system with the minimum prescribed limit of insurance coverage or failing to pay the insurance premium,

- 4. in contravention of § 54k para. 1, failing to issue a confirmation of competence for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation or issuing this confirmation without the conditions for doing so being met,

- 5. in contravention of § 54k para. 2, failing to ensure that training is conducted to the prescribed extent, in the prescribed manner, or in cooperation with a person authorized by the Office,

- 6. in contravention of § 54k para. 9 letter a), failing to send a copy of the confirmation of competence of a remote pilot in the specific category of operation issued by them to the Office within the prescribed period, or

- 7. in contravention of § 54k para. 9 letter b), failing to inform the Office within the prescribed period that a reason for its return has arisen for the holder of the confirmation of competence for operating an unmanned aircraft system in the specific category of operation issued by them,

- d) as a member of an authorized association,

- 1. in contravention of § 54o para. 4, operating or piloting an unmanned aircraft system, or

- 2. operating an unmanned aircraft system in contravention of § 54p para. 2, failing to have liability insurance for damages from the operation of an unmanned aircraft system with the minimum prescribed limit of insurance coverage or failing to pay the insurance premium, or

- e) as a person whose membership in an authorized association has ceased, in contravention of § 54p para. 3, using a member card of an authorized association operating an unmanned aircraft system or failing to return this card within the prescribed period.

(4) A fine may be imposed for an administrative offense:

- a) up to CZK 50,000, in the case of an administrative offense under paragraph 1 letter a) point 2, 3 or 8, letter d) point 5 or letter e), paragraph 2 letter b) or paragraph 3 letter a), letter b) point 1, 2 or 3, letter c) point 1, 2 or 7 or letter e),

- b) up to CZK 75,000, in the case of an administrative offense under paragraph 1 letter a) point 4 or 6, letter c) point 2 or 5 or letter d) point 3 or 4 or paragraph 3 letter c) point 6,

- c) up to CZK 100,000, in the case of an administrative offense under paragraph 1 letter a) point 1, 5 or 7, letter b), letter c) point 1, 3 or 4 or letter d) point 1 or 2, paragraph 2 letter a) or c) or paragraph 3 letter b) point 4, letter c) point 3, 4 or 5 or letter d), or

- d) up to CZK 3,000,000, in the case of an administrative offense under paragraph 1 letter c) point 6.

Part 2

Administrative offenses of legal entities and natural persons carrying out business activities

p93.text:
(1) A legal entity or a natural person engaged in business commits an administrative offense by:

- a) failing to provide requested data to the Office upon request, in contravention of § 12a para. 4,

- b) using products, aircraft parts or equipment, or aeronautical ground equipment in civil aviation without a valid certificate, in contravention of § 16 para. 1,

- c) operating equipment or carrying out activities in the protection zone of an aviation structure without the consent of the Office, in contravention of § 40,

- d) constructing a building or facility outside protection zones in contravention of § 41 para. 1,

- e) placing a building or facility outside protection zones in contravention of § 41 para. 1,

- f) operating a public air display without consent, in contravention of § 78,

- g) failing to comply with an order issued by the Ministry of Transport pursuant to § 88 para. 5 or disobeying a prohibition issued by the Office pursuant to § 89 para. 2 letter o) point 6 to maintain civil aviation safety,

- h) failing to comply with an order issued by an airport operator, an air service provider, an air carrier, an air navigation service provider, or an operator of aerial work or aerial activities for own use, issued to fulfill their tasks,

- i) using aviation personnel for its aviation activity without a valid competency certificate,

- j) carrying out development, design, production, testing, installation, maintenance, repairs, modifications or structural changes of products, aircraft parts or equipment, or aeronautical ground equipment according to directly applicable European Union regulations governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c) without authorization,

- k) placing products on the market without a valid certificate, or

- k) operating an airport without a valid permit, operating commercial air transport without a license or without a permit, or operating aerial work or aerial activities for own use without a permit.

(2) A legal entity or a natural person engaged in business commits an administrative offense by, as a:

- a) operator of an aircraft registered in the aircraft register, owner of an aircraft, lien creditor, or person to whom the entry in the aircraft register pertains, failing to report or substantiate to the Office all changes to the data entered in the aircraft register, in contravention of § 6,

- b) aircraft operator, operator of a sport flying device, or operator of a coded device, using an aircraft address in contravention of § 17c para. 1,

- c) coordinator of a coordinated airport, carrying out its activity without liability insurance for damage caused by its performance, in contravention of § 32 para. 4,

- d) airport operator,

- 1. failing to send a report on the noise situation at the airport to the Office within the specified period pursuant to § 42a para. 1,

- 2. failing to supplement the report on the noise situation at the airport pursuant to § 42a para. 2,

- 3. failing to implement operational restrictions to reduce noise at the airport as stipulated in the Office's decision issued on the basis of § 42b para. 1,

- 4. referred to in § 42d para. 1, failing to negotiate the airport price list with air carriers pursuant to § 42g, or

- 5. capable of accepting instrument flights, failing to provide terrain and obstacle data or providing such data in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing the characteristics of aeronautical data and aeronautical information^31), in contravention of § 51a para. 7,

- e) aircraft operator, aircraft pilot, or air service provider, failing to notify the Institute without undue delay of an incident other than a serious incident, in contravention of § 55b para. 1,

- f) airport operator, domestic air carrier, or air traffic service provider, failing to provide the specified data, in contravention of § 98a para. 1,

- g) ground handling service provider, failing to provide the airport operator with data on the number of passengers handled by it, in contravention of § 98a para. 2,

- h) operator of an aircraft or sport flying device, failing to maintain airworthiness or failing to subject the aircraft or sport flying device to regular airworthiness checks,

- i) aircraft operator or air carrier, operating an aircraft without liability insurance for damages caused by its operation,

- j) operator of a sport flying device, operating a sport flying device without liability insurance for damages caused by its operation, or

- k) air carrier or operator of aerial work, violating the approved operator's safety flight standard.

(3) A legal entity or a natural person engaged in business commits an administrative offense by:

- a) intentionally and repeatedly using a coordinated airport at a time that differs substantially from the airport slot allocated by the coordinator, or using an allocated airport slot in a manner substantially different from that specified by the coordinator, in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing rules for the allocation of airport slots^25), thereby disrupting air traffic or airport operations,

- b) in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing the continuing airworthiness of aircraft and aeronautical products, parts and appliances^32),

- 1. carrying out continuing airworthiness management activities without authorization,

- 2. issuing or extending an airworthiness review certificate for an aircraft, or

- 3. certifying the release of an aircraft or aircraft component to service,

- c) conducting flights in the airspace of the Czech Republic in contravention of the conditions stipulated in this Act or directly applicable European Union regulations governing the conduct of flights in airspace^26),

- d) endangering flight safety by violating an aviation regulation or directly applicable European Union regulations governing the conduct of flights in airspace^26),

- e) causing an accident in air traffic during the operation of an aircraft or sport flying device by violating an obligation stipulated by this Act or directly applicable European Union regulations governing the conduct of flights in airspace^26),

- f) in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing the investigation of accidents and incidents in civil aviation^19),

- 1. making available or using records obtained during the investigation of an aviation accident or serious incident for purposes other than their investigation or the improvement of aviation operational safety,

- 2. as a participating person, failing to immediately notify the Institute that an accident or serious incident has occurred,

- 3. failing to inform the Institute within the specified period of measures it has taken based on its safety recommendation,

- 4. failing to implement appropriate procedures when implementing measures adopted following a safety recommendation issued by the Institute,

- 5. as a domestic air carrier, failing to implement procedures enabling the preparation of a list of persons and a list of dangerous goods on board the aircraft within the specified period from the notification of an aviation accident or failing to make these lists available to authorized entities, or

- 6. as a domestic air carrier, disclosing contact details intended for an accident to a third party or using them without authorization, or unlawfully publishing the names of passengers in connection with an aviation accident,

- g) in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing the reporting of occurrences in civil aviation^37),

- 1. as a person obliged to report an occurrence, failing to report this occurrence or failing to report it within the specified period or in the specified manner, or

- 2. as a participating person, using information provided from the European central repository,

- h) in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing the reporting of occurrences in civil aviation^37), as an organization,

- 1. failing to establish a mandatory reporting system facilitating the collection of data on occurrences or failing to establish a voluntary reporting system facilitating the collection of data on occurrences and other information related to an actual or potential threat to aviation safety,

- 2. failing to report to the Institute within the specified period data on an occurrence or other information related to an actual or potential threat to aviation safety collected through the mandatory or voluntary reporting system,

- 3. failing to designate one or more persons responsible for the collection, evaluation, processing, analysis, and storage of data on occurrences and other information related to an actual or potential threat to aviation safety,

- 4. failing to ensure that information obtained during the processing of occurrence reports is not used for non-safety purposes, or failing to ensure the protection of personal data or the identity of the reporting person or the person mentioned in the report,

- 5. failing to ensure that occurrence reports prepared based on data collected from the mandatory or voluntary occurrence reporting system contain the specified information, or that these occurrence reports are stored in the specified manner,

- 6. failing to implement procedures for quality control of collected and stored occurrence data,

- 7. failing to inform its employees or other persons about the analysis of occurrence data collected from the mandatory or voluntary occurrence reporting system or about corrective or preventive measures taken,

- 8. failing to develop a procedure for the analysis of occurrence data collected from the mandatory or voluntary occurrence reporting system, failing to take appropriate corrective or preventive measures based on this analysis, failing to implement these measures without undue delay, or failing to implement a procedure for monitoring the implementation and effectiveness of these measures,

- 9. penalizing its employee or another person who submitted an occurrence report or who is mentioned in this report,

- 10. failing to submit to the Institute within the specified period the preliminary results of the analysis of occurrence data collected from the mandatory or voluntary occurrence reporting system or the measures to be taken based on this analysis, or failing to notify the Institute within the specified period of the final results of the analysis performed, or

- 11. failing to adopt, after consultation with employees, internal regulations describing how to ensure and implement a just culture approach, or adopting such internal regulations that do not ensure the implementation of this approach,

- i) in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the field of aviation fuels^46), as an air carrier or operator of aviation activity other than commercial air transport,

- 1. failing to notify the Office or the Agency of the specified data in a timely manner,

- 2. failing to refuel the minimum annual quantity of aviation fuel at the specified airport,

- 3. failing to provide the necessary cooperation to the airport operator, or

- 4. using an environmental label that has not been assigned by the Agency, or not using this label in the specified manner, or

- j) in contravention of directly applicable European Union regulations governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the field of aviation fuels^46), as an airport operator,

- 1. failing to take necessary measures to address insufficient access for air carriers or operators of aviation activity other than commercial air transport to aviation fuels containing a minimum share of sustainable aviation fuels,

- 2. failing to provide the necessary cooperation to the Office, or

- 3. failing to submit to the Office or the Agency in a timely manner a progress report on facilitating access for air carriers or operators of aviation activity other than commercial air transport to hydrogen or electricity at the specified airport, containing the specified particulars.

(4) A legal entity or a natural person engaged in business further commits an administrative offense by:

- a) as an air carrier, failing to provide the specified data, in contravention of § 69 para. 1,

- b) as an air carrier, failing to provide the collected passenger name record data or failing to provide such data within the specified period or in the specified manner, in contravention of § 69a para. 1, 2 or 4,

- c) as a domestic air carrier, failing to develop a plan for assisting victims of an aviation accident and their relatives or failing to proceed in accordance therewith, in contravention of § 68 para. 2,

- d) as an air carrier of the European Union^20), operating commercial air transport without granted traffic rights,

- e) as an air carrier of the European Union^20) to whom traffic rights have been granted, failing to notify the Ministry of Transport of the facts specified in § 70c para. 6 or failing to fulfill the condition stipulated for the exercise of traffic rights in the decision pursuant to § 70c para. 4,

- f) as an air carrier of another Member State,

- 1. failing to notify the commencement of scheduled commercial air transport or failing to fulfill the conditions stipulated for such notification, in contravention of § 70f para. 1, or

- 2. failing to notify the commencement of non-scheduled commercial air transport or failing to fulfill the conditions stipulated for such notification, in contravention of § 70f para. 5, or

- g) as a third-country air carrier,

- 1. operating scheduled commercial air transport without a permit or failing to fulfill the obligation stipulated in § 71 para. 6,

- 2. failing to notify the commencement of direct or indirect sale of scheduled commercial air transport services or failing to fulfill the obligation stipulated in § 71b para. 3, or

- 3. operating non-scheduled commercial air transport without a permit or failing to fulfill the obligation stipulated in § 71c para. 7.

(5) A fine may be imposed for an administrative offense:

- a) up to CZK 100,000, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 1 letter a), paragraph 2 letter a) or g), or paragraph 4 letter f) or letter g) point 2,

- b) up to CZK 300,000, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 1 letter g), paragraph 2 letter e) or f), paragraph 3 letter f) point 2 or 3, letter g), letter i) point 1 or 4, or letter j) point 2 or 3, or paragraph 4 letter e),

- c) up to CZK 500,000, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 1 letter c) or d), paragraph 2 letter b) or letter d) point 5, paragraph 3 letter a), letter b) point 2, letter f) point 1, 5 or 6, letter h) points 1 to 7, or letter i) point 3, or paragraph 4 letter a), b), c) or d),

- d) from CZK 50,000 to CZK 1,000,000, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 1 letter b), e) or h), paragraph 2 letter c), letter d) point 1, 2 or 4, letter h) or j), paragraph 3 letter b) point 3, letter c), d), letter f) point 4, letter h) points 8 to 11, or letter j) point 1, or paragraph 4 letter g) point 1 or 3,

- e) from CZK 75,000 to CZK 3,000,000, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 2 letter k), or

- f) from CZK 100,000 to CZK 5,000,000, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 1 letter f), i), j) or k), paragraph 2 letter d) point 3 or letter i), or paragraph 3 letter b) point 1 or letter e).

(6) An air carrier or operator of aviation activity other than commercial air transport may be exempted from liability for an administrative offense according to paragraph 3 letter i) point 2 only for a reason stipulated by directly applicable European Union regulations governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the field of aviation fuels^46). For this administrative offense, a fine shall be imposed in the amount from the lower limit of the fine rate stipulated according to directly applicable European Union regulations governing conditions for sustainable aviation in the field of aviation fuels^46) up to double the thus stipulated lower limit of the fine rate. For this administrative offense, the administrative penalty of forfeiture of property or substitute value cannot be imposed, the imposition of an administrative penalty for this administrative offense cannot be waived or conditionally waived, and the lower limit of the fine rate for this administrative offense cannot be exceptionally reduced.

(7) A prohibition of activity may be imposed for an administrative offense:

- a) up to 6 months, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 2 letter i),

- b) up to 1 year, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 1 letter b), paragraph 2 letter h), or paragraph 3 letter d), or

- c) up to 2 years, if it is an administrative offense according to paragraph 1 letter f), j) or k), or paragraph 3 letter e).

p93a.heading: Administrative offenses of legal entities and natural persons engaged in business activities in the field of ensuring the protection of civil aviation against unlawful acts

p93b.heading: Offences committed by legal entities and natural persons engaged in business in the field of airspace use
p93b.text:
(1) A legal entity or a natural person engaged in business commits an offence by failing to ensure the update or cancellation of a flight plan in the event of its suspension, in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing air traffic flow management^29).

(2) A legal entity or a natural person engaged in business commits an offence by, in contravention of a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the licensing of air traffic controllers and the conditions for the exercise of their functions^28):

- a) as an air navigation service provider,

- 1. failing to ensure that air traffic control or student air traffic control activities are performed by a person holding a valid licence,

- 2. failing to establish local competence schemes or failing to submit such schemes or their amendments to the Office for approval,

- 3. failing to keep records of the hours during which each licence holder exercises the rights conferred by the licence, or failing to provide such data to the Office or the licence holder, or

- 4. failing to develop or implement objective, transparent, and non-discriminatory procedures for reporting medical incapacitation of an air traffic controller, student air traffic controller, instructor, or assessor that limits or precludes the performance of such activity, or

- b) as an organisation for the training of air traffic controllers, student air traffic controllers, or the training of instructors or assessors,

- 1. conducting training of air traffic controllers, student air traffic controllers, or training of instructors or assessors without a certificate or in contravention of the conditions stipulated therein,

- 2. entrusting the conduct of training of air traffic controllers, student air traffic controllers, instructors, or assessors to a natural person who does not meet the conditions stipulated for such activity,

- 3. failing to submit to the Office for approval amendments to the syllabi of training courses or to the plans for initial, unit, or ongoing training,

- 4. failing to notify the Office of the termination of its activity,

- 5. failing to return the certificate to the Office within the stipulated period after its revocation or after the termination of its activity,

- 6. failing to implement safety measures imposed by the Office,

- 7. failing to report an accident, serious incident, or event resulting from training activity within the stipulated period or failing to produce a subsequent report,

- 8. failing to establish, implement, or manage a management system or failing to do so in the stipulated manner,

- 9. failing to appoint an accountable manager or a person responsible for training,

- 10. failing to keep records of instructors or persons qualified to assess the competence of practical training instructors and assessors or failing to establish a procedure for maintaining the competence of theoretical instruction instructors,

- 11. failing to ensure that practical training instructors and assessors undergo recurrent training,

- 12. failing to keep records of persons undergoing or having undergone training,

- 13. failing to establish or manage a system for recording the qualifications of instructors and assessors and the assessment of their skills and the subjects they are authorised to teach, or failing to keep such records, or

- 14. failing to have liability insurance for damage caused in connection with the provision of training and paid insurance premiums.

(3) A legal entity or a natural person engaged in business commits an offence by:

- a) providing air services without authorisation or consent, in contravention of § 46 (1) or § 49 (1), or

- b) providing, in contravention of § 49a (1),

- 1. air navigation services without a certificate issued pursuant to a directly applicable European Union regulation governing the provision of air navigation services in the single European sky^18), or

- 2. air traffic services and meteorological services without designation by the Office or in contravention of § 49 (4).

(4) A fine may be imposed for an offence:

- a) up to CZK 100,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 1,

- b) up to CZK 300,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 2 (b) point 7,

- c) up to CZK 500,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 2 (a) point 4 or (b) point 4, 5 or 9,

- d) from CZK 50,000 to CZK 1,000,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 2 (a) point 1 or 3 or (b) point 6 or 10 to 14,

- e) from CZK 75,000 to CZK 3,000,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 2 (a) point 2 or (b) point 2, 3 or 8, or

- f) from CZK 100,000 to CZK 5,000,000, in the case of an offence under paragraph 2 (b) point 1 or paragraph 3.

(5) A prohibition of activity for up to 2 years may be imposed for an offence under paragraph 2 (b) point 1 or paragraph 3.

p93c.heading: Offences committed by legal entities and natural persons engaged in business in the field of passenger rights protection in commercial air transport operations

p93d.heading: Administrative offenses by legal entities and natural persons carrying out business activities in the field of operating unmanned systems

p94.text:
(1) Misdemeanours under this Act shall be heard by

- a) the Ministry of Transport or the Office, according to their competence specified in § 88 and 89, in the case of

- 1. misdemeanours committed by natural persons, with the exception of misdemeanours under § 92 (2) (c) or (d) or § 92 (3) (e) or (f), or

- 2. misdemeanours committed by legal entities and natural persons engaged in business, with the exception of misdemeanours under § 93 (2) (d) point 4 or (e), § 93 (3) (f) to (h), § 93 (4) (a) or (b), § 93c (1) (a) or § 93c (2), if the perpetrator of such misdemeanour is a tour operator or travel agency operator,

- b) the Police of the Czech Republic, in the case of misdemeanours committed by a legal entity or a natural person engaged in business under § 93 (4) (a) or (b),

- c) the municipal trade licensing office, in the case of misdemeanours committed by legal entities or natural persons engaged in business under § 93c (1) (a) or § 93c (2), if the perpetrator of such misdemeanour is a tour operator or travel agency operator,

- d) the Institute, in the case of misdemeanours committed by natural persons under § 92 (2) (c) or (d) or § 92 (3) (e) or (f), or misdemeanours committed by legal entities or natural persons engaged in business under § 93 (2) (e) or § 93 (3) (f) to (h), or

- e) the Office for the Protection of Competition, in the case of a misdemeanour committed by a legal entity or a natural person engaged in business under § 93 (2) (d) point 4.

(2) Misdemeanours under this Act may also be heard by the Police of the Czech Republic by on-the-spot order, in the case of misdemeanours under

- a) § 92c (1) (a) point 1 or 2 or § 92c (3) (b), or

- b) § 92c (1) (c) point 3 or 4 or § 93d (1) (b) point 3 or 4, if the unmanned system operator controls such system at the time of committing the misdemeanour.

(3) The Ministry of the Interior shall decide on appeals against decisions of the Police of the Czech Republic under paragraph 1 (b).

(4) The Office for the Protection of Competition shall collect the fine it has imposed.

p104.text:
Act No. 455/1991 Coll., on Trade Licensing (Trade Licensing Act), as amended by Act No. 231/1992 Coll., Act of the Czech National Council No. 591/1992 Coll., Act No. 600/1992 Coll., Act No. 273/1993 Coll., Act No. 303/1993 Coll., Act No. 38/1994 Coll., Act No. 42/1994 Coll., Act No. 136/1994 Coll., Act No. 200/1994 Coll., Act No. 237/1995 Coll., Act No. 286/1995 Coll., Act No. 94/1996 Coll., Act No. 95/1996 Coll., Act No. 147/1996 Coll. and Act No. 19/1997 Coll., is hereby amended and supplemented as follows:

- 1. In § 3 (3) (x), the full stop at the end of the sentence is replaced by a comma and letter y) is added, which, including footnote No. 23f), reads:

- „y) operation of airports, operation of commercial air transport and aerial work, and provision of air navigation services.^23f)

^23f) Act No. 49/1997 Coll., on Civil Aviation and on Amendment and Supplementation of Act No. 455/1991 Coll., on Trade Licensing (Trade Licensing Act), as amended.“.

- 2. In Annex No. 3 CONCESSIONED TRADES, in group 314: Other, the field „Air transport and aerial work“ is deleted.

p105.text: This Act shall enter into force on 1 April 1997.

p95.text: If the conditions stipulated by a directly applicable European Union regulation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^1c), under which an aircraft may be exempted from its scope, are met, this directly applicable regulation nor directly applicable European Union regulations issued thereunder shall not apply to the design, production, maintenance and operation of the aircraft; this shall not apply to the design, production, maintenance and operation of an aircraft for which a certificate has been issued or a declaration made under a directly applicable European Union regulation governing common rules in the field of civil aviation^45).

p96.text: The provisions of this Act shall apply only if an international treaty, which is part of the legal order, does not stipulate otherwise.

p97.text: For the definition of the status of the responsible representative, § 11 of the Trade Licensing Act shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p98.text:
(1) The price for the use of an airport and the provision of air navigation services shall be agreed upon by airport operators and air navigation service providers in accordance with price regulations.^9) When agreeing on the price, international treaties that are part of the legal order and that limit the amount of the price must be observed. The Ministry of Transport shall supervise whether airport operators and air navigation service providers observe these international treaties when agreeing on prices. This provision does not affect Title VI of Part Four.

(2) The scope and conditions of liability insurance for damage caused by aircraft operation shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation, unless a directly applicable European Union regulation^10a) stipulates otherwise.

p98a.text:
(1) For the purpose of statistical surveys, the Ministry of Transport shall be provided with:

- a) by the aerodrome operator

- 1. data on the number of aircraft movements, the number of passengers handled, the volume of cargo and mail handled at the aerodrome for the calendar year, no later than the end of April of the following calendar year,

- 2. data on the number of aircraft movements, the number of passengers handled, the volume of cargo and mail handled at the aerodrome and on the origin and destination of commercial flights carried out in the past quarter, if it is an international aerodrome handling at least 90 % of the total passengers or cargo transported in the Czech Republic, or an aerodrome handling at least 1 million passengers or 100 thousand tonnes of cargo annually, always no later than the end of February, May, August, and November of the respective calendar year,

- 3. data on the revenues, costs, and investments of the aerodrome operator and on the number and structure of its employees, if it is an international aerodrome handling at least 90 % of the total passengers or cargo transported in the Czech Republic, or an aerodrome handling at least 1 million passengers or 100 thousand tonnes of cargo annually, no later than the end of April of the following calendar year,

- b) by a domestic air carrier

- 1. data on the number of passengers, cargo, and mail transported by it and on the utilized capacity of aircraft operated by it for the calendar year, always no later than the end of April of the following calendar year,

- 2. data on the number and type of aircraft operated by it, their offered and utilized capacity, on the number and structure of aviation personnel and on related wage costs, for the calendar year, always no later than the end of March of the following calendar year,

- 3. data on fuel consumption for flights carried out by aircraft type for the calendar year, always no later than the end of February of the following calendar year,

- c) by a domestic air carrier operating at least one aircraft with a total number of passenger seats exceeding 30 or an aircraft exclusively for the transport of cargo and mail with a maximum take-off weight of more than 10 tonnes

- 1. data on the number of passengers, cargo, and mail transported by it and on the utilized capacity of aircraft operated by it for the calendar month, always no later than the end of the second calendar month following the month decisive for the submission of such data,

- 2. data on the origin and destination of commercial flights carried out in the past quarter, always no later than the end of February, May, August, and November of the respective calendar year,

- 3. a balance sheet, profit and loss statement, statement of retained earnings, and categorization of revenues by transported persons, cargo, and mail and by whether it concerns the operation of scheduled or non-scheduled commercial air transport, for the calendar year, always no later than the end of April of the following calendar year,

- d) by a domestic air carrier operating international scheduled commercial air transport, data on the number of flights carried out, the type of aircraft operated by it, the organizational and geographical scope of aircraft operations, the offered capacity of aircraft operated by it, and the number of passengers, cargo, and mail transported for the calendar year, no later than the end of February of the following calendar year,

- e) by the air navigation service provider

- 1. data on revenues, costs, and investments and data on the number and structure of its employees for the calendar year, always no later than the end of May of the following calendar year,

- 2. data on air navigation services provided for the calendar year, always no later than the end of March of the following calendar year.

(2) The handling service provider shall continuously submit data on the number of passengers handled by it to the aerodrome operator.

(3) The individual data submitted for the purpose of statistical surveys to the Ministry of Transport, the data on the number of passengers handled submitted by the handling service provider to the aerodrome operator, and their structure shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p99.text: The Ministry of Transport shall ensure the issuance of the Aeronautical Information Publication, which publishes decisive facts affecting the safety of air traffic.

p99a.text:
(1) Where this Act refers to a natural person with permanent residence in the Czech Republic, a citizen of the Czech Republic, or a legal entity with its registered office in the Czech Republic, this shall also mean a national of another Member State of the European Union or a legal entity with its registered office, central administration, or principal place of business in another Member State of the European Union.

(2) The provision of paragraph 1 shall also apply to a national of another state or a legal entity with its registered office, central administration, or principal place of business in another state, if this results from an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound^11a).

(3) The provision of paragraph 1 shall not apply to § 54n (2), § 54o (1) (a), and § 82.

p100.heading: Transitional provisions
p100.text:
(1) Unless otherwise provided, this Act shall also govern legal relations established under legal regulations valid until the effective date of this Act.

(2) Penalties under this Act may only be imposed for unlawful acts committed after the effective date of this Act.

(3) The aerodrome operator shall be obliged to apply to the Office for the issuance of an aerodrome operating permit within one year of the effective date of this Act. Until the Office's decision, the aerodrome operator shall be deemed to be an aerodrome operator to whom a permit has been issued under this Act. If the aerodrome operator fails to apply for a permit within the stipulated period, the existing aerodrome operating permit shall expire.

(4) A legal or natural person providing air traffic services shall be obliged to apply to the Ministry of Transport for the issuance of an authorisation within one year of the effective date of this Act. Until the Ministry of Transport's decision on the issuance of the authorisation, the legal or natural person shall be deemed authorised to provide these services. If the legal or natural person providing air traffic services fails to apply for an authorisation within the stipulated period, the existing authorisation to provide air traffic services shall expire.

(5) A legal or natural person providing aeronautical telecommunication, aeronautical meteorological, aeronautical information services and services during the handling process at an aerodrome shall be obliged to apply to the Ministry of Transport for consent within one year of the effective date of this Act. Until consent is granted, the legal or natural person shall be deemed authorised to provide these services. If the natural or legal person fails to apply for consent within the stipulated period, the existing authorisation to provide services shall expire.

(6) Carriers operating commercial air transport on the basis of a granted concession and wishing to continue operating commercial air transport on the basis of a licence shall be obliged to apply to the Ministry of Transport for the grant of a licence within one year of the effective date of this Act. Until the Ministry of Transport's decision, these carriers shall be deemed to be carriers to whom a licence has been granted under this Act. If the carrier fails to apply for the grant of a licence within the stipulated period, the existing authorisation to operate commercial air transport shall expire. Carriers operating commercial air transport on the basis of a granted concession and wishing to continue operating air taxi transport shall be obliged to apply to the Office for the issuance of a permit within one year of the effective date of this Act. If the carrier fails to apply for the issuance of a permit within the stipulated period, the existing authorisation to operate commercial air transport shall expire.

(7) A legal or natural person operating aerial work on the basis of a concession shall be obliged to apply to the Office for the issuance of a permit within one year of the effective date of this Act. Until the Office's decision, this person shall be deemed authorised to operate aerial work under this Act. If the legal or natural person fails to apply for the issuance of a permit within the stipulated period, the existing authorisation to operate aerial work shall expire.

(8) Administrative proceedings initiated before the effective date of this Act, which do not need to be completed under this Act, shall be terminated on the effective date of this Act.

Final Provisions

p101.text: The administrative authority, the State Aviation Inspection, established by Act No. 305/1993 Sb., is hereby abolished. The rights and obligations arising from labour-law and other relations shall be transferred from the administrative authority, the State Aviation Inspection, to the Office established by this Act.

p102.text:
(1) The Ministry of Transport shall issue a legal regulation for the implementation of § 4 (2) (d), § 4 (4), § 5 (2), § 5a (2), § 7 (8), § 12 (2) (b), § 12a (7), § 16 (1) and (2), § 17 (1) and (3), § 17a (1), § 17b (6), § 17d (4), § 22a (1), § 25 (3), § 32 (1) and (3), § 34a (4), § 35 (1) and (3), § 42a (3), § 44 (7), § 44j (6), § 45 (4), § 49c (2), § 49f (2), § 49g (2), § 51a (11), § 54d (3), § 54e (9), § 54i (9), § 54k (10), § 54l (4), § 55a (6), § 55c (5), § 58 (2), § 67 (4), § 74 (3), § 76 (3), § 81 (8), § 82 (6), § 84b (3), § 84c (8), § 85j (6), § 85m (4), § 85r (3), § 85x (7), § 85y (3), § 85z (6), § 90 (4), § 91a (11), § 98 (2) and § 98a (3) of this Act.

(2) Operators of aerodromes and aeronautical structures, persons authorised to operate air services, operators of aeronautical activities and other persons involved in civil aviation shall be obliged to comply with aviation regulations which are in accordance with international treaties forming part of the legal order, issued by

- a) the International Civil Aviation Organization,^1k)

- b) the Joint Aviation Authorities in accordance with European Union regulations,^13) and

- c) the European Organisation for the Safety of Air Navigation (EUROCONTROL),^14)

in the wording adopted by the Czech Republic represented by the Ministry of Transport. These regulations shall be published in the Aeronautical Information Publication and shall be available at the Ministry of Transport and the Office.
